      ```lean
            BHW.matrixBlockColumns r (d + 1 - r)
              (fun k a =>
                BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameHeadBlock
                  d n r hrn p.head p.mixed ι
                    (Fin.cast
                      (Nat.add_sub_of_le (Nat.le_of_lt hrD)) k) a)
              (fun k μ =>
                BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameTailBlock
                  d n r hrD hrn p.tail ι
                    (Fin.cast
                      (Nat.add_sub_of_le (Nat.le_of_lt hrD)) k) μ)

      def BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (H : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) ℂ)
          (L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) ℂ)
          (T : BHW.SourceShiftedTailOrientedData d r hrD (n - r))
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) : ℂ :=
        ∑ S : Set.powersetCard (Fin (r + (d + 1 - r))) r,
          BHW.matrixRowSubsetLaplaceSign r (d + 1 - r) S.val S.prop *
            (Matrix.det
              (fun a b =>
                BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameHeadBlock
                  d n r hrn H L ι
                  (Fin.cast (Nat.add_sub_of_le (Nat.le_of_lt hrD))
                    (BHW.matrixRowSubsetHeadRows
                      r (d + 1 - r) S.val S.prop a)) b)) *
            BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameTailRowsDet
              d n r hrD hrn T ι
              { toFun := fun μ =>
                  Fin.cast (Nat.add_sub_of_le (Nat.le_of_lt hrD))
                    (BHW.matrixRowSubsetTailRows
                      r (d + 1 - r) S.val S.prop μ)
                inj' := by
                  intro μ ν hμν
                  exact (BHW.matrixRowSubsetTailRows
                    r (d + 1 - r) S.val S.prop).injective
                    (Fin.cast_injective
                      (Nat.add_sub_of_le (Nat.le_of_lt hrD)) hμν) }

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p) =
            BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              p.head p.mixed
              (BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant
                d r hrD (n - r) p.tail)
              ι

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula_of_laplace
          (hlaplace :
            ∀ (r D : Nat)
              (M : Matrix (Fin (r + D)) (Fin r) ℂ)
              (Q : Matrix (Fin (r + D)) (Fin D) ℂ),
              (BHW.matrixBlockColumns r D M Q).det =
                BHW.matrixBlockColumnLaplaceSum r D M Q)
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι
              (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector d n r hrD hrn p) =
            BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              p.head p.mixed
              (BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant
                d r hrD (n - r) p.tail)
              ι

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              R.headFactor R.L R.tail ι =
            G.det ι

      theorem BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur_headTail
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          (lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r)) :
          BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              R.headFactor R.L R.tail
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                d n r hrD hrn lam) =
            R.headFactor.det * R.tail.det lam

      theorem BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur_headTail_eq_source_det
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          (lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r)) :
          BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              R.headFactor R.L R.tail
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                d n r hrD hrn lam) =
            G.det
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                d n r hrD hrn lam)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_det_of_fullFrameReconstruct
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          {p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn}
          (hhead : p.head = R.headFactor)
          (hmixed : p.mixed = R.L)
          (htail :
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD (n - r) p.tail =
              R.tail)
          (hdet :
            ∀ ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n,
              BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
                R.headFactor R.L R.tail ι = G.det ι) :
          (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
              d n r hrD hrn p)).det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_det
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          {p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn}
          (hhead :
            p.head = R.headFactor)
          (hmixed : p.mixed = R.L)
          (htail :
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD (n - r) p.tail =
              R.tail) :
          (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
              d n r hrD hrn p)).det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_of_fullFrameReconstruct
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          {p :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalParameter d n r hrD hrn}
          (hhead : p.head = R.headFactor)
          (hmixed : p.mixed = R.L)
          (htail :
            BHW.sourceShiftedTailOrientedInvariant d r hrD (n - r) p.tail =
              R.tail)
          (hdet :
            ∀ ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n,
              BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
                R.headFactor R.L R.tail ι = G.det ι) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            (BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
              d n r hrD hrn p) = G
      ```

      `sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur` is now a concrete Laplace expansion.
      It first forms the `(d+1) × r` head-column block: selected head labels
      contribute standard coefficient rows, while selected tail labels
      contribute their Schur mixed row `L u`; multiplying by `H` gives the
      actual head-coordinate columns.  The residual-tail contribution for an
      ordered row subset is zero unless every chosen row is a tail source
      label; in the nonzero case it is exactly `T.det` of the induced ordered
      embedding into `Fin (n-r)`.  The checked theorem
      `sourceNormalFullFrameTailRowsDet_eq_det_tailBlock` identifies these
      stored coordinates with the ordinary minors of the explicit residual
      tail-coordinate block, including the zero-row case when a chosen residual
      row is actually a head source label.  The coefficient/head-block
      evaluation lemmas, residual-tail block evaluation lemmas, and the
      two coordinate projection theorems
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_headCoord_eq_headBlock` and
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_tailCoord_eq_tailBlock` are checked.
      Consequently the arbitrary-frame matrix identity
      `sourceFullFrameMatrix_normalParameter_eq_blockColumns` is also checked:
      after reindexing `Fin (d+1)` as `Fin (r + (d+1-r))`, the selected full
      frame matrix of the normal parameter vector is exactly
      `matrixBlockColumns` of the explicit head and residual-tail blocks.
      These five definition layers (`sourceNormalFullFrameCoeff`,
      `sourceNormalFullFrameHeadBlock`, `sourceNormalFullFrameTailBlock`,
      `sourceNormalFullFrameTailRowsDet`, and
      `sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur`) are now checked in Lean.  The row
      subset bookkeeping definitions `matrixBlockColumns`,
      `matrixBlockColumns_inl`, `matrixBlockColumns_inr`,
      `matrixBlockColumns_reindex_finSum`,
      `matrixRowSubset_compl_card`, `matrixRowSubsetHeadRows`,
      `matrixRowSubsetTailRows`, `matrixRowSubsetSumEquiv`,
      `matrixRowSubsetLaplaceSign`, `matrixBlockColumnLaplaceTerm`, and
      `matrixBlockColumnLaplaceSum` are also checked in Lean; they use
      mathlib's `Finset.orderEmbOfFin` and `finSumEquivOfFinset` rather than a
      custom split structure.  The finite theorem
      `matrix_det_blockColumn_laplace` is the ordinary determinant Laplace
      expansion along the first `r` columns and the last `d+1-r` columns, with
      `matrixRowSubsetLaplaceSign` carrying the row-shuffle sign.  Thus
      `sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula` is proved by
      rewriting the selected full-frame matrix as `[headBlock | residualTail]`
      and applying this Laplace theorem.  No determinant relation from the
      variety is used in this theorem; it is pure finite row algebra for the
      explicit normal parameter vector.
      The conditional theorem
      `sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula_of_laplace` is still
      checked as a reusable bridge from a supplied finite Laplace theorem, but
      the unconditional theorem
      `sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula` now supplies that
      bridge with the checked theorem `matrix_det_blockColumn_laplace`.
      The determinant-field consumer
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_det_of_fullFrameReconstruct`
      is checked in `SourceOrientedSchurResidual.lean`: after substituting
      `p.head = R.headFactor`, `p.mixed = R.L`, and the shifted-tail invariant
      equality, it calls
      `sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula` and the supplied
      full-frame reconstruction hypothesis.  The no-hypothesis wrapper
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_det` is now checked
      in the hard range by applying
      `sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct`.
      The full oriented-data consumer
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_of_fullFrameReconstruct`
      is checked as the product-coordinate extensionality assembly of the
      checked Gram theorem and this determinant consumer.
      The selected head-tail specialization of that reconstruction is now
      checked separately as
      `sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur_headTail` and
      `sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur_headTail_eq_source_det`: choose an
      actual shifted-tail realization from `R.tail_mem`, compare the Schur
      formula with `sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_headTail`, then rewrite
      `R.tail_det_eq` and cancel `R.headFactor.det` using
      `R.headFactor_det_unit.ne_zero`.

      Checked implementation transcript for `matrix_det_blockColumn_laplace`:
      first rewrite the determinant as the top full-exterior-algebra coordinate
      using `exteriorAlgebra_top_repr_iMulti_matrixColumns`.  Rewrite the wedge
      of the concatenated block columns with
      `matrixBlockColumns_iMulti_eq_mul`.  Expand the head wedge with
      `exteriorAlgebra_iMulti_matrixColumns_eq_sum_minors`, distribute
      multiplication and the basis-coordinate map over the finite sum, and
      evaluate each summand by
      `exteriorAlgebra_basis_mul_iMulti_compl_repr`.  That coefficient lemma
      expands the tail wedge, kills non-complementary row subsets with
      `ExteriorAlgebra.basis_mul_of_not_disjoint`, uses
      `ExteriorAlgebra.basis_mul_of_disjoint` on the complementary subset
      `Set.powersetCard.compl`, identifies the disjoint-union top index with
      `Finset.univ`, and matches the surviving `permOfDisjoint` sign with
      `matrixRowSubsetLaplaceSign`.  The head and tail minor row enumerations
      are the `Set.powersetCard.ofFinEmbEquiv.symm` order embeddings, which are
      definitionally the `matrixRowSubsetHeadRows` and
      `matrixRowSubsetTailRows` order embeddings after unfolding.  This is
      finite Cauchy-Binet/Laplace only; it does not use source geometry.

      The theorem `sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct` is the only
      place where non-selected full-frame determinants are recovered from the
      original oriented datum `G`, and it must assume
      `hGvar : G ∈ sourceOrientedGramVariety d n`.  Without that range
      hypothesis, `G` is just a product-coordinate object and its unselected
      determinant coordinates are arbitrary, so the theorem would be false.
      Its proof uses the oriented algebraic relations supplied by `hGvar` in
      two steps.  First, `R.tail_det_eq` defines the selected head-tail
      residual determinant coordinates by
      `G.det(head ∪ lam) / R.headFactor.det`.  Second, the actual source tuple
      obtained from `hGvar` satisfies the Plucker/Cauchy-Binet relations, so
      every other ordered full-frame determinant rewrites as the same Laplace
      sum with head coefficient rows, Schur mixed coefficients `R.L`, and those
      selected residual determinants.  The selected head-tail specialization
      of the formula reduces to `R.headFactor.det * R.tail.det lam`, so the
      quotient definition is cancellable using `R.headFactor_det_unit`; this
      selected-frame specialization is already checked as
      `sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur_headTail_eq_source_det`.  Downstream
      code must call this theorem rather than redoing multilinearity or
      dividing by a determinant inferred only from the Gram block.

      The actual residual-vector setup for the non-selected exterior proof is
      now checked.  Define
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector d n r hrn z R.L u` as
      `z_tail u - ∑ a, R.L u a • z_head a`.  The checked decomposition theorem
      rewrites each actual tail vector as its head projection plus this
      residual, and the checked orthogonality theorems
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_inner_head` and
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_head_inner` prove that the residual
      lies in the orthogonal complement of the selected head span.  The checked
      theorem `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_inner_residual` then identifies
      the Gram matrix of the actual residual vectors with the stored
      Schur-complement coordinate `R.tail.gram`.  Its Lean proof avoids a
      separate symmetric-matrix quadratic-form lemma: decompose only the left
      tail vector to replace the first residual by the actual tail vector, then
      expand the right residual and use `R.L * R.A = mixed` to identify the
      single mixed projection term with `(R.L * R.A * R.Lᵀ) u v`.

      The remaining non-selected determinant proof is now exactly the oriented
      complement-volume propagation theorem.  Its Lean-ready decomposition is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) ℂ)
          (lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r)) :
          Matrix.det
              (fun k μ =>
                if h : k.val < r then
                  z (BHW.finSourceHead hrn ⟨k.val, h⟩) μ
                else
                  BHW.sourceActualSchurResidualVector d n r hrn z L
                    (lam ⟨k.val - r, by omega⟩) μ) =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail d n r hrD hrn lam) z

      theorem BHW.sourceActualSchurResidual_fullFrameExpansion
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (L : Matrix (Fin (n - r)) (Fin r) ℂ)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z =
            -- the Cauchy-Binet/Laplace expansion obtained by replacing each
            -- selected tail vector by `headProjection + actualResidual`.
            -- Terms are indexed by the rows whose contribution is taken from
            -- the selected head span; repeated head rows vanish by
            -- alternation, and the surviving terms pair a head coefficient
            -- minor with an actual residual complement-volume minor.
            BHW.sourceActualSchurResidualFullFrameExpansion
              d n r hrD hrn z L ι

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedSchur_complementVolumePropagation
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z))
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              R.headFactor R.L R.tail ι =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n r : Nat}
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hA :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hheadTail :
            ∀ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r),
              H.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam)) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_exists_nonzero
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hheadTail :
            ∀ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r),
              H.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam))
          (hNZ :
            ∃ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r),
              G.det
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                  d n r hrD hrn lam) ≠ 0) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_zero_of_not_maxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hlow : ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G) :
          G.det = 0

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_of_not_maxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hlowG : ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_allZero_notMaxRank
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (hallZero_notMax :
            ∀ {K : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n},
              K ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
              IsUnit
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn K).det ->
              (∀ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r),
                K.det
                    (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                      d n r hrD hrn lam) = 0) ->
              ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n K)
          {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hH : H ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hA :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det)
          (hgram : H.gram = G.gram)
          (hheadTail :
            ∀ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r),
              H.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam)) :
          H.det = G.det

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety_notMaxRank_of_headTailDet_eq_zero
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hn : d + 1 <= n)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (hA :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det)
          (hzero :
            ∀ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r),
              G.det
                  (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                    d n r hrD hrn lam) = 0) :
          ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct_of_headTailPropagation
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n)
          (hprop :
            ∀ {G H : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n},
              G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
              H ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ->
              IsUnit
                (BHW.sourceOrientedSchurHeadBlock n r hrn G).det ->
              H.gram = G.gram ->
              (∀ lam : Fin (d + 1 - r) ↪ Fin (n - r),
                H.det
                    (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                      d n r hrD hrn lam) =
                  G.det
                    (BHW.sourceFullFrameEmbeddingOfHeadTail
                      d n r hrD hrn lam)) ->
              H.det = G.det)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hGvar : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n)
          (R : BHW.SourceOrientedSchurResidualData d n r hrD hrn G)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceNormalFullFrameDetFromSchur d n r hrD hrn
              R.headFactor R.L R.tail ι =
            G.det ι
      ```

      The first theorem is now checked in Lean as
      `sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet`, with the explicit support
      definitions `sourceActualSchurSelectedOriginalMatrix`,
      `sourceActualSchurSelectedResidualMatrix`, and
      `sourceSchurHeadTailRowOperation`: subtracting the `L`-linear combination
      of the selected head rows from the selected tail rows is multiplication
      by a block lower-triangular row-operation matrix whose determinant is
      `1`.  The checked theorem
      `sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet_eq_headFactor_mul_tail_det`
      combines that row operation with `R.tail_det_eq`, proving that each
      selected actual residual head-tail determinant is exactly
      `R.headFactor.det * R.tail.det lam`.  This theorem is only the selected
      head-tail calibration case; it must not be generalized by row operations
      to an arbitrary selected frame, because an arbitrary frame need not
      contain the head rows being subtracted.  The second theorem is the finite
      multilinear expansion for arbitrary frames: substitute
      `tail = headProjection + actualResidual`, expand by determinant
      multilinearity, and kill repeated-head terms by alternation.  The third
      theorem is the only geometric comparison: the actual residual tuple and
      the stored shifted-tail tuple have the same residual Gram by
      `sourceActualSchurResidualVector_inner_residual`; their selected
      complement-volume coordinates are calibrated by
      `sourceActualSchurResidual_selectedFrameDet_eq_headFactor_mul_tail_det`;
      the shifted-tail oriented variety equations then propagate those
      complement-volume coordinates to every residual minor appearing in the
      finite expansion.  The same hard content is now isolated as
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq`: two
      oriented source-variety points with the same ordinary Gram coordinates,
      an invertible selected head Gram block, and matching all selected
      head-tail full-frame determinants have identical determinant-coordinate
      functions.  The checked theorem
      `sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct_of_headTailPropagation`
      proves that this single propagation theorem mechanically implies the
      Schur reconstruction equality for every ordered full frame: choose a
      shifted-tail realization from `R.tail_mem`, build the normal parameter,
      use the checked Gram realization theorem and the checked selected
      head-tail determinant theorem, then apply the propagation theorem and
      rewrite by `sourceFullFrameDet_normalParameter_eq_schurFormula`.  A Gemini
      theorem-shape check on 2026-05-05 agreed that this is the missing
      oriented-volume uniqueness/propagation input and explicitly warned that
      both Gram-only and naive arbitrary-row operation arguments are false.
      The determinant-nonzero branch of this propagation theorem is now
      checked as
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_exists_nonzero`
      in `SourceOrientedSchurPropagation.lean`: if some selected `head ∪ lam`
      determinant is nonzero, use that full frame as the selected chart,
      recover equality of selected full-frame coordinates from `hgram` and
      `hheadTail`, recover equality of mixed rows from `hgram`, and call the
      checked full-frame identity theorem
      `sourceOrientedGramData_eq_of_selectedCoord_eq_mixedRows_eq`.  The
      non-max-rank bridge is also checked in the same file:
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_zero_of_not_maxRank` proves that
      outside `SourceOrientedMaxRankAt` all full-frame determinant coordinates
      vanish, and
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq_of_allZero_notMaxRank`
      assembles the full propagation theorem from the all-zero branch.  The
      all-zero branch itself is now checked as
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_notMaxRank_of_headTailDet_eq_zero` in the
      hard range `d + 1 <= n`: its contrapositive first proves
      `sourceHeadRows_linearIndependent_of_schurHeadBlock_isUnit`, then uses
      `exists_headTail_fullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_headRows_linearIndependent_span_top`
      to extend the nondegenerate head rows to a full source frame by tail rows
      after quotienting by the head span.  Therefore the hard-range theorem
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety_det_eq_of_gram_eq_headTailDet_eq` is checked.
      The Schur reconstruction wrappers
      `sourceOrientedSchur_fullFrameDet_reconstruct`,
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur_det`, and
      `sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_realizes_schur` are also checked.
      This is a finite basis-extension theorem; it is not a full-frame chart
      argument and not a Gram-only comparison.

      From this chart the local analytic estimates become genuine theorem
      surfaces:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz0 : z0 ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz0 : z0 ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          ∃ Ω : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            IsOpen Ω ∧
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 ∈ Ω ∧
            ∃ C : ℝ, 0 <= C ∧
              ∀ G,
                G ∈ Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ->
                ‖BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F G‖ <= C

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz0 : z0 ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          ContinuousWithinAt
            (BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F)
            (BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
            (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)
      ```

      The boundedness proof is compactness: `extendF F ∘ toVec` is continuous
      on compact `K`, hence bounded; `image_surj` and `hBranch` rewrite every
      quotient value in the nearby oriented domain to a value on that compact
      family.  The continuity proof is the same first-countable compact
      cluster argument made explicit below: for a sequence `G_j -> G0` in the
      within-domain filter, choose parameters `c_j ∈ K` with invariant `G_j`;
      compactness gives convergent subsequences in `K`, continuity of the
      invariant gives invariant `G0`, and `hBranch` identifies all possible
      cluster values with `extendF F z0`.  The dense max-rank parameter field
      is then used to identify the normal-space extension obtained from the
      regular stratum with this branch-defined value at the singular point.

      Historical extended-tube residual-chart sketch.  This block is not the
      active no-tube connected-basis producer, and its ambient
      `SourceOrientedInvariantTransportEquiv` may only be used for genuine
      full-coordinate homeomorphisms.  Source-matrix normal-form changes must
      use the variety-relative packet
      `SourceOrientedRankDeficientAlgebraicNormalFormData` together with
      `N.originalNormalVarietyPoint`, as recorded in the active ledger below.
      The following order remains useful only for the later compact
      extended-tube residual chart where tube-stability fields are present.

      ```lean
      structure BHW.SourceOrientedInvariantTransportEquiv
          (d n : Nat) where
        toHomeomorph :
          BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n ≃ₜ
            BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n
        mem_variety_iff :
          ∀ G,
            toHomeomorph G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ↔
              G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        maxRank_iff :
          ∀ G,
            BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n (toHomeomorph G) ↔
              BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G
      -- Checked derived API:
      -- `toFun`, `invFun`, `left_inv`, `right_inv`, `continuous_toFun`,
      -- `continuous_invFun`, `isOpen_invFun_image`,
      -- `invFun_mem_variety_iff`, `invFun_maxRank_iff`, and `refl`.

      structure BHW.HWLemma3AdaptedNormalForm
          [NeZero d]
          (d n r : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) where
        rank_eq :
          r =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        span_finrank_eq :
          Module.finrank ℂ
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)) = r

      structure BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          (z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) where
        r : Nat
        hrD : r < d + 1
        hrn : r <= n
        adaptedBase : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        adaptedBase_mem : adaptedBase ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        adaptedBase_same_oriented :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n adaptedBase =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0
        adapted :
          BHW.HWLemma3AdaptedNormalForm d n r adaptedBase
        normalBase : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        normalBase_eq :
          normalBase = BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r
        head_unit :
          IsUnit
            (Matrix.det
              (fun a b =>
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n normalBase
                  (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                  (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b)))
        tail_zero :
          BHW.sourceTailResidualCoordinates d n r hrD hrn normalBase = 0
        normalTransport :
          BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r adaptedBase
        varietyTransport :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n
        toOriginal :
          (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ->
            Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        toOriginal_continuous : Continuous toOriginal
        toOriginal_normalBase_eq_adaptedBase :
          toOriginal normalBase = adaptedBase
        toOriginal_normalBase_invariant :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (toOriginal normalBase) =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0
        toOriginal_oriented :
          ∀ z,
            BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (toOriginal z) =
            varietyTransport.invFun
              (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z)

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_notMaxRank_sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_fullFrame
          (n : Nat)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hlow :
            ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z)) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1 := by
        -- Checked in `SourceOrientedRankBridge.lean`.  First prove strict
        -- inequality below `min (d+1) n`, using the rank upper bound for
        -- actual source configurations and failure of the equality defining
        -- `SourceOrientedMaxRankAt`; then compose with
        -- `min (d+1) n <= d+1`.
        exact
          BHW.sourceOriented_notMaxRank_sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_fullFrame
            d n z hlow

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_eq_zero_of_sourceRank_lt
          (d n : Nat)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z = 0 := by
        by_contra hdet
        let M := BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ι z
        have hGramDet :
            (Matrix.of (BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d M)).det ≠ 0 := by
          rw [BHW.sourceFullFrameGram_det_eq]
          exact mul_ne_zero (BHW.minkowskiMetricDet_ne_zero d)
            (pow_ne_zero 2 hdet)
        have hminor :
            Matrix.det
                (fun a b : Fin (d + 1) =>
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z (ι a) (ι b)) ≠ 0 := by
          simpa [M, BHW.sourceFullFrameGram_sourceFullFrameMatrix]
            using hGramDet
        have hrank_ge :
            d + 1 ≤
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) := by
          have h :=
            BHW.matrix_rank_ge_of_nondegenerate_square_submatrix
              (A := Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n =>
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z i j)
              (I := ι) (J := ι) hminor
          simpa [BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank] using h
        exact (not_le_of_gt hrank) hrank_ge

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_eq_of_sameGram_rank_lt
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w := by
        apply BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.ext
        · simpa [BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant,
            BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.gram] using hgram
        · funext ι
          have hzdet :
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z = 0 :=
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_eq_zero_of_sourceRank_lt
              d n hrank ι
          have hrank_w :
              BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) < d + 1 := by
            simpa [hgram] using hrank
          have hwdet :
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι w = 0 :=
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_eq_zero_of_sourceRank_lt
              d n hrank_w ι
          simp [BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant,
            BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.det, hzdet, hwdet]

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_head_unit
          (d n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n) :
          IsUnit
            (Matrix.det
              (fun a b =>
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r)
                  (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                  (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b))) := by
        -- The canonical head Gram block is the signature diagonal
        -- `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD`; every diagonal entry is ±1, hence the
        -- determinant is a unit.
        exact
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_head_unit
            d n r hrD hrn

      theorem BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_tail_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n r : Nat)
          (hrD : r < d + 1)
          (hrn : r <= n) :
          BHW.sourceTailResidualCoordinates d n r hrD hrn
              (BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r) = 0 := by
        -- The canonical source has zero vectors on the tail labels.
        ext u μ
        simp [BHW.sourceTailResidualCoordinates,
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource]

      noncomputable def BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n :=
        ⟨BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z, ⟨z, rfl⟩⟩

      theorem BHW.exists_sourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv_of_normalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base) :
          ∃ Q : BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n,
            (∀ z,
              BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec z) =
                Q.toFun (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z)) ∧
            (∀ z,
              BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec.symm z) =
                Q.invFun (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z)) := by
        exact
          ⟨T.varietyTransport, T.variety_commute,
            T.variety_symm_commute⟩

      noncomputable def
        BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.ofHWNormalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv d n :=
        Classical.choose
          (BHW.exists_sourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv_of_normalFormTransport
            (d := d) (n := n) r T)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyPoint_of_normalFormTransport_symm
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec.symm z) =
            (BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.ofHWNormalFormTransport
              (d := d) (n := n) r T).invFun
              (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z) := by
        exact
          (Classical.choose_spec
            (BHW.exists_sourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv_of_normalFormTransport
              (d := d) (n := n) r T)).2 z

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyPoint_toOriginal_of_normalFormTransport
          [NeZero d]
          (r : Nat)
          {base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (T : BHW.HWLemma3NormalFormTransport d n r base)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n (T.toNormalVec.symm z) =
            (BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.ofHWNormalFormTransport
              (d := d) (n := n) r T).invFun
              (BHW.sourceTupleOrientedVarietyPoint d n z) := by
        -- This is the source-variety transport statement, not an ambient
        -- coordinate-homeomorphism statement on arbitrary determinant data.
        exact
          BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyPoint_of_normalFormTransport_symm
            (d := d) (n := n) r T z

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_normalFormData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz0 : z0 ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0 := by
        classical
        let G0 := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0
        have hrank_lt :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n G0 < d + 1 :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_notMaxRank_sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_fullFrame
            d n z0 hlow
        rcases
          BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative
            (d := d) hd n hz0 with
          ⟨base, hbase_ET, hbase_gram, hbase_span⟩
        have hbase_rank_lt :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n base) < d + 1 := by
          simpa [hbase_gram] using hrank_lt
        have hbase_oriented :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n base =
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_eq_of_sameGram_rank_lt
            (d := d) n hbase_gram hbase_rank_lt
        let r :=
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n base)
        have hrD : r < d + 1 := by
          simpa [r, hbase_gram.symm, G0] using hrank_lt
        have hrn : r <= n :=
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_le_arity
            (n := n) (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n base)
        have hspan :
            Module.finrank ℂ
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n base)) = r := by
          simpa [r, hbase_gram] using hbase_span
        let adapted :
            BHW.HWLemma3AdaptedNormalForm d n r base :=
          { rank_eq := rfl
            span_finrank_eq := hspan }
        rcases
          BHW.hwLemma3_normalFormTransportData
            (d := d) hd n (z0 := base) r rfl hspan with
          ⟨Tnormal, _⟩
        let normalBase : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r
        have hhead :
            IsUnit
              (Matrix.det
                (fun a b =>
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n normalBase
                    (BHW.finSourceHead hrn a)
                    (BHW.finSourceHead hrn b))) :=
          by
            simpa [normalBase] using
              BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_head_unit
                (d := d) hd n r hrD hrn
        have htail :
            BHW.sourceTailResidualCoordinates d n r hrD hrn normalBase = 0 :=
          BHW.hwLemma3CanonicalSource_tail_zero
            (d := d) hd n r hrD hrn
        have htoOrig_base :
            Tnormal.toNormalVec.symm normalBase = base := by
          rw [normalBase, ← Tnormal.z0_to_canonical]
          simp
        let varietyTransport :=
          BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.ofHWNormalFormTransport
            (d := d) (n := n) r Tnormal
        refine
          { r := r
            hrD := hrD
            hrn := hrn
            adaptedBase := base
            adaptedBase_mem := hbase_ET
            adaptedBase_same_oriented := hbase_oriented
            adapted := adapted
            normalBase := normalBase
            normalBase_eq := rfl
            head_unit := hhead
            tail_zero := htail
            normalTransport := Tnormal
            varietyTransport := varietyTransport
            toOriginal := Tnormal.toNormalVec.symm
            toOriginal_continuous := Tnormal.toNormalVec.symm.continuous
            toOriginal_normalBase_eq_adaptedBase := htoOrig_base
            toOriginal_normalBase_invariant := by
              simpa [htoOrig_base] using hbase_oriented
            toOriginal_oriented := by
              intro z
              simpa [varietyTransport] using
                BHW.sourceOrientedVarietyPoint_toOriginal_of_normalFormTransport
                  (d := d) (n := n) r Tnormal z }

      structure BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0) where
        m : Nat
        c0 : Fin m -> ℂ
        K : Set (Fin m -> ℂ)
        P : Set (Fin m -> ℂ)
        K_compact : IsCompact K
        P_open : IsOpen P
        P_subset_K : P ⊆ K
        c0_mem : c0 ∈ P
        residualVector :
          (Fin m -> ℂ) -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        residualVector_continuousOn :
          ContinuousOn residualVector K
        residualVector_c0 :
          residualVector c0 = N.normalBase
        toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET :
          ∀ c ∈ K,
            N.toOriginal (residualVector c) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        Ω : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n)
        Ω_open : IsOpen Ω
        Ω_center :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 ∈ Ω
        originalInvariant_mem :
          ∀ c ∈ K,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              (N.toOriginal (residualVector c)) ∈
              Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n
        image_surj :
          Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ⊆
            (fun c =>
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (residualVector c))) '' P
        maxRank_dense_original :
          ∀ c ∈ P,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (residualVector c)) ∈
              closure
                ((fun c' =>
                  BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                    (N.toOriginal (residualVector c'))) ''
                  {c' | c' ∈ P ∧
                    BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
                      (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                        (N.toOriginal (residualVector c')))})

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydiscData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N := by
        classical
        rcases
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_tubeResidualPolydisc
            (d := d) hd n N with
          ⟨m, encode, K, P, c0, hK_compact, hP_open, hP_subset_K,
            hc0_mem, hcenter_encode, htube,
            Ω, hΩ_open, hΩ_center, hΩ_image, hΩ_surj, hmax_dense⟩
        refine
          { m := m
            c0 := c0
            K := K
            P := P
            K_compact := hK_compact
            P_open := hP_open
            P_subset_K := hP_subset_K
            c0_mem := hc0_mem
            residualVector := fun c =>
              BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                d n N.r N.hrD N.hrn (encode.symm c)
            residualVector_continuousOn := by
              exact
                (BHW.continuous_sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector
                  (d := d) n N.r N.hrD N.hrn).comp_continuousOn
                  encode.symm.continuous.continuousOn
            residualVector_c0 := by
              have hc0 :
                  encode.symm c0 =
                    BHW.sourceOrientedNormalCenterParameter
                      d n N.r N.hrD N.hrn := by
                apply encode.injective
                simpa using hcenter_encode.symm
              simpa [hc0] using
                BHW.sourceOrientedNormalParameterVector_center
                  (d := d) n N.r N.hrD N.hrn
            toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET := by
              intro c hc
              exact htube c hc
            Ω := Ω
            Ω_open := hΩ_open
            Ω_center := hΩ_center
            originalInvariant_mem := hΩ_image
            image_surj := hΩ_surj
            maxRank_dense_original := hmax_dense }

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_tubeStability
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0)
          (P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N) :
          ∀ c ∈ P.K,
            N.toOriginal (P.residualVector c) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        P.toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_imageSurj
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0)
          (P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N) :
          ∃ Ω : Set (BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n),
            IsOpen Ω ∧
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 ∈ Ω ∧
            (∀ c ∈ P.K,
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (P.residualVector c)) ∈
                Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n) ∧
            Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ⊆
              (fun c =>
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                  (N.toOriginal (P.residualVector c))) '' P.P :=
        ⟨P.Ω, P.Ω_open, P.Ω_center, P.originalInvariant_mem, P.image_surj⟩

      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_maxRankDense
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (N :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData d n z0)
          (P :
            BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData d n N) :
          ∀ c ∈ P.P,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                (N.toOriginal (P.residualVector c)) ∈
              closure
                ((fun c' =>
                  BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                    (N.toOriginal (P.residualVector c'))) ''
                  {c' | c' ∈ P.P ∧
                    BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
                      (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                        (N.toOriginal (P.residualVector c')))}) :=
        P.maxRank_dense_original
      ```

      `sourceOriented_rankDeficient_normalFormData` consumes the ordinary
      adapted Hall-Wightman Lemma-3 representative `adaptedBase` in the
      extended tube, then records the canonical normal-form source
      `normalBase = hwLemma3CanonicalSource d n r` separately.  This
      separation is load-bearing.  The canonical normal source is the point at
      which the head block is the signature diagonal
      `sourceHeadMetric d r hrD` and the tail is zero; it is not asserted to
      lie in `ExtendedTube`.  The inverse map `toOriginal`
      transports normal-form source tuples back to the adapted extended-tube
      coordinates.  The source normal-form transport may be used on actual
      source tuples, but the proof may not require it to be an ambient
      homeomorphism of all oriented Gram/determinant coordinates.
      `sourceOriented_residualPolydiscData` then chooses the closed residual
      polydisc `K` and open interior `P` in normal coordinates after all tube
      shrinkings.  Its field `toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET`, not any
      global invariance of the normal-form transport, is the theorem that
      every chosen residual tuple lands back in `ExtendedTube`.  Its fields
      `Ω`, `originalInvariant_mem`, `image_surj`, and
      `maxRank_dense_original` are already in the original oriented
      coordinates.  In particular, `Ω_open` must be an ambient open set in
      `SourceOrientedGramData`, chosen after the variety-relative normal-form
      image has been pulled back and represented by an ambient open
      neighborhood.  The residual-chart assembly must not manufacture this
      ambient open set by applying an unavailable ambient source-matrix
      coordinate homeomorphism.  Image surjectivity is the Schur theorem: for
      a nearby oriented-variety point, compute its head block, mixed
      coefficients, and residual oriented tail; realize the shifted residual
      tail by
      `sourceShiftedTailSmallRealization`;
      reconstruct the full normal-form source tuple; and transport back.
      Max-rank density perturbs only the residual Schur complement and
      determinant sheet inside `P`, then reconstructs by the same formula.
      The checked connectedness theorem to use after this rewrite is
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchur_varietyTransported_orientedMaxRank_preimage_of_eq`.
      The normal Schur parameter is first bundled as a
      `BHW.SourceOrientedVariety d n` point, then the source-change factor is
      applied by `BHW.SourceOrientedVarietyTransportEquiv.invFun`; its
      `invFun_maxRank_iff` absorbs the inverse transport in the max-rank
      predicate.  The older ambient
      `BHW.isConnected_sourcePrincipalSchur_transported_orientedMaxRank_parameterSet`
      is reserved for genuine full-coordinate oriented-invariant
      homeomorphisms, not for determinant-coordinate source changes.

      The proof body of the chart theorem is consequently fixed.  The Lean
      interface part of this correction is now checked in
      `OSReconstruction/ComplexLieGroups/Connectedness/BHWPermutation/SourceOrientedRankDeficientTubeResidual.lean`:
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData` records the actual
      source-level `toOriginal` return map and its variety-relative
      compatibility, `BHW.SourceOrientedResidualPolydiscData` records the
      compact residual family with original-coordinate `Ω`,
      `originalInvariant_mem`, `image_surj`, and `maxRank_dense_original`, and
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.ofSourceMatrixLorentz`
      checks the concrete return-map construction from an invertible source
      matrix and Lorentz normalizing equation:
      `toOriginal z = sourceTupleLinearChange d n M⁻¹
      (complexLorentzAction Λ⁻¹ z)`,
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientNormalFormData.exists_ofAdaptedBase`
      packages the existing Schur normal-form matrix construction and
      Lorentz/Witt normalization once an adapted ET base is supplied, and
      `BHW.sourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer_of_tubeResidualPolydiscProducer`
      mechanically converts such a producer into the already checked
      `BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartProducer`.  This is only
      the checked interface/reducer; the hard theorem remains the construction
      of the normal-form data and tube residual-polydisc fields.

      Notice that
      it consumes the original-coordinate polydisc fields directly; it does
      not apply `SourceOrientedInvariantTransportEquiv.invFun` to a normal
      open set.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz0 : z0 ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hlow :
            ¬ BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)) :
          BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0 := by
        let N :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_normalFormData
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hlow
        let P :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydiscData
            (d := d) hd n N
        obtain ⟨Ω, hΩ_open, hΩ_center, hΩ_image_mem,
          hΩ_surj⟩ :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_imageSurj
            (d := d) hd n N P
        refine
          { m := P.m
            Ω := Ω
            Ω_open := hΩ_open
            center_mem_ET := hz0
            K := P.K
            K_compact := P.K_compact
            P := P.P
            P_open := P.P_open
            P_subset_K := P.P_subset_K
            c0 := P.c0
            c0_mem := P.c0_mem
            toVec := fun c => N.toOriginal (P.residualVector c)
            toVec_continuousOn :=
              N.toOriginal_continuous.comp_continuousOn
                P.residualVector_continuousOn
            toVec_mem := ?_
            toVec_c0_invariant := ?_
            toInv_eq := ?_
            image_surj := ?_
            maxRank_dense_parameters := ?_ }
        · intro c hc
          exact
            BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_tubeStability
              (d := d) hd n N P c hc
        · simpa [P.residualVector_c0] using
            N.toOriginal_normalBase_invariant
        · intro c hc
          exact hΩ_image_mem c hc
        · intro G hG
          exact hΩ_surj G hG
        · intro c hcP
          exact
            BHW.sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_maxRankDense
              (d := d) hd n N P c hcP
      ```

      The transport lemmas used in this script are finite linear algebra:
      source permutations and determinant-`1` Lorentz changes are homeomorphisms
      of the oriented invariant coordinate space, preserve
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety`, and preserve the max-rank predicate.  They
      do **not** assert that an arbitrary normal-form source tuple remains in
      `ExtendedTube` after inverse source-coordinate transport.  Extended-tube
      membership is supplied only by the shrunk residual-polydisc field
      `toOriginal_residualVector_mem_ET` and its theorem wrapper
      `sourceOriented_residualPolydisc_tubeStability`.  This separation is
      essential: Hall-Wightman's source-coordinate changes are finite
      linear-algebra devices, while tube stability is the analytic
      coefficient-freedom theorem.

      Finally, the compactness arguments used downstream should appear as
      small SCV/topology lemmas rather than as prose inside the descent proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_compactBound
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (R :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0) :
          ∃ C : ℝ, 0 <= C ∧
            ∀ c ∈ R.K, ‖BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)‖ <= C

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (R :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG : G ∈ R.Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
          {c : Fin R.m -> ℂ}
          (hc : c ∈ R.P)
          (hcG :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c) = G) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F G =
            BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_ext_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n))
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (R :
            BHW.SourceOrientedRankDeficientResidualChartData d n z0)
          {c : Fin R.m -> ℂ}
          (hc : c ∈ R.K)
          (hc0 :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c) =
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0) :
          BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c) = BHW.extendF F z0
      ```

      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart` is then
      `compactBound` plus `image_surj` and
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter`.
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart` is the
      finite-dimensional first-countable compact-parameter theorem applied
      to `R.K`, followed by `sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue`.  The
      Lean proof below uses the existing cluster/subsequence APIs
      `IsCompact.tendsto_nhds_of_unique_mapClusterPt`,
      `TopologicalSpace.FirstCountableTopology.tendsto_subseq`, and
      `IsCompact.tendsto_subseq'`; it does not require an extra subnet
      abstraction layer.
      No choice of parameters may be made outside `R.K`; otherwise the
      compactness proof and the branch-law identification break.

      Production-order correction for these compactness helpers: the
      definitions `sourceOrientedQuotientValue` and
      `sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined`, displayed below with the
      descent theorem, must be placed before
      `sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter` in Lean.  The
      residual-chart theorem statements are grouped here because they belong
      mathematically to the rank-deficient chart, but their proof scripts use
      the quotient-value well-definedness lemma.

      The compact-bound proof is ordinary Mathlib compactness.  The only
      source-specific input is that `R.toVec` maps the whole closed parameter
      polydisc into the extended tube:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_compactBound ... := by
        classical
        have hcomp :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> ℂ => BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c))
              R.K := by
          exact hF_ext_cont.comp R.toVec_continuousOn R.toVec_mem
        have hnorm :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> ℂ =>
                ‖BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)‖)
              R.K := hcomp.norm
        obtain ⟨B, hB⟩ :=
          R.K_compact.exists_bound_of_continuousOn
            (f := fun c : Fin R.m -> ℂ =>
              ‖BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c)‖)
            hnorm
        refine ⟨max 0 B, le_max_left _ _, ?_⟩
        intro c hc
        exact le_trans (hB c hc) (le_max_right _ _)
      ```

      The quotient-to-parameter rewrite is also mechanical after
      well-definedness.  The membership proof for `R.toVec c` uses
      `R.P_subset_K`; the fact that `G` lies in the extended-tube oriented
      image is present only to match the caller's local-domain hypotheses:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter ... := by
        have hcK : c ∈ R.K := R.P_subset_K hc
        exact
          BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined
            (d := d) hd n F hBranch
            (R.toVec_mem c hcK) hcG
      ```

      Local boundedness near the rank-deficient point is then not another
      analytic theorem.  Use the residual chart itself as the neighborhood,
      bound the compact parameter family, and rewrite every local quotient
      value through `image_surj`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_locallyBounded_of_residualChart
          ... := by
        classical
        let R :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hlow
        obtain ⟨C, hC_nonneg, hC⟩ :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_compactBound
            (d := d) hd n F hF_ext_cont R
        refine ⟨R.Ω, R.Ω_open, R.center_mem, C, hC_nonneg, ?_⟩
        intro G hG
        rcases R.image_surj ⟨hG.1, ?_⟩ with ⟨c, hcP, hcG⟩
        · have hcK : c ∈ R.K := R.P_subset_K hcP
          have hq :
              BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F G =
                BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c) :=
            BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter
              (d := d) hd n F hBranch R hG hcP hcG
          simpa [hq] using hC c hcK
        · exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_subset_variety
              (d := d) (n := n) hG.2
      ```

      The auxiliary subset theorem in the last line is definitional:
      `sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n` is the image of
      `BHW.ExtendedTube d n` under
      `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n`, while
      `sourceOrientedGramVariety d n` is the full range of that same map.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_subset_variety
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ⊆
            BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n := by
        intro G hG
        rcases hG with ⟨z, _hzET, rfl⟩
        exact ⟨z, rfl⟩
      ```

      Continuity at a rank-deficient point requires one reusable topology
      lemma so the Lean proof does not have to manipulate compact
      subsequences in the middle of the Hall-Wightman descent.  The helper is
      deliberately finite-dimensional/first-countable: it says that a quotient
      function locally represented by a compact parameter family is continuous
      at the base point, provided the base point lies in the represented
      domain and all compact cluster parameters over the base fibre have the
      base value.

      ```lean
      theorem SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_compact_parameter_surjection
          {P Y : Type*}
          [TopologicalSpace P] [FirstCountableTopology P]
          [TopologicalSpace Y] [FirstCountableTopology Y] [T2Space Y]
          {K P0 : Set P} {Ω U : Set Y}
          {y0 : Y}
          {param : P -> Y}
          {value : P -> ℂ}
          {phi : Y -> ℂ}
          (hK_compact : IsCompact K)
          (hP0_sub : P0 ⊆ K)
          (hparam_cont : ContinuousOn param K)
          (hvalue_cont : ContinuousOn value K)
          (hy0U : y0 ∈ U)
          (hy0Ω : y0 ∈ Ω)
          (hΩ_open : IsOpen Ω)
          (hsurj :
            Ω ∩ U ⊆ param '' P0)
          (hphi_param :
            ∀ y, y ∈ Ω ∩ U ->
              ∀ c ∈ P0, param c = y -> phi y = value c)
          (hcluster_base :
            ∀ c ∈ K, param c = y0 -> value c = phi y0) :
          ContinuousWithinAt phi U y0
      ```

      Proof transcript for this helper.  Work from the identity
      `ContinuousWithinAt phi U y0 = Tendsto phi (𝓝[U] y0) (𝓝 (phi y0))`
      and use `Filter.tendsto_iff_seq_tendsto`, which is available because
      `FirstCountableTopology` gives
      `IsCountablyGenerated (𝓝[U] y0)`.  Fix a sequence
      `u : ℕ -> Y` with `hu : Tendsto u atTop (𝓝[U] y0)`.
      The open-neighborhood hypothesis gives
      `hΩ_ev : ∀ᶠ j in atTop, u j ∈ Ω`, and the within-filter gives
      `hU_ev : ∀ᶠ j in atTop, u j ∈ U`.  Since
      `hy0Ω` and `hy0U` put the base point in `Ω ∩ U`, `hsurj`
      supplies a fallback parameter `cBase ∈ P0` with
      `param cBase = y0`.  Define a total chooser
      `cseq : ℕ -> P` by choosing a witness from `hsurj` whenever
      `u j ∈ Ω ∩ U`, and using `cBase` otherwise.  Then eventually
      `cseq j ∈ P0`, `param (cseq j) = u j`, and
      `phi (u j) = value (cseq j)` by `hphi_param`; also eventually
      `cseq j ∈ K` by `hP0_sub`.

      It remains to prove
      `Tendsto (fun j => value (cseq j)) atTop (𝓝 (phi y0))`.
      Let `Kval := value '' K`.  The compactness theorem
      `hK_compact.image_of_continuousOn hvalue_cont` gives compactness of
      `Kval`, and the previous paragraph gives eventual membership in
      `Kval`.  Apply
      `IsCompact.tendsto_nhds_of_unique_mapClusterPt` to `Kval`.  For the
      required uniqueness clause, take any
      `a ∈ Kval` with
      `MapClusterPt a atTop (fun j => value (cseq j))`.  First use
      `TopologicalSpace.FirstCountableTopology.tendsto_subseq` to pass to a
      subsequence on which `value (cseq j)` tends to `a`.  Since the
      corresponding parameter subsequence is still eventually in `K`, apply
      `hK_compact.tendsto_subseq'` to pass to a further subsequence with
      `cseq j -> c*` for some `c* ∈ K`.  Continuity-on-`K` of `value`
      gives `value (cseq j) -> value c*`, while the first subsequence limit
      still gives `value (cseq j) -> a`; `tendsto_nhds_unique` in `ℂ`
      yields `a = value c*`.  Continuity-on-`K` of `param`, the eventual
      equality `param (cseq j) = u j`, and `hu` give
      `param c* = y0`, again by `tendsto_nhds_unique` in the Hausdorff space
      `Y`.  Hence `value c* = phi y0` by `hcluster_base`, so
      `a = phi y0`.  This proves the unique-cluster condition and therefore
      the scalar convergence.  Finally use `Filter.Tendsto.congr'` with the
      eventual equality `phi (u j) = value (cseq j)` and discharge the
      original `ContinuousWithinAt` by
      `Filter.tendsto_iff_seq_tendsto.2`.

      With that helper, the rank-deficient continuity proof has no hidden
      analytic step:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_continuous_of_residualChart
          ... := by
        classical
        let R :=
          BHW.sourceOriented_rankDeficient_residualChart
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hlow
        have hvalue_cont :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> ℂ => BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c))
              R.K := by
          exact hF_ext_cont.comp R.toVec_continuousOn R.toVec_mem
        have hparam_cont :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun c : Fin R.m -> ℂ =>
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c))
              R.K := by
          exact
            (BHW.continuous_sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n).continuousOn.comp
              R.toVec_continuousOn
              (by intro c hc; trivial)
        have hy0U :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0 ∈
              BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n := by
          exact ⟨z0, R.center_mem_ET, rfl⟩
        refine
          SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_compact_parameter_surjection
            (K := R.K) (P0 := R.P)
            (Ω := R.Ω)
            (U := BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
            (y0 := BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z0)
            (param := fun c =>
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (R.toVec c))
            (value := fun c => BHW.extendF F (R.toVec c))
            (phi := BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F)
            R.K_compact R.P_subset_K hparam_cont hvalue_cont hy0U
            R.center_mem R.Ω_open ?_ ?_ ?_
        · intro G hG
          exact R.image_surj ⟨hG.1,
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain_subset_variety
              (d := d) (n := n) hG.2⟩
        · intro G hG c hcP hcG
          exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_quotient_eq_parameter
              (d := d) hd n F hBranch R hG hcP hcG
        · intro c hcK hc0
          exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue
              (d := d) hd n F hF_ext_cont hBranch R hcK hc0
      ```

      Finally, the cluster-value lemma uses the branch law only once.  The
      residual chart stores the original endpoint membership
      `R.center_mem_ET`; it does not try to derive `z0 ∈ ExtendedTube d n`
      from equality of source tuples at the compact-parameter center.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedResidualChart_clusterValue ... := by
        have hz_param : R.toVec c ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          R.toVec_mem c hc
        exact hBranch hz_param R.center_mem_ET hc0
      ```

      At the point where this paragraph was first written, this
      rank-deficient residual package was the main unnamed mathematical gap in
      the oriented source layer.  The later residual-normal-form, Schur-tail,
      compact-polydisc, and compact-parameter continuity packets below now
      decompose it into named theorem surfaces with implementation-level proof
      transcripts.  The remaining gate is Lean implementation of those named
      finite-dimensional/SCV support lemmas; it is not permission to replace
      the oriented residual chart by ordinary
      `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen`, an arbitrary extended-tube lift,
      or a wrapper around the public scalar representative.

      Holomorphic descent through the oriented invariant must then be proved
      by the same regular-stratum/removable-singularity architecture as the
      ordinary Hall-Wightman descent.  Local holomorphic sections are required
      on the maximal-rank oriented stratum; at exceptional rank, the proof
      must use normality and a Riemann extension theorem, not a fictitious
      local section through the singular quotient map.  The representative is
      not an arbitrary function on the ambient coordinate space; it is the
      quotient value of `extendF F` on the oriented image:

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) : ℂ :=
        if hG : G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n then
          BHW.extendF F hG.choose
        else
          0

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hGz : BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z = G) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F G = BHW.extendF F z := by
        classical
        have hGmem :
            G ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n :=
          ⟨z, hz, hGz⟩
        unfold BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue
        rw [dif_pos hGmem]
        let zpick : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ := Classical.choose hGmem
        have hzpick : zpick ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hGmem).1
        have hpickG :
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n zpick = G :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hGmem).2
        exact hBranch hzpick hz (hpickG.trans hGz.symm)

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_holomorphicOn_maxRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n z =
                BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {G0 : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n}
          (hG0 :
            G0 ∈ BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n)
          (hReg : BHW.SourceOrientedMaxRankAt d n G0) :
          ∃ Ω Ψ,
            IsOpen Ω ∧ G0 ∈ Ω ∧
            DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ Ω ∧
            Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n ⊆
              BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n ∧
            Set.EqOn (BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue n F) Ψ
              (Ω ∩ BHW.sourceOrientedGramVariety d n) := by
        obtain S :=
          BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTube_holomorphicLocalSection
            (d := d) hd n hG0 hReg
        refine ⟨S.Ω,
          fun G => BHW.extendF F (S.section G),
          S.Ω_open, S.center_mem, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
        · exact
            (BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn
              (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv).comp
              S.section_holomorphic S.section_mem
        · intro G hG
          exact ⟨S.section G, S.section_mem G hG.1,
            (S.section_right_inv G hG).symm⟩
        · intro G hG
          exact
            BHW.sourceOrientedQuotientValue_wellDefined
              (d := d) hd n F hBranch
              (S.section_mem G hG.1)
              (S.section_right_inv G hG)

      theorem SCV.continuousWithinAt_of_local_eqOn_relNeighborhood
          {E : Type*} [TopologicalSpace E]
          {U V Ω : Set E} {x : E} {phi Ψ : E -> ℂ}
          (hU_sub : U ⊆ V)
          (hxU : x ∈ U)
          (hΩ_open : IsOpen Ω)
          (hxΩ : x ∈ Ω)
      ```
