        BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension
          (d := d) hd n hU_rel hphi_cont hphi_bdd
          hExceptionalAnalytic hDense hregular
      ```

      Step 2 is not a placeholder for "normality of the target".  It must
      contain `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_schurLocalProductModel`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum_complexDimension`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim_ge_two`,
      `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay`, and
      `normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre`, or consume an already implemented
      sorry-free theorem with exactly that finite-dimensional
      algebraic-geometry content.  Step 4 must consume the OS-free
      `normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension`/Riemann
      theorem and return the domain-control field
      `U0 ∩ sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U`.  None of this support may
      mention OS, Wightman fields, extended tubes beyond the source-domain
      input, EOW, PET, theorem-2 locality, `bvt_W`, or
      `sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`.  If any of those appear in the
      normality/removable proof term, the exceptional-rank chart is still not
      production-Lean-ready.

      Implementation-level algebraic source of the last theorem.  The Lean
      port may either prove this in the SCV/algebraic-geometry support layer
      or consume an already implemented sorry-free theorem with exactly this
      content; it may not introduce it as an axiom or as a theorem-specific
      placeholder.  Work in the polynomial ring whose variables are indexed by
      unordered pairs of source indices, build the generic symmetric matrix,
      and let `sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D` be the ideal generated by all
      `(D+1) x (D+1)` minors of that matrix.  The algebraic input is the
      standard symmetric determinantal theorem:

      ```lean
      def BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex (n : Nat) : Type :=
        Sym2 (Fin n)

      def BHW.sourceGenericSymmetricMatrix
          (n : Nat) :
          Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n)
            (MvPolynomial (BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex n) ℂ)

      def BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal
          (n D : Nat) :
          Ideal (MvPolynomial (BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex n) ℂ)

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal_zeroLocus
          (n D : Nat) :
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ |
              Z ∈ BHW.sourceSymmetricMatrixSpace n ∧
              Matrix.rank (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j) <= D}
            =
          BHW.zeroLocus
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D)

      /-- Standard Jozefiak-Pragacz/straightening-law theorem for the
      symmetric determinantal coordinate ring over an algebraically closed
      characteristic-zero field, specialized here to `ℂ`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLE_coordinateRing_prime_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D).IsPrime ∧
          BHW.QuotientRingCohenMacaulay
            (MvPolynomial (BHW.sourceSymmetricCoordIndex n) ℂ)
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal n D)

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.ReducedAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) ∧
            BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) := by
        have hzero :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEIdeal_zeroLocus n D
        have halg :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLE_coordinateRing_prime_cohenMacaulay n D
        exact
          BHW.reduced_cohenMacaulay_analyticSubvariety_of_prime_CM_ideal
            hzero halg.1 halg.2
      ```

      The analytic translation theorem
      `reduced_cohenMacaulay_analyticSubvariety_of_prime_CM_ideal` is pure
      finite-dimensional SCV/commutative algebra: prime gives reduced
      analytic local rings, and the Cohen-Macaulay quotient-ring theorem
      localizes to all analytic local rings.  It carries no extended-tube,
      Wightman, OS, EOW, PET, or locality content.  If this coordinate-ring
      theorem is not available in local support, the normal-removability part
      of `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` is not Lean-ready.

      The Serre assembly is then mechanical and must stay visible:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) :=
        (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay n D).2

      theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne
          (n D : Nat) :
          BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
            (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D) := by
        by_cases hD0 : D = 0
        · exact BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_point_regularInCodimOne
            (n := n) (by simpa [hD0])
        by_cases hDfull : n <= D
        · exact
            BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_fullSpace_regularInCodimOne
              (n := n) (D := D) hDfull
        have hD0' : 0 < D := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hD0
        have hDlt : D < n := Nat.lt_of_not_ge hDfull
        have hsing :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank
            n D hD0' hDlt
        have hcodim :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim_ge_two
            n D hD0' hDlt
        exact
          BHW.regularInCodimensionOne_of_singularLocus_codim_ge_two
            hsing hcodim

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
        have hCM :
            BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)) :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay n (d + 1)
        have hR1 :
            BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)) :=
          BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne n (d + 1)
        have hnormal :
            BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
              (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1)) :=
          BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre hCM hR1
        simpa [BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety]
          using hnormal
      ```

      theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre
          {n : Nat}
          {V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hCM : BHW.CohenMacaulayAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hR1 : BHW.RegularInCodimensionOne V) :
          BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V

      Archived optional strong-ambient bridge:

      The following Stein/Siu/Cartan surfaces are **not** part of the active
      source-scalar route.  They document the additional SCV theorem package
      that would be needed only if the old production predicate
      `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` were kept unchanged.  The local
      Hall-Wightman audit does not prove that `S'_n` is a domain of
      holomorphy or that it admits a global Stein ambient neighborhood; it
      proves the analytic-space germ statement formalized above.  Therefore no
      theorem in this archived block may be moved to production as a wrapper
      around Hall-Wightman, and no implementation of
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` may depend on it unless the
      extra SCV hypotheses are independently proved and explicitly reopened.
      Gemini Deep Research interaction
      `v1_ChZIeHYwYWE5cng1Mzd4Z19ldGRDQURREhZIeHYwYWE5cng1Mzd4Z19ldGRDQURR`
      judged this stronger bridge mathematically sound if Step 1 is sourced
      not as a bare Hall-Wightman assertion, but as the additional
      Bochner-Martin/invariant-quotient theorem that the scalar image `S'_n`
      is a domain of holomorphy in the normal Gram analytic space (with the
      extended-tube/Stein perspective related to Zhou's theorem).  This
      validates the bridge as optional SCV support, not as an already
      Lean-ready consequence of the local Hall-Wightman source import.

      /-- A Stein open set in the finite ambient scalar-coordinate space.
      The Lean port should implement this as the chosen SCV API's Stein
      predicate; mathematically the fields are open, holomorphically
      separable, holomorphically convex on compact subsets, and equivalently
      the existence of a smooth strictly plurisubharmonic exhaustion. -/
      structure BHW.SteinOpen
          {n : Nat}
          (Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)) : Prop where
        isOpen : IsOpen Ω
        holomorphically_separable :
          ∀ Z ∈ Ω, ∀ W ∈ Ω, Z ≠ W ->
            ∃ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
              DifferentiableOn ℂ f Ω ∧ f Z ≠ f W
        holomorphically_convex :
          ∀ K : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
            IsCompact K -> K ⊆ Ω ->
              ∃ L : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
                IsCompact L ∧ L ⊆ Ω ∧ K ⊆ L ∧
                ∀ Z, Z ∈ Ω -> Z ∉ L ->
                  ∃ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
                    DifferentiableOn ℂ f Ω ∧
                    (∀ W, W ∈ K -> ‖f W‖ <= 1) ∧
                    2 <= ‖f Z‖

      /-- Closed analytic subvariety in the finite source-Gram coordinate
      space, stated only at the level needed by the BHW source route. -/
      structure BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)) : Prop where
        isClosed : IsClosed V
        locally_cut_out :
          ∀ Z, ∃ U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
            IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
            ∃ m : Nat, ∃ g : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
              (∀ a, DifferentiableOn ℂ (g a) U0) ∧
              V ∩ U0 = {W | W ∈ U0 ∧ ∀ a, g a W = 0}

      /-- Coherent ideal sheaf cutting out a closed analytic subvariety.  The
      Lean port can replace this with the eventual SCV sheaf API, but the
      theorem-2 route must keep the finite local generator condition visible. -/
      structure BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf
          {n : Nat}
          (V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)) : Prop where
        local_finite_presentation :
          ∀ Z, ∃ U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
            IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
            ∃ m r : Nat,
            ∃ g : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
            ∃ a : Fin r -> Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
              (∀ i, DifferentiableOn ℂ (g i) U0) ∧
              (∀ b i, DifferentiableOn ℂ (a b i) U0) ∧
              V ∩ U0 = {W | W ∈ U0 ∧ ∀ i, g i W = 0} ∧
              (∀ b W, W ∈ U0 ->
                ∑ i : Fin m, a b i W * g i W = 0) ∧
              ∀ h : Fin m -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
                (∀ i, DifferentiableOn ℂ (h i) U0) ->
                (∀ W, W ∈ U0 ->
                  ∑ i : Fin m, h i W * g i W = 0) ->
                ∀ P, P ∈ U0 ->
                  ∃ V0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
                    IsOpen V0 ∧ P ∈ V0 ∧ V0 ⊆ U0 ∧
                    ∃ c : Fin r -> (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
                      (∀ b, DifferentiableOn ℂ (c b) V0) ∧
                      ∀ i, Set.EqOn (h i)
                        (fun W => ∑ b : Fin r, c b W * a b i W) V0

      /-- Stein analytic subspace structure on a relatively open subset of a
      closed analytic subvariety.  The fields deliberately name the
      analytic-space Stein theorem rather than pretending that relative
      openness alone gives a Stein ambient set. -/
      structure BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)) : Prop where
        subset : U ⊆ V
        relOpen : ∃ Ω0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ), IsOpen Ω0 ∧ U = Ω0 ∩ V
        holomorphically_separable :
          ∀ Z ∈ U, ∀ W ∈ U, Z ≠ W ->
            ∃ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
              (∀ P, P ∈ U ->
                ∃ U0 Ψ,
                  IsOpen U0 ∧ P ∈ U0 ∧
                  DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                  Set.EqOn f Ψ (U0 ∩ V)) ∧
              f Z ≠ f W
        holomorphically_convex :
          ∀ K : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
            IsCompact K -> K ⊆ U ->
              ∃ L : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
                IsCompact L ∧ L ⊆ U ∧ K ⊆ L ∧
                ∀ Z, Z ∈ U -> Z ∉ L ->
                  ∃ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
                    (∀ P, P ∈ U ->
                      ∃ U0 Ψ,
                        IsOpen U0 ∧ P ∈ U0 ∧
                        DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                        Set.EqOn f Ψ (U0 ∩ V)) ∧
                    (∀ W, W ∈ K -> ‖f W‖ <= 1) ∧
                    2 <= ‖f Z‖

      The generic relative-open predicate `BHW.IsRelOpenIn` is declared in
      the active standard-geometry block above, before the normal Riemann
      theorem.  The archived Stein/Cartan bridge reuses that same predicate;
      it must not redeclare a second incompatible relative-openness notion.

      /-- A scalar-space holomorphic function analytically continues across a
      boundary point of a relatively open analytic-space domain if it has an
      ambient holomorphic representative on a neighborhood of that boundary
      point which agrees with the original germ on a nonempty relatively open
      piece of the old domain. -/
      structure BHW.AnalyticallyContinuesAcross
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ))
          (f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) : Prop where
        Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)
        Ψ : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ
        Ω_open : IsOpen Ω
        center_mem : Z ∈ Ω ∩ V
        Ψ_hol : DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ Ω
        agreement_set : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)
        agreement_relOpen : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V agreement_set
        agreement_nonempty : agreement_set.Nonempty
        agreement_subset : agreement_set ⊆ U ∩ Ω ∩ V
        agreement : Set.EqOn f Ψ agreement_set

      /-- Domain-of-holomorphy predicate for a relatively open subset of the
      source Gram analytic space.  In this blueprint it is archived support
      for the optional strong ambient API only.  It is deliberately not
      recorded as a Hall-Wightman consequence: the paper gives openness and
      connectedness of `S'_n` and the invariant analytic germs, while also
      warning that this set need not be the natural domain of analyticity for
      field-theory functions. -/
      structure BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn
          {n : Nat}
          (V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)) : Prop where
        subset : U ⊆ V
        relOpen : ∃ Ω0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ), IsOpen Ω0 ∧ U = Ω0 ∩ V
        maximal_for_scalar_holomorphic_functions :
          ∀ Z, Z ∈ frontier U ∩ V ->
            ∃ f : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
              (∀ P, P ∈ U ->
                ∃ U0 Ψ,
                  IsOpen U0 ∧ P ∈ U0 ∧
                  DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                  Set.EqOn f Ψ (U0 ∩ V)) ∧
              ¬ BHW.AnalyticallyContinuesAcross V U f Z

      /-- Closed analytic-subspace structure of the source Gram variety.  This
      packages the already checked closedness and the polynomial defining
      equations: symmetry equations `Z i j - Z j i = 0` plus the
      `(d + 2) x (d + 2)` minors in the strict rank-bounded case. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Optional strong-API theorem, not active: if one independently proves
      that `S'_n` is a domain of holomorphy in the source Gram analytic space,
      then the Stein/Cartan bridge below can be used to recover the old total
      ambient-function API.  This statement is not currently justified as a
      Hall-Wightman source import. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      /-- Cartan-Thullen/Bochner-Martin theorem on normal analytic spaces:
      a domain of holomorphy in a finite-dimensional normal analytic space is
      Stein. -/
      theorem BHW.domainOfHolomorphy_steinAnalyticSubspace
          {n : Nat}
          {V U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hU_domain : BHW.DomainOfHolomorphyIn V U) :
          BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace V U

      /-- Optional strong-API consequence, not active: if the independent
      domain-of-holomorphy theorem above is proved, then the scalar image is
      Stein as an open analytic subspace of the closed source Gram variety.
      The active germ route does not use this theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      Lean-shaped proof:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) := by
        exact BHW.domainOfHolomorphy_steinAnalyticSubspace
          (V := BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          (U := BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal d n)
          (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy
            (d := d) hd n)
      ```

      /-- Siu's Stein-neighborhood theorem in the finite ambient scalar space:
      a Stein analytic subspace of a complex space admits an open Stein
      neighborhood realizing it by intersection with the ambient closed
      analytic subvariety. -/
      theorem BHW.siu_steinNeighborhood_sourceGramSubspace
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U) :
          ∃ Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
            IsOpen Ω ∧ BHW.SteinOpen Ω ∧
            U = Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      /-- Optional Stein-neighborhood support for the source Gram domain.
      This is needed only for the archived total ambient-function API. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat) :
          ∃ Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
            IsOpen Ω ∧ BHW.SteinOpen Ω ∧
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n =
              Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n

      /-- Coherence of the ideal sheaf of the source Gram variety.  In this
      concrete finite-dimensional setting the generators are the symmetry
      equations and the Gram minors, so coherence is the Oka coherence theorem
      for finitely generated ideals of holomorphic functions. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramIdealSheaf_coherent
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Generic Cartan-B restriction theorem in the exact shape consumed
      below: for a coherent ideal sheaf cutting out a closed analytic
      subvariety `V` inside a Stein open set `Ω`, every holomorphic section of
      the structure sheaf on `Ω ∩ V` is the restriction of a holomorphic
      function on `Ω`. -/
      theorem BHW.cartanB_restriction_surjective_for_coherentIdeal
          {n : Nat}
          {Ω V : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hV_closedAnalytic : BHW.ClosedAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hV_ideal : BHW.CoherentIdealSheaf V)
          (hΩ_open : IsOpen Ω)
          (hΩ_stein : BHW.SteinOpen Ω)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hphi_germ :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ Ω ∩ V ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧ U0 ⊆ Ω ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ (U0 ∩ V)) :
          ∃ Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
            DifferentiableOn ℂ Phi Ω ∧
            Set.EqOn Phi phi (Ω ∩ V)

      /-- Cartan theorem B / Oka extension in the form used by the source Gram
      variety: restriction from holomorphic functions on a Stein ambient
      neighborhood to holomorphic sections on the closed analytic subvariety is
      surjective.  The ideal sheaf is coherent because the source Gram variety
      is cut out by finitely many Gram minors. -/
      theorem BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety
          (d n : Nat)
          {Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hΩ_open : IsOpen Ω)
          (hΩ_stein : BHW.SteinOpen Ω)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hphi_germ :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧ U0 ⊆ Ω ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          ∃ Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
            DifferentiableOn ℂ Phi Ω ∧
            Set.EqOn Phi phi
              (Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Archived Cartan/Oka extension bridge forced only by the old
      production API `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn`.  A holomorphic scalar germ
      on the closed
      source Gram analytic subspace, given by local ambient representatives
      that agree as germs on the variety, can be represented by one total
      ambient function `Phi` near the relatively open scalar domain.  The proof
      uses a Stein neighborhood of the source Gram domain in the finite ambient
      scalar-coordinate space and Cartan theorem B for the ideal sheaf of the
      closed analytic subvariety.  This is not part of the current
      Hall-Wightman route unless the Stein-neighborhood input is separately
      proved. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            ∃ Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
              IsOpen Ω ∧ BHW.SteinOpen Ω ∧
              U = Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hlocal :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ U ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U) :
          ∃ Phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
            BHW.SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn d n Phi U ∧
            Set.EqOn Phi phi U

      Archived Lean-shaped proof of the optional Stein/Cartan bridge:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat) := by
        have hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected
            (d := d) hd n).1
        have hU_stein :
            BHW.SteinAnalyticSubspace
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace
            (d := d) hd n
        exact BHW.siu_steinNeighborhood_sourceGramSubspace
          (d := d) (n := n) hU_rel hU_stein

      theorem BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety ... := by
        -- Let `V := sourceComplexGramVariety d n`.
        -- `sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic` gives the coherent ideal
        -- sheaf `I_V` generated by the symmetry equations and Gram minors.
        -- The sequence `0 -> I_V -> O_Ω -> O_{Ω ∩ V} -> 0` is exact.
        -- Since `Ω` is Stein, Cartan theorem B gives `H^1(Ω, I_V) = 0`;
        -- hence global sections of `O_Ω` surject onto global sections of
        -- `O_{Ω ∩ V}`.  The local data `hphi_germ` is exactly the section of
        -- `O_{Ω ∩ V}` represented by `phi`, so choose its ambient lift
        -- `PhiΩ`.  Extend `PhiΩ` arbitrarily outside `Ω`; the conclusion only
        -- asks for `DifferentiableOn ℂ Phi Ω` and equality on `Ω ∩ V`.
        exact BHW.cartanB_restriction_surjective_for_coherentIdeal
          (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic d n)
          (BHW.sourceGramIdealSheaf_coherent d n)
          hΩ_open hΩ_stein hphi_germ

      theorem BHW.sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension
          [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hU_stein :
            ∃ Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ),
              IsOpen Ω ∧ BHW.SteinOpen Ω ∧
              U = Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hlocal :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ U ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U) := by
        rcases hU_stein with ⟨Ω, hΩ_open, hΩ_stein, hU_eq⟩
        have hphi_germ :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧ U0 ⊆ Ω ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro Z hZ
          have hZU : Z ∈ U := by simpa [hU_eq] using hZ
          rcases hlocal Z hZU with
            ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hEq, hU0_sub⟩
          refine ⟨U0 ∩ Ω, Ψ, hU0_open.inter hΩ_open,
            ⟨hZU0, hZ.1⟩, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
          · exact fun W hW => hW.2
          · exact hΨ_diff.mono (by intro W hW; exact hW.1)
          · intro W hW
            exact hEq ⟨hW.1.1, hW.2⟩
        rcases BHW.cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety
            (d := d) (n := n) hΩ_open hΩ_stein hphi_germ with
          ⟨Phi, hPhi_diff, hPhi_eq_phi⟩
        refine ⟨Phi, ?_, ?_⟩
        · intro Z hZU
          have hZΩV : Z ∈ Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
            simpa [hU_eq] using hZU
          refine ⟨Ω, hΩ_open, hZΩV.1, hPhi_diff, ?_⟩
          intro W hW
          simpa [hU_eq] using hW
        · intro Z hZU
          have hZΩV : Z ∈ Ω ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
            simpa [hU_eq] using hZU
          exact hPhi_eq_phi hZΩV
      ```

      /-- Riemann extension on the normal source Gram variety, in exactly the
      form needed by Hall-Wightman's Bochner-Martin conclusion.  A scalar
      germ which is ambient holomorphic on the maximal-rank regular locus and
      continuous or locally bounded on the whole `S'_n` domain has a strong
      local ambient holomorphic representative at every point.  Implement
      `BHW.LocallyBoundedOn` before this theorem; its exact definition is
      displayed again in the chart packet below. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z})
          (hMaxDense :
            U ⊆ closure
              (U ∩ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}))
          (hregular :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ U ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          ∀ Z, Z ∈ U ->
            ∃ U0 Ψ,
              IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
              DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
              U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U ∧
              Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Generic normal analytic-space Riemann extension theorem in a
      finite-dimensional complex ambient vector space.  This is the SCV theorem
      consumed by both the ordinary Gram route and the oriented Gram-plus-volume
      route.  It is allowed as a standard complex-analytic support theorem only;
      it carries no QFT or locality content. -/
      theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension
          {E : Type*}
          [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℂ E]
          [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional ℂ E]
          {V U A : Set E}
          {phi : E -> ℂ}
          (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V U)
          (hA_analytic : BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn V A)
          (hDense : U ⊆ closure (U \ A))
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hregular :
            ∀ x, x ∈ U \ A ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ x ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                U0 ∩ V ⊆ U ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ (U0 ∩ V)) :
          ∀ x, x ∈ U ->
            ∃ U0 Ψ,
              IsOpen U0 ∧ x ∈ U0 ∧
              DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
              U0 ∩ V ⊆ U ∧
              Set.EqOn phi Ψ (U0 ∩ V)

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.to_isRelOpenIn
          {d n : Nat}
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          BHW.IsRelOpenIn (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U
      ```

      Proof transcript for these standard-geometry inputs:

      1. The finite coordinate change diagonalizing the complexified
         Minkowski form is the already-implemented
         `BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv`; its preservation theorem is
         the already-implemented
         `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_eq_dot_after_equiv`.  Over `ℂ`,
         signs are removed by multiplying the time coordinate by `I`; the
         linear equivalence is explicit in coordinates and the preservation
         theorem is a direct finite sum calculation.  Production Lean should
         not introduce a second "orthogonal model" definition for the same
         map.
      2. `complexMinkowski_wittExtension` is Witt extension for a
         nondegenerate symmetric bilinear form over an algebraically closed
         field of characteristic not two, transported back through the model
         above.  In Lean this can be proved by a Gram-Schmidt/Witt basis
         construction: split off hyperbolic pairs for the radical, extend an
         isometry on the nondegenerate quotient, then add matching orthogonal
         complements.
         Local audit: Mathlib/local search does not expose a ready theorem
         named `Witt`, `wittExtension`, or `extend_isometry` for bilinear
         forms.  Therefore this is a real ordinary finite-dimensional
         support theorem, not an import-name lookup.

         The production theorem must be the general subspace-isometry form
         first:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension
             {D : Nat}
             {M N : Submodule ℂ (Fin D -> ℂ)}
             (T : M ≃ₗ[ℂ] N)
             (hT :
               ∀ x y : M,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   ((T x : N) : Fin D -> ℂ)
                   ((T y : N) : Fin D -> ℂ) =
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (x : Fin D -> ℂ) (y : Fin D -> ℂ)) :
             ∃ A : (Fin D -> ℂ) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (Fin D -> ℂ),
               (∀ u v,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear (A u) (A v) =
                   BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear u v) ∧
               ∀ x : M, A (x : Fin D -> ℂ) = (T x : Fin D -> ℂ)
         ```

         Its proof is not a one-theorem import.  The Lean port should first
         expose the standard form as a `LinearMap.BilinForm` so Mathlib's
         orthogonal-complement API can be used directly:

         ```lean
         def BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm (D : Nat) :
             LinearMap.BilinForm ℂ (Fin D -> ℂ)

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_apply
             (D : Nat) (u v : Fin D -> ℂ) :
             BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D u v =
               BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear u v

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_isRefl
             (D : Nat) :
             (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).IsRefl

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_isSymm
             (D : Nat) :
             (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).IsSymm

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_nondegenerate
             (D : Nat) :
             (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).Nondegenerate

         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_dualVectorOfLinearFunctional
             (D : Nat)
             (ell : (Fin D -> ℂ) ->ₗ[ℂ] ℂ)
             (v : Fin D -> ℂ) :
             BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
               (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricDualVectorOfLinearFunctional D ell)
               v = ell v
         ```

         The nondegeneracy and dual-vector proofs are finite coordinate
         calculations against `Pi.basisFun`, exactly parallel to the checked
         source-Minkowski theorem
         `BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_dualVectorOfLinearFunctional`.
         The Mathlib APIs pinned for the proof are
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.orthogonal`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.mem_orthogonal_iff`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.isCompl_span_singleton_orthogonal`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_orthogonal_spanSingleton`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.toDual`,
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.apply_toDual_symm_apply`,
         `Submodule.exists_isCompl`, `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl`,
         `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl`, `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton`,
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl`, and `LinearMap.exists_extend`.
         Local API audit, 2026-05-01: projection and complement maps should be
         used in method form where possible:
         `(ℂ ∙ u).linearProjOfIsCompl C hC`,
         `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_left hC`,
         `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_right hC`,
         `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_left hC`,
         `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_right hC`,
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl hC φ ψ`,
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl_left_apply hC`, and
         `LinearMap.ofIsCompl_right_apply hC`.  The extension theorem has
         exact shape
         `LinearMap.exists_extend (f : M ->ₗ[ℂ] ℂ) :
          ∃ g : U ->ₗ[ℂ] ℂ, g.comp M.subtype = f`
         for `M : Submodule ℂ U`.  The dual-vector orientation is
         `LinearMap.BilinForm.apply_toDual_symm_apply`:
         if `p0 := (BU.toDual hU).symm ellU`, then
         `BU p0 v = ellU v`; any proof needing `BU v p0` must explicitly use
         symmetry of the restricted form.

         The induction theorem should be proved in the restricted ambient form
         below, then specialized to `U = U' = ⊤`.  This avoids an otherwise
         false same-ambient induction step: after splitting an anisotropic
         line or hyperbolic plane, the domain and codomain induction ambients
         are different orthogonal complements.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension_restricted
             {D : Nat}
             {U U' : Submodule ℂ (Fin D -> ℂ)}
             (hU :
               ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                 Nondegenerate)
             (hU' :
               ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U').
                 Nondegenerate)
             (hdimUU' :
               Module.finrank ℂ U = Module.finrank ℂ U')
             {M : Submodule ℂ U} {N : Submodule ℂ U'}
             (T : M ≃ₗ[ℂ] N)
             (hT :
               ∀ x y : M,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (((T x : N) : U') : Fin D -> ℂ)
                   (((T y : N) : U') : Fin D -> ℂ) =
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (((x : M) : U) : Fin D -> ℂ)
                   (((y : M) : U) : Fin D -> ℂ)) :
             ∃ A : U ≃ₗ[ℂ] U',
               (∀ u v : U,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   ((A u : U') : Fin D -> ℂ)
                   ((A v : U') : Fin D -> ℂ) =
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (u : Fin D -> ℂ) (v : Fin D -> ℂ)) ∧
               ∀ x : M, A (x : U) = (T x : U')
         ```

         The restricted theorem proceeds by well-founded induction on
         `Module.finrank ℂ M`, with these finite-dimensional sublemmas.  They
         remain required before the Witt packet is Lean-ready:

         * `standardComplexSymmetric_anisotropic_line_split`: for
           `u : U` with `B u u ≠ 0`, the line `ℂ ∙ u` is nondegenerate and
           complementary to its restricted orthogonal complement.

           ```lean
           theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_anisotropic_line_split
               {D : Nat}
               {U : Submodule ℂ (Fin D -> ℂ)}
               (hU :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                   Nondegenerate)
               {u : U}
               (hu : ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   u u ≠ 0) :
               let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
               let L : Submodule ℂ U := ℂ ∙ u
               (BU.restrict L).Nondegenerate ∧
               IsCompl L (BU.orthogonal L) ∧
               (BU.restrict (BU.orthogonal L)).Nondegenerate
           ```

           Proof: convert `hu` to
           `¬ BU.IsOrtho u u`, apply
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.isCompl_span_singleton_orthogonal`, obtain
           nondegeneracy of the line from
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal`
           and nondegeneracy of the orthogonal complement from
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_orthogonal_spanSingleton
           BU hU BU_isRefl`.  The only form-specific input is
           `standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm_isRefl`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_hyperbolic_plane_split`: if
           `B u u = 0`, `B p p = 0`, and `B u p = 1`, then
           `H = span ℂ {u, p}` is nondegenerate and complementary to `Hᗮ`.

           ```lean
           theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_hyperbolic_plane_split
               {D : Nat}
               {U : Submodule ℂ (Fin D -> ℂ)}
               (hU :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                   Nondegenerate)
               {u p : U}
               (huu :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   u u = 0)
               (hpp :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   p p = 0)
               (hup :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U)
                   u p = 1) :
               let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
               let H : Submodule ℂ U :=
                 Submodule.span ℂ ({u, p} : Set U)
               (BU.restrict H).Nondegenerate ∧
               IsCompl H (BU.orthogonal H) ∧
               (BU.restrict (BU.orthogonal H)).Nondegenerate
           ```

           Proof transcript: first prove `u ≠ 0` by pairing `u = 0` with `p`,
           and `p ≠ 0` similarly.  Prove
           `LinearIndependent ℂ ![u, p]`: if `a • u + b • p = 0`, pair with
           `p` to get `a = 0` from `huu`, `hpp`, `hup`, and symmetry; pair
           with `u` to get `b = 0`.  For nondegeneracy, take
           `x : H` with `∀ y : H, BU x y = 0`.  Write
           `(x : U) = a • u + b • p` using the `Fin 2` span
           coordinates from the linear-independent pair.  Orthogonality to
           `p` gives `a = 0`; orthogonality to `u` gives `b = 0`; hence
           `x = 0`.  The right-kernel side follows from symmetry.  Then
           `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal
           BU_isRefl` gives `IsCompl H (BU.orthogonal H)`, and the
           orthogonal-complement restriction is nondegenerate by applying the
           same theorem to that complement, using `BU.orthogonal_orthogonal`
           with `hU` and reflexivity.
         * `standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical`: define the radical of
           the restricted form on a subspace by

           ```lean
           def BHW.standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical
               {D : Nat} (U : Submodule ℂ (Fin D -> ℂ))
               (M : Submodule ℂ U) : Submodule ℂ M :=
             { x : M | ∀ y : M,
                 BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinear
                   (((x : M) : U) : Fin D -> ℂ)
                   (((y : M) : U) : Fin D -> ℂ) = 0 }
           ```

         * `standardComplexSymmetric_all_isotropic_imp_restrictedRadical_top`:
           if `∀ x : M, B x x = 0`, then the restricted radical is `⊤`.
           The proof is the polarization identity over `ℂ`:
           `0 = B (x + y) (x + y) =
             B x x + B x y + B y x + B y y = 2 * B x y`;
           `two_ne_zero` gives `B x y = 0`.  This replaces the earlier
           polynomial-existence sketch and is the exact Lean route.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_subspace_has_anisotropic_or_radical`:
           if `M ≠ ⊥`, then either
           `∃ u : M, u ≠ 0 ∧ B u u ≠ 0`, or
           `∃ u : M, u ≠ 0 ∧ u ∈
             BHW.standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical U M`.
           In Lean, use `Submodule.exists_mem_ne_zero_of_ne_bot`; if the
           anisotropic alternative is false, the previous polarization lemma
           puts every nonzero vector of `M` in the restricted radical.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_radical_partner_of_isCompl`: if
           `u : M` is nonzero and lies in the restricted radical, and
           `hC : IsCompl (ℂ ∙ u) C` in `M`, construct a hyperbolic partner
           `p : U` orthogonal to `C`.  This is the exact theorem surface; it
           is the key place where the proof uses nondegeneracy of the current
           induction ambient `U`, not nondegeneracy of the full coordinate
           space.

           ```lean
           theorem BHW.standardComplexSymmetric_radical_partner_of_isCompl
               {D : Nat}
               {U : Submodule ℂ (Fin D -> ℂ)}
               (hU :
                 ((BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U).
                   Nondegenerate)
               {M : Submodule ℂ U}
               {u : M}
               (hu_ne : u ≠ 0)
               (hu_rad :
                 u ∈ BHW.standardComplexSymmetricRestrictedRadical U M)
               {C : Submodule ℂ M}
               (hC : IsCompl (ℂ ∙ u) C) :
               let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
               ∃ p : U,
                 BU ((u : M) : U) p = 1 ∧
                 BU p p = 0 ∧
                 (∀ c : C, BU ((c : M) : U) p = 0)
           ```

           The coefficient functional is not informal, and the representing
           vector is obtained inside the current nondegenerate induction
           ambient `U`:

           ```lean
           let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
           let L : Submodule ℂ M := ℂ ∙ u
           let coeffLine : L ->ₗ[ℂ] ℂ :=
             (LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton ℂ M u hu_ne).symm.toLinearMap
           let coord : M ->ₗ[ℂ] ℂ :=
             coeffLine.comp (L.linearProjOfIsCompl C hC)
           obtain ⟨ellU, hellU⟩ := LinearMap.exists_extend coord
           let p0 : U := (BU.toDual hU).symm ellU
           let p : U := p0 - ((BU p0 p0) / 2) • ((u : M) : U)
           ```

           The proof obligations are:

           ```lean
           have hcoord_u : coord u = 1 := by
             -- `L.linearProjOfIsCompl C hC` is identity on `L`, and
             -- `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton_symm_apply_self` gives
             -- coefficient `1` on `u`.
           have hcoord_C : ∀ c : C, coord (c : M) = 0 := by
             intro c
             -- `Submodule.linearProjOfIsCompl_apply_right hC c`
           have hp0_u_right :
               BU p0 ((u : M) : U) = 1 := by
             -- `apply_toDual_symm_apply` plus `hellU` and `hcoord_u`
           have hp0_C_right :
               ∀ c : C, BU p0 ((c : M) : U) = 0 := by
             intro c
             -- `apply_toDual_symm_apply` plus `hellU` and `hcoord_C c`
           ```

           Symmetry of `BU` turns these into the left-oriented pairings
           needed by the theorem statement.  Since `u` is radical in `M`,
           `BU ((u : M) : U) ((u : M) : U) = 0` and
           `BU ((u : M) : U) ((c : M) : U) = 0` for every `c : C`; symmetry
           gives the reversed pairings.  Therefore the adjustment
           `p = p0 - (BU p0 p0 / 2) • u` preserves
           `BU u p = 1` and `BU c p = 0`, while
           `BU p p = 0` is the explicit calculation
           `B00 - (B00 / 2) * 1 - (B00 / 2) * 1 + 0 = 0`, closed by `ring`
           after rewriting `2 ≠ 0` in `ℂ`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_isometry_step_anisotropic`: choose
           `u : M` with `B u u ≠ 0`.  Then `T u` has the same nonzero norm.
           The complement used for the recursive subproblem is not an
           unspecified set intersection; it is the restricted orthogonal
           complement inside `M`:

           ```lean
           let BU := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U
           let BU' := (BHW.standardComplexSymmetricBilinForm D).restrict U'
           let uU : U := (u : M)
           let TuU' : U' := ((T u : N) : U')
           let L : Submodule ℂ U := ℂ ∙ uU
           let L' : Submodule ℂ U' := ℂ ∙ TuU'
           let U1 : Submodule ℂ U := BU.orthogonal L
           let U1' : Submodule ℂ U' := BU'.orthogonal L'
           let C : Submodule ℂ M :=
             { x : M | ((x : M) : U) ∈ U1 }
           let C' : Submodule ℂ N :=
             { y : N | ((y : N) : U') ∈ U1' }
           ```

           Because `BU uU uU ≠ 0`, every `x : M` decomposes by the explicit
           projection formula
           `x = ((BU ((x:M):U) uU / BU uU uU) • u) +
             (x - (BU ((x:M):U) uU / BU uU uU) • u)`,
           and the second summand lies in `C`.  This gives
           `hMC : IsCompl (ℂ ∙ u) C` inside `M`.  The same construction gives
           `hNC : IsCompl (ℂ ∙ T u) C'` inside `N`.  The preservation
           hypothesis gives

           ```lean
           let fC : C ->ₗ[ℂ] C' :=
             (T.toLinearMap.comp C.subtype).codRestrict C' (by
               intro c
               -- use `hT c u` to rewrite orthogonality to `Tu`)
           let gC : C' ->ₗ[ℂ] C :=
             (T.symm.toLinearMap.comp C'.subtype).codRestrict C (by
               intro c'
               -- use `hT (T.symm c') u` and `T.apply_symm_apply`)
           let T_C : C ≃ₗ[ℂ] C' :=
             LinearEquiv.ofLinear fC gC
               (by ext c; simp [fC, gC])
               (by ext c'; simp [fC, gC])
           ```

           In the recursive call, `C` and `C'` are viewed as submodules of
           `U1` and `U1'` by codomain restriction of their subtype maps; the
           proofs that their images land in the orthogonal ambients are their
           defining membership fields.  The ambient nondegeneracy hypotheses
           are supplied by
           `standardComplexSymmetric_anisotropic_line_split`.  The ambient
           finrank equality is

           ```lean
           have hdimU1 :
               Module.finrank ℂ U1 = Module.finrank ℂ U1' := by
             rw [LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU L,
                 LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU' L']
             have hL : Module.finrank ℂ L = 1 := by
               simpa using
                 (LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton ℂ U uU huU_ne).finrank_eq
             have hL' : Module.finrank ℂ L' = 1 := by
               simpa using
                 (LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton ℂ U' TuU' hTu_ne).finrank_eq
             omega
           ```

           The line map is
           `(LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton ℂ U uU huU_ne).symm ≪≫ₗ
             LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton ℂ U' TuU' hTu_ne`.
           After the recursive equivalence `A1 : U1 ≃ₗ[ℂ] U1'` is obtained,
           the global extension is the product-conjugate

           ```lean
           let decU := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl L U1 hLU
           let decU' := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl L' U1' hLU'
           let A : U ≃ₗ[ℂ] U' :=
             decU.symm.trans ((Aline.prod A1).trans decU')
           ```

           `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_left/right` proves that
           `A` sends `u` to `T u` and agrees with the recursive map on `C`;
           the product orthogonality and the two component isometry proofs
           prove preservation of `BU`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_isometry_step_radical`: choose
           `u : M` in the restricted radical and a complement
           `hC : IsCompl (ℂ ∙ u) C` from `Submodule.exists_isCompl`.
           Transport `C` to `C' = C.map T.toLinearMap` and use the partner
           theorem to obtain `p` for `(u, C)` and `p'` for
           `(T u, C')`.  The map on hyperbolic planes is
           `u ↦ T u`, `p ↦ p'`; it is constructed from the two ordered bases
           of `H = span ℂ {uU, p}` and
           `H' = span ℂ {TuU', p'}` obtained in
           `standardComplexSymmetric_hyperbolic_plane_split`.  The Gram
           matrix in both bases is `!![0, 1; 1, 0]`, so this plane map is an
           isometry.

           The recursive subspaces are explicitly the images of `C` and `C'`
           inside the orthogonal complements:

           ```lean
           let H : Submodule ℂ U := Submodule.span ℂ ({uU, p} : Set U)
           let H' : Submodule ℂ U' := Submodule.span ℂ ({TuU', p'} : Set U')
           let U1 : Submodule ℂ U := BU.orthogonal H
           let U1' : Submodule ℂ U' := BU'.orthogonal H'
           let C_in_U1 : Submodule ℂ U1 :=
             (⊤ : Submodule ℂ C).map
               (LinearMap.codRestrict U1
                 (M.subtype.comp C.subtype)
                 (by
                   intro c
                   -- orthogonal to `u` by radicality, orthogonal to `p` by
                   -- the partner theorem, hence orthogonal to all of `H`))
           ```

           Define `C'_in_U1'` analogously.  The restricted map
           `T_C : C_in_U1 ≃ₗ[ℂ] C'_in_U1'` is induced by `T` and the
           definition `C' = C.map T.toLinearMap`; preservation of the form is
           the original `hT` restricted to `C`.  The recursive ambient finrank
           equality is again a direct subtraction:

           ```lean
           have hdimU1 :
               Module.finrank ℂ U1 = Module.finrank ℂ U1' := by
             rw [LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU H,
                 LinearMap.BilinForm.finrank_orthogonal hU' H']
             have hH : Module.finrank ℂ H = 2 := by
               -- ordered hyperbolic basis `{u,p}`
             have hH' : Module.finrank ℂ H' = 2 := by
               -- ordered hyperbolic basis `{T u,p'}`
             omega
           ```

           After induction gives `A1 : U1 ≃ₗ[ℂ] U1'`, combine it with the
           hyperbolic-plane isometry exactly as in the anisotropic branch:

           ```lean
           let decU := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl H U1 hHU1
           let decU' := Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl H' U1' hHprimeU1prime
           let A : U ≃ₗ[ℂ] U' :=
             decU.symm.trans ((AH.prod A1).trans decU')
           ```

           `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl_symm_apply_left/right` proves the
           agreement on `H` and on the recursive complement.  Agreement with
           the original `T` on all of `M` follows from the decomposition
           `hC : IsCompl (ℂ ∙ u) C`: the line component is handled by
           `AH u = T u`, and the complement component by the recursive
           agreement on `C`.
         * `standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension_induction`: combines the
           anisotropic and radical cases.  The measure drops because in each
           branch the recursive subspace is a complement to a nonzero line in
           `M`; use `Submodule.prodEquivOfIsCompl` to identify
           `M ≃ₗ (ℂ ∙ u) × C` and close
           `Module.finrank ℂ C < Module.finrank ℂ M` by
           `LinearEquiv.finrank_eq`, `Module.finrank_prod`, and
           `LinearEquiv.toSpanNonzeroSingleton`.

         After `standardComplexSymmetric_wittExtension`, the Minkowski theorem
         is mechanical: conjugate `T` by
         `BHW.complexMinkowskiToDotLinearEquiv d`, apply the standard
         extension, conjugate the resulting global isometry back, and package
         its matrix as `ComplexLorentzGroup d` using the preservation
         equation.  This packaging is valid only when the extended global
         isometry has determinant `1`.  If the source subspace has codimension
         one, choose the sign on the orthogonal complement to force determinant
         `1`; if it is the whole ambient space, the determinant is already
         fixed by `T`, and same scalar products alone do not force it to be
         `1`.  Consequently the full-rank high-branch consumer must either
         carry Hall-Wightman's improper-component invariance or call a
         full-complex-orthogonal BHW source theorem.  The determinant-`1`
         shortcut `complexMinkowski_wittExtension` is obtained from
         `complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` only for the
         determinant-`1` case, by reversing the equality orientation if needed.
      3. `hwLemma3_adaptedBase_transport_smallPerturbation_extendedTube` is
         the quantitative Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 in Schur-complement
         coordinates.  The public theorem first replaces the given
         extended-tube representative by an adapted same-Gram representative
         whose vector span has dimension equal to the scalar rank; this is the
         exact first reduction in Hall-Wightman's proof and avoids the false
         operation of killing radical tail vectors by an invertible source
         change.  For the adapted base, choose a principal rank `r` minor,
         move it to the upper-left block, normalize it to the identity, and
         write nearby rank-`<= d + 1` matrices as
         `[[A, B], [Bᵀ, Bᵀ A⁻¹ B + S]]` with `S` symmetric of rank
         `<= d + 1 - r`.  Realize `A` and `B` by analytic perturbations of the
         first `r` source vectors and realize `S` by vectors in the
         `(d + 1 - r)`-dimensional orthogonal complement via
         `complexMinkowski_realizeSmallSymmetricRankLE_inOrthogonalTail`.
         The estimates are Hall-Wightman's inequality (48), with `4 - r`
         replaced by `d + 1 - r`.  Choose `ε` smaller than the coordinate tube
         radius at the adapted representative; then all realized perturbations
         remain in the ordinary extended tube.
      4. `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety` follows
         from the same Schur realization: every source Gram matrix is
         symmetric and has rank at most `d + 1`; conversely every complex
         symmetric matrix of rank `<= d + 1` is congruent to
         `diag(1, ..., 1, 0, ..., 0)` and is realized by `d + 1` standard
         complex source vectors.  The existing checked companion
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_rank_le` is always the conjunction of
         symmetry and the rank bound, never the bare rank condition.
      5. `sourceComplexGramVariety_normal` is not a QFT theorem.  The Lean port
         should prove or import the standard symmetric-determinantal package in
         this order:

         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_schurLocalProductModel`: choose a
           rank-`r` principal block by symmetric Gaussian elimination and
           reindex it by `e : Fin n ≃ r ⊕ q`.  On the determinant-unit patch,
           use the checked coordinate equality
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_rankLE_image_eq_openCoordinatePatch`:
           membership in `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D` is equivalent to
           the Schur coordinate
           `S = C - Bᵀ A⁻¹ B` being symmetric and having rank
           `<= D-r`.  The inverse chart is
           `Z ↦ (A(Z), B(Z), S(Z))`; the forward chart is
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph n e A B S`.  The block-recovery lemmas
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_toBlocks₁₁`,
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_toBlocks₁₂`, and
           `sourcePrincipalSchurGraph_schurComplement` give
           `to_inv_on` and `inv_to_on`.  All coordinate maps are holomorphic
           on the determinant-unit patch because matrix inverse is
           holomorphic there and the remaining operations are polynomial.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_singularLocus_eq_lowerRank`: in the
           nontrivial range `0 < D < n`, a rank-`D` point has residual cone
           `rank <= 0`, hence the Schur product is smooth.  A rank
           `r < D` point has residual cone `Sym_{n-r}^{<= D-r}` at `0`, with
           `0 < D-r < n-r`; all defining `(D-r+1)`-minors have vanishing
           linear part at `0`, so the Zariski tangent space is the whole
           symmetric ambient space while the cone has smaller dimension.
           Therefore those and only those points are singular.  The cases
           `D = 0` and `n <= D` are separated before this theorem: a point and
           the full symmetric vector space are smooth.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum_complexDimension`: the
           determinant-unit Schur chart at rank `D` has free symmetric
           `A`-coordinates and rectangular `B`-coordinates, with
           `S = 0`; its dimension is
           `D*(D+1)/2 + D*(n-D) = n*D - D*(D-1)/2`.  In Lean this reuses the
           already documented checked dimension theorem
           `finrank_sourceSelectedComplexSymCoordSubspace` and the arithmetic
           packet
           `sourceRankExactChartDim_sub_previous`.
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_lowerRank_codim`: subtract the same
           dimension formula for ranks `D` and `D-1` to get
           `n - D + 1`; because the singular case has `D < n`, this is at
           least two.  This is the exact proof of
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne`, not a phrase
           "singular locus has codimension at least two".
         * `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_reduced_cohenMacaulay`: use the
           standard symmetric-determinantal straightening-law theorem over
           `ℂ` (equivalently the Jozefiak-Pragacz resolution) to get reduced
           and Cohen-Macaulay local rings.  If the Lean port imports this as a
           standard algebraic-geometry theorem, the imported theorem must be
           stated for symmetric determinantal varieties over an algebraically
           closed field of characteristic zero and specialized to `ℂ`; it may
           not mention OS, Wightman fields, locality, extended tubes, or the
           scalar representative target.
         * Apply `normalAnalyticSubvariety_of_serre` to
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_cohenMacaulay` and
           `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_regularInCodimOne`; finally rewrite
           through
           `sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety`.

         The local Schur chart is the same algebraic chart family used in the
         checked rank-exact source infrastructure, but here its role is
         commutative algebra/normality, not analytic continuation.
      6. `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank` is
         the density/properness input for the removable theorem.  In the hard
         range `d + 1 < n`, it is the checked rank-exact density theorem
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_rankExact`,
         because max rank is `d + 1`; in the easy range `n <= d + 1`, it is
         the elementary density of invertible symmetric matrices inside the
         full symmetric coordinate space.  This theorem is what prevents the
         exceptional analytic set from becoming an unconstrained component.

         Implementation-level density proof.  Work first on the symmetric
         rank-bounded model `sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D`.

         ```lean
         /-- Rank-exact points are dense in the symmetric rank-`<= D`
         determinantal variety.  At a point of rank `r <= D`, choose a
         determinant-unit principal `r`-block and use the Schur product model.
         In the residual symmetric cone, add the diagonal path
         `t • diag(1,...,1,0,...)` of rank `D-r`; its Schur graph has total
         rank `D`, stays in rank `<= D`, and tends to the original point as
         `t -> 0`.  The cases `r = D`, `D = 0`, and `n <= D` are immediate. -/
         theorem BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_rankExact_dense
             (n D : Nat) :
             BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n D ⊆
               closure (BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n (min D n))

         theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_maxRank_dense
             (d n : Nat) :
             BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆
               closure {Z | Z ∈ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ∧
                 BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z} := by
           have hVar :
               BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n =
                 BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety n (d + 1) :=
             BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_eq_sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety
               (d := d) (n := n)
           have hRankSet :
               {Z | Z ∈ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ∧
                 BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z} =
               BHW.sourceSymmetricRankExactStratum n (min (d + 1) n) := by
             ext Z
             simp [hVar, BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank,
               BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank]
           simpa [hVar, hRankSet] using
             BHW.sourceSymmetricRankLEVariety_rankExact_dense n (d + 1)

         theorem BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank
             (d n : Nat)
             {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
             (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
             U ⊆ closure
               (U ∩ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}) := by
           rcases hU_rel with ⟨Ω, hΩ_open, hU_eq⟩
           intro Z hZU
           have hZV : Z ∈ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n := by
             simpa [hU_eq] using hZU.2
           have hZΩ : Z ∈ Ω := by
             simpa [hU_eq] using hZU.1
           refine
             BHW.closure_inter_open_of_mem_open
               hΩ_open hZΩ
               (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_maxRank_dense
                 (d := d) (n := n) hZV)
               (by
                 ext W
                 simp [hU_eq, Set.inter_assoc, Set.left_comm,
                   Set.inter_left_comm])
         ```

         The last helper
         `BHW.closure_inter_open_of_mem_open` is the standard topological
         lemma: if `x ∈ Ω` and `x ∈ closure A`, with `Ω` open, then
         `x ∈ closure (Ω ∩ A)`.  Here `A` is the maximal-rank subset of the
         source Gram variety, so rewriting `U = Ω ∩ sourceComplexGramVariety`
         gives the required relative-open statement.  The proof does not use
         extended tubes, OS data, or the scalar function `phi`.
      7. `sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension` is the analytic Riemann
         extension theorem on normal analytic spaces, specialized to the
         source Gram variety.  Its inputs must include the analytic exceptional
         set and the dense maximal-rank complement, in addition to
         `hphi_cont`, `hphi_bdd`, and the max-rank local representatives.
         Its proof first rewrites `hU_rel` as a relative-open hypothesis
         `BHW.IsRelOpenIn (sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U`; then it calls the
         generic SCV theorem
         `BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension` with
         `V := sourceComplexGramVariety d n` and
         `A := {Z | HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}`.  The regular input
         on `U \ A` is exactly `hregular`, because
         `HWSourceGramMaxRank` is the complement of
         `HWSourceGramExceptionalRank`.  Local boundedness near the exceptional
         set is supplied by `hphi_bdd`, while continuity and the dense
         complement ensure that the extended section agrees with the original
         scalar value `phi` on exceptional points.  The resulting holomorphic
         section of the normal analytic space is represented locally by an
         ambient holomorphic function because
         `sourceComplexGramVariety_closedAnalytic` identifies the source Gram
         variety as a closed analytic subvariety of the finite scalar-coordinate
         space.  Both the regular-locus chart input and the conclusion must
         also carry the local-domain field `U0 ∩ V ⊆ U`; otherwise the
         asserted equality with `phi` would mention values outside the
         relatively open scalar domain.

         The generic theorem is itself implemented through the following
         standard SCV support, with no QFT or locality content:

         ```lean
         /-- Weakly holomorphic local extension on a normal analytic subvariety.
         Here `f` is represented by compatible ambient holomorphic charts on
         `U \ A`, is locally bounded on `U`, and `U \ A` is dense in `U`.
         Normality converts this weakly holomorphic section into a genuine
         structure-sheaf section near `x`, chosen inside the relative domain. -/
         theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension
             {E : Type*}
             [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℂ E]
             [CompleteSpace E] [FiniteDimensional ℂ E]
             {V U A : Set E}
             {phi : E -> ℂ}
             (hV_normal : BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety V)
             (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenIn V U)
             (hA_analytic : BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn V A)
             (hDense : U ⊆ closure (U \ A))
             (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
             (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
             (hregular :
               ∀ x, x ∈ U \ A ->
                 ∃ U0 Ψ,
                   IsOpen U0 ∧ x ∈ U0 ∧
                   DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                   U0 ∩ V ⊆ U ∧
                   Set.EqOn phi Ψ (U0 ∩ V))
             {x : E}
             (hxU : x ∈ U) :
             ∃ U0 Ψ,
               IsOpen U0 ∧ x ∈ U0 ∧
               DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
               U0 ∩ V ⊆ U ∧
               Set.EqOn phi Ψ (U0 ∩ V)

         theorem BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension ... := by
           intro x hxU
           exact
             BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension
               hV_normal hU_rel hA_analytic hDense
               hphi_cont hphi_bdd hregular hxU
         ```

         Proof transcript for
         `normalAnalyticSubvariety_weaklyHolomorphic_localExtension`:

         * Use `hU_rel` to choose an ambient open set `ΩU` with
           `U = ΩU ∩ V`; shrink every returned neighborhood inside `ΩU`, so
           `U0 ∩ V ⊆ U` is a theorem field rather than an informal
           convention.
         * On `U \ A`, the `hregular` charts define a holomorphic section of
           the structure sheaf of the analytic space `V`; overlap equality
           follows because every chart equals the same scalar function `phi`
           on its intersection with `V`.
         * `hphi_bdd` gives local boundedness of this section near points of
           `A`, and `hDense` ensures `U \ A` is the dense complement in every
           relative neighborhood used in the extension argument.
         * Apply the standard normal-space Riemann theorem:
           a locally bounded weakly holomorphic function on a normal analytic
           space extends uniquely across a nowhere-dense analytic subset.
           In Lean this theorem should live in the SCV support layer with a
           finite-dimensional-polymorphic statement not mentioning Wightman,
           OS, locality, Gram maps, or fields.  The ordinary source Gram space
           and the oriented Gram-plus-volume space must both be applications
           of this one SCV theorem, not separate black boxes.
         * Choose an ambient holomorphic representative of the resulting
           structure-sheaf germ using the closed analytic embedding of `V` in
           the finite coordinate space.  Equality with `phi` at exceptional
           points is obtained from continuity of both sides and equality on
           the dense set `U \ A`; no value is assigned by arbitrary choice.

         Lean-shaped specialization:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension ... := by
           have hV_normal :
               BHW.NormalAnalyticSubvariety
                 (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :=
             BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal d n
           have hU_rel' :
               BHW.IsRelOpenIn (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) U :=
             BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.to_isRelOpenIn hU_rel
           have hDense' :
               U ⊆ closure
                 (U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}) := by
             have hSet :
                 U ∩ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z} =
                   U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z} := by
               ext Z
               constructor
               · rintro ⟨hZU, hMax⟩
                 refine ⟨hZU, ?_⟩
                 intro hExc
                 exact
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).1 hExc) hMax
               · rintro ⟨hZU, hNotExc⟩
                 refine ⟨hZU, ?_⟩
                 by_contra hNotMax
                 exact hNotExc
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).2 hNotMax)
             simpa [hSet] using hMaxDense
           have hregular' :
               ∀ Z, Z ∈ U \ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z} ->
                 ∃ U0 Ψ,
                   IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                   DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                   U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U ∧
                   Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                     (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
             intro Z hZ
             have hZU : Z ∈ U := hZ.1
             have hMax : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z :=
               by
                 by_contra hnotMax
                 exact hZ.2
                   ((BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
                     (d := d) (n := n)).2 hnotMax)
             exact hregular Z hZU hMax
           exact
             BHW.normalAnalyticSubvariety_riemannExtension
               hV_normal hU_rel' hExceptionalAnalytic hDense'
               hphi_cont hphi_bdd hregular'
         ```
      8. `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAmbientNeighborhood` and
         `cartan_surjective_restriction_sourceGramVariety` are archived
         Siu/Cartan support for the old total ambient-function API only.  The
         first would be a four-step theorem, not a consequence of relative
         openness alone: an independent theorem would have to supply
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_domainOfHolomorphy`; the normality of
         the source Gram variety would upgrade this to
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_steinAnalyticSpace`; the source domain
         is already relatively open by
         `sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected`; Siu's theorem would
         then give a Stein ambient neighborhood `Ω` with
         `S'_n = Ω ∩ sourceComplexGramVariety`.  The second uses the coherent
         ideal sheaf generated by the symmetry equations and Gram minors,
         applies the exact sequence
         `0 -> I_V -> O_Ω -> O_{Ω∩V} -> 0`, and invokes Cartan theorem B on
         the Stein open `Ω` to prove surjectivity of
         `Γ(Ω, O_Ω) -> Γ(Ω ∩ V, O_{Ω∩V})`.
      9. `sourceVariety_holomorphicGerm_cartanAmbientExtension` is not a
         Hall-Wightman theorem and is not active.  It is only the bridge from
         the usual analytic-space output to the repo's stronger total-ambient
         `SourceVarietyHolomorphicOn` predicate.  The active route instead
         changes `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.Phi_holomorphic` to the germ
         predicate, so this bridge is unnecessary for theorem-2 production
         Lean unless the old strong API is explicitly retained and the
         independent Stein input is proved.

      ```lean
      /-- Rank of a source Gram matrix in Hall-Wightman's scalar-product
      coordinates.  This is a predicate on scalar products, not on the
      dimension of the source-vector span. -/
      def BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank
          (n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) : Nat :=
        (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => Z i j).rank

      def BHW.sourceConfigGramMatrixRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) : Nat :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      /-- Dimension-general Hall-Wightman orbit-rank condition.  For the
      original four-vector theorem, `d = 3`, so this says rank
      `min 3 n` or higher: rank three or four in the hard arity range, and
      nonsingular scalar matrices in the small-arity range. -/
      def BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        min d n <= BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z

      def BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z < min d n

      def BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      def BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      /-- Hall-Wightman's non-exceptional scalar-rank condition for the
      local holomorphic chart part of Theorem I.  This is a different
      threshold from the Lemma-2 orbit condition: it is maximal possible
      scalar rank in spacetime dimension `d + 1`. -/
      def BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        min (d + 1) n <= BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z

      def BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank
          (d n : Nat)
          (Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n Z < min (d + 1) n

      def BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ} :
          BHW.HWSourceGramLowRank d n Z ↔
            ¬ BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRank d n Z := by
        constructor
        · intro hlow horbit
          exact (not_lt_of_ge horbit) hlow
        · intro hnot
          exact Nat.lt_of_not_ge hnot

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGram_exceptionalRank_iff_not_maxRank
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ} :
          BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z ↔
            ¬ BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z := by
        constructor
        · intro hlow hmax
          exact (not_lt_of_ge hmax) hlow
        · intro hnot
          exact Nat.lt_of_not_ge hnot

      theorem BHW.hwSourceGramOrbitRankAt_of_sourceGram_eq
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z ↔
            BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n w := by
        simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt, hgram]

      /- These rank-predicate lemmas are only arithmetic and definitional
      bookkeeping.  The hypothesis `hd` on
      `hwSourceGram_lowRank_iff_not_orbitRank` is retained only to match the
      Hall-Wightman theorem surfaces where it is consumed; the proof itself is
      `Nat.lt_of_not_ge` and `not_lt_of_ge`.  Thus none of the real
      Hall-Wightman content is hidden in the rank split: the genuine work
      starts with the high-rank span isometry and the low-rank isotropic
      contraction data below. -/

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma-2 low-rank normal form before taking limits.
      After applying `Λ0` to the left configuration, the two equal-Gram
      configurations differ by vectors in a common totally isotropic residual
      subspace orthogonal to the common selected span.  In four spacetime
      dimensions this residual subspace is at most one-dimensional, giving the
      printed Hall-Wightman formula with one null vector.  For the repo's
      dimension-general surfaces it must be kept as a finite isotropic frame;
      replacing it by one `q` would be false in higher dimension. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm
          (d n : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) where
        Λ0 : ComplexLorentzGroup d
        ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        s : Nat
        aLeft : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ
        aRight : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ
        q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        left_eq :
          BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ0 z =
            fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c μ
        right_eq :
          w = fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c μ
        base_mem :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        q_pair_zero :
          ∀ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0
        qDual_pair_zero :
          ∀ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0
        q_dual :
          ∀ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
              if c = c' then (1 : ℂ) else 0
        q_orth :
          ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0
        qDual_orth :
          ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (ξ i) = 0
        contract : ℝ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d
        contract_fix_ξ :
          ∀ t i μ,
            (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val μ ν * ξ i ν) = ξ i μ
        contract_scale_q :
          ∀ t c μ,
            (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val μ ν * q c ν) =
              Real.exp (-t) * q c μ
        contract_scale_qDual :
          ∀ t c μ,
            (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1),
              (contract t).val μ ν * qDual c ν) =
              Real.exp t * qDual c μ
        contracted_left_mem :
          ∀ t,
            (fun i μ =>
              ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        contracted_right_mem :
