          ∀ t,
            (fun i μ =>
              ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        contracted_left_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ℝ =>
              fun i μ =>
                ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aLeft i c * q c μ)
            atTop (nhds ξ)
        contracted_right_tendsto :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ℝ =>
              fun i μ =>
                ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * aRight i c * q c μ)
            atTop (nhds ξ)

      /-- Linear algebra inside the low-rank branch: after choosing a nonzero
      principal scalar block, the equal Gram data determine the same
      nondegenerate selected span, scalar-coordinate coefficients against that
      span, and residual radical pairings. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame
          (d n r : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n) where
        I_injective : Function.Injective I
        principal_minor_ne :
          BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ≠ 0
        selected_gram_eq :
          ∀ a b,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z (I a) (I b) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w (I a) (I b)
        coeff : Fin n -> Fin r -> ℂ
        left_residual_orth :
          ∀ i a,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff i b • z (I b))
              (z (I a)) = 0
        right_residual_orth :
          ∀ i a,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (w i - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff i b • w (I b))
              (w (I a)) = 0
        residual_pairing_eq :
          ∀ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff i b • z (I b))
              (z j - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff j b • z (I b)) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (w i - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff i b • w (I b))
              (w j - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff j b • w (I b))
        left_residual_pair_zero :
          ∀ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff i b • z (I b))
              (z j - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff j b • z (I b)) = 0
        right_residual_pair_zero :
          ∀ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (w i - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff i b • w (I b))
              (w j - ∑ b : Fin r, coeff j b • w (I b)) = 0

      def BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) :
          Matrix (Fin r) (Fin r) ℂ :=
        Matrix.of fun a b => G (I a) (I b)

      noncomputable def BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff
          (n r : Nat)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)
          (i : Fin n) :
          Fin r -> ℂ :=
        fun b =>
          ∑ a : Fin r,
            G i (I a) *
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G)⁻¹ a b

      theorem BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_projection_eq
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G).det)
          (i : Fin n) (a : Fin r) :
          ∑ b : Fin r,
            BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G i b *
              G (I b) (I a) =
            G i (I a)

      theorem BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff_selected_eq
          {G : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I G).det)
          (a b : Fin r) :
          BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G (I a) b =
            if a = b then 1 else 0

      theorem BHW.hw_selected_residual_selected_eq_zero
          [NeZero d]
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)).det)
          (a : Fin r) :
          z (I a) -
              ∑ b : Fin r,
                BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) (I a) b • z (I b) =
            0

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_orthogonal
          [NeZero d]
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)).det) :
          ∀ i a,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i -
                ∑ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b • z (I b))
              (z (I a)) = 0

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_complement_residual_pairing_eq_schur
          [NeZero d]
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hI : Function.Injective I)
          (u v : BHW.selectedIndexComplement I) :
          let G := BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z
          let e := BHW.selectedIndexSumEquiv I hI
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (z (e.symm (Sum.inr u)) -
              ∑ b : Fin r,
                BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G
                  (e.symm (Sum.inr u)) b • z (I b))
            (z (e.symm (Sum.inr v)) -
              ∑ b : Fin r,
                BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I G
                  (e.symm (Sum.inr v)) b • z (I b)) =
          BHW.reindexedRectSchurComplement
            (Matrix.of fun i j : Fin n => G i j) e e u v

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residual_pairing_eq_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hunit :
            IsUnit
              (BHW.sourcePrincipalGramMatrix n r I
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)).det)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          ∀ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i -
                ∑ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b • z (I b))
              (z j -
                ∑ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) j b • z (I b)) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (w i -
                ∑ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b • w (I b))
              (w j -
                ∑ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) j b • w (I b))

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selected_residual_pairing_zero
          [NeZero d]
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (hrank :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = r)
          (hminor :
            BHW.sourceMatrixMinor n r I I
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ≠ 0) :
          ∀ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (z i -
                ∑ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) i b • z (I b))
              (z j -
                ∑ b : Fin r,
                  BHW.hw_selectedSpanCoeff n r I
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) j b • z (I b)) = 0

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanFrame_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          ∃ r : Nat, ∃ I : Fin r -> Fin n,
            r = BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) ∧
            BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I

      def BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace
          (d : Nat)
          (R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) : Prop :=
        ∀ x y : R,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
            (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0

      /-- Data obtained after the selected nondegenerate spans have first been
      matched by a Witt-extension Lorentz transformation.  The common selected
      span is the `w` selected span; only after applying `Λsel` to the left
      endpoint do the two projected residual families live in the same
      orthogonal complement. -/
      structure BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment
          (d n r : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (I : Fin r -> Fin n)
          (S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I) where
        M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
        Λsel : ComplexLorentzGroup d
        ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        leftResidual : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        rightResidual : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        M_eq :
          M = Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range (fun a : Fin r => w (I a)))
        M_nondeg : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M
        selected_mem : ∀ a, w (I a) ∈ M
        Λsel_selected :
          ∀ a,
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λsel (z (I a)) = w (I a)
        ξ_eq :
          ∀ i, ξ i = ∑ b : Fin r, S.coeff i b • w (I b)
        ξ_mem : ∀ i, ξ i ∈ M
        leftResidual_eq :
          ∀ i,
            leftResidual i =
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λsel (z i) - ξ i
        rightResidual_eq :
          ∀ i, rightResidual i = w i - ξ i
        left_decomp :
          ∀ i,
            BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λsel (z i) =
              ξ i + leftResidual i
        right_decomp : ∀ i, w i = ξ i + rightResidual i
        left_residual_orth_M :
          ∀ i (m : M),
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (leftResidual i)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0
        right_residual_orth_M :
          ∀ i (m : M),
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (rightResidual i)
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0
        left_residual_pair_zero :
          ∀ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (leftResidual i) (leftResidual j) = 0
        right_residual_pair_zero :
          ∀ i j,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (rightResidual i) (rightResidual j) = 0

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_selectedSpanAlignment_of_selectedSpanFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          (S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I) :
          Nonempty
            (BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S)

      /-- Once the selected spans are aligned, the projected residual vectors
      on both sides lie in the orthogonal complement of the common selected
      span and span totally isotropic subspaces. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_residualSubspaces_after_selectedAlignment
          [NeZero d]
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {I : Fin r -> Fin n}
          {S : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanFrame d n r z w I}
          (A : BHW.HWLowRankSelectedSpanAlignment d n r z w I S) :
          ∃ (Rleft Rright : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)),
            Rleft =
              Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range A.leftResidual) ∧
            Rright =
              Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range A.rightResidual) ∧
            (∀ x : Rleft, ∀ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0) ∧
            (∀ x : Rright, ∀ m : A.M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0) ∧
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rleft ∧
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rright

      /-- Finite-dimensional Witt geometry in the orthogonal complement of the
      selected span: two finite totally isotropic residual families can be put
      into one common totally isotropic frame by a complex Lorentz
      transformation fixing the selected nondegenerate span.  This is the
      dimension-general replacement for Hall-Wightman's one-null-vector
      alignment in four spacetime dimensions. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_alignResidualSubspaces_to_commonIsotropicFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M Rz Rw : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hRz_orth :
            ∀ x : Rz, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hRw_orth :
            ∀ x : Rw, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hRz_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rz)
          (hRw_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d Rw) :
          ∃ (Λfix : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
            (s : Nat)
            (q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            (∀ m : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λfix
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
              (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) ∧
            LinearIndependent ℂ q ∧
            (∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0) ∧
            (∀ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0) ∧
            (∀ x : Rz,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λfix
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ∈
              Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range q)) ∧
            (∀ x : Rw,
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ∈
              Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range q))

      The proof of this alignment theorem is a finite-dimensional Witt
      construction in the orthogonal complement of `M`; it must not be left
      as a black-box "put both residual spaces in the same null frame".  The
      required support packet is:

      ```lean
      def BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) :
          Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
        { carrier := {v | ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0}
          zero_mem' := by simp
          add_mem' := by
            intro x y hx hy m
            simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_left, hx m, hy m]
          smul_mem' := by
            intro c x hx m
            simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left, hx m] }

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)

      theorem BHW.subspace_le_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
          {M R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hR_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0) :
          R ≤ BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M

      structure BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn
          (d : Nat)
          (N : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) where
        s : Nat
        q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        q_mem : ∀ c, q c ∈ N
        q_independent : LinearIndependent ℂ q
        q_pair_zero :
          ∀ c c',
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0
        maximal :
          ∀ R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            R ≤ N ->
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R ->
            Module.finrank ℂ R ≤ s

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_exists
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hN :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N) :
          Nonempty
            (BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hN :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N)
          (hR_le : R ≤ N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          ∃ F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N,
            R ≤ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range F.q)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (F : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiMaximalIsotropicFrameIn d N)
          (hR_le : R ≤ N)
          (hR_iso : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiTotallyIsotropicSubspace d R) :
          ∃ E : R ->ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            Function.Injective E ∧
            (∀ x : R,
              E x ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range F.q)) ∧
            (∀ x y : R,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x)
                (E y)
              =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))

      theorem BHW.span_frame_orthogonal_to_subspace
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          {s : Nat}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          {v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hv : v ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range q)) :
          ∀ m : M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              v (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_disjoint_of_nondegenerate_orthogonal
          [NeZero d]
          {M R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0) :
          Disjoint M R

      noncomputable def BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
          [NeZero d]
          (M R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (E : R ->ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
          (hE_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hE_preserves :
            ∀ x y : R,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (E x) (E y) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) :
          (M ⊔ R) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (M ⊔ LinearMap.range E)

      theorem BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_preserves
          [NeZero d]
          {M R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          {E : R ->ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
          (hE_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hE_preserves :
            ∀ x y : R,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (E x) (E y) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) :
          let T :=
            BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
              (d := d) M R E hM hR_orth hE_inj hE_orth hE_preserves
          ∀ x y : M ⊔ R,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              ((T x : M ⊔ LinearMap.range E) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              ((T y : M ⊔ LinearMap.range E) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)

      theorem BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_maps_right
          [NeZero d]
          {M R : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          {E : R ->ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hR_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hE_inj : Function.Injective E)
          (hE_orth :
            ∀ x : R, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hE_preserves :
            ∀ x y : R,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (E x) (E y) =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (x : R) :
          ((BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
              (d := d) M R E hM hR_orth hE_inj hE_orth hE_preserves)
            ⟨(x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ), Submodule.mem_sup_right x.2⟩ :
              M ⊔ LinearMap.range E) =
            ⟨E x, Submodule.mem_sup_right ⟨x, rfl⟩⟩
      ```

      Proof transcript for this packet: `complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule`
      is a submodule because the bilinear form is linear in the first
      variable.  If `M` is nondegenerate in the ambient nondegenerate complex
      Minkowski space, the standard finite-dimensional decomposition
      `ambient = M ⊔ Mᗮ` gives nondegeneracy of `Mᗮ`; in Lean this is the
      same Gram-matrix block diagonal argument used in
      `complexMinkowski_wittExtension`.  A maximal isotropic frame in a
      nondegenerate finite-dimensional complex bilinear space is obtained by
      induction on finrank: if there is no nonzero isotropic vector, take
      `s = 0`; otherwise split off a hyperbolic pair `(q, qDual)` and recurse
      on the orthogonal complement of their span.  Maximality records the
      Witt-index dimension bound for every totally isotropic subspace.  The
      strengthened constructor
      `complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending` starts with a
      basis of the given totally isotropic subspace `R`, splits off dual
      hyperbolic partners for that basis, and then completes the basis to a
      maximal isotropic frame inside the remaining orthogonal complement.  Its
      output is essential here: the right residual subspace `Rw` must already
      lie in the final frame span before the left residual subspace is moved.

      For `complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame`, choose a
      basis of `R`.  The maximality field gives
      `finrank R <= F.s`, so inject that basis index into `Fin F.s` and send
      the basis vectors of `R` to the corresponding `F.q` vectors.  Since the
      bilinear form vanishes identically on `R` and on the span of the frame,
      the map preserves pairings automatically; the selected `F.q` subfamily
      is independent, so the map is injective.  The theorem deliberately
      returns an injective map into the frame span, not an equivalence onto the
      full frame span: `R` may have smaller dimension than the chosen maximal
      frame.

      Lean-shaped proof of the residual alignment theorem after the support
      packet exists:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_alignResidualSubspaces_to_commonIsotropicFrame
          ... := by
        let N := BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M
        have hN_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
            (d := d) hd hM
        have hRz_le : Rz ≤ N :=
          BHW.subspace_le_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
            (d := d) hRz_orth
        have hRw_le : Rw ≤ N :=
          BHW.subspace_le_complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule
            (d := d) hRw_orth
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_maximalIsotropicFrameIn_extending
            (d := d) hd hN_nondeg hRw_le hRw_iso with
          ⟨F, hRw_Q⟩
        rcases
          BHW.complexMinkowski_totallyIsotropic_embedding_into_frame
            (d := d) hd F hRz_le hRz_iso with
          ⟨Eleft, hEleft_inj, hEleft_Q, hEleft_preserves⟩
        let Qspan : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
          Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range F.q)
        let Qleft : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
          LinearMap.range Eleft
        have hQleft_le_Qspan : Qleft ≤ Qspan := by
          rintro v ⟨x, rfl⟩
          exact hEleft_Q x
        have hEleft_orth_M :
            ∀ x : Rz, ∀ m : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (Eleft x) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0 := by
          intro x m
          exact
            BHW.span_frame_orthogonal_to_subspace
              (d := d)
              (M := M)
              (q := F.q)
              (by
                intro c m
                exact F.q_mem c m)
              (hEleft_Q x) m
        let Domain : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) := M ⊔ Rz
        let Target : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) := M ⊔ Qleft
        let T : Domain ≃ₗ[ℂ] Target :=
          BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding
            (d := d) M Rz Eleft hM hRz_orth
            hEleft_inj hEleft_orth_M hEleft_preserves
        have hT_preserves :
            ∀ x y : Domain,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                ((T x : Target) : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                ((T y : Target) : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
          BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_preserves
            (d := d) hM hRz_orth hEleft_inj
            hEleft_orth_M hEleft_preserves
        rcases BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry
            (d := d) hd T hT_preserves with
          ⟨Λfix, hΛfix⟩
        exact ⟨Λfix, F.s, F.q,
          by
            intro m
            have hmDomain : (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ∈ Domain :=
              Submodule.mem_sup_left m.2
            exact hΛfix ⟨m, hmDomain⟩ |> congrArg Subtype.val,
          F.q_independent,
          F.q_pair_zero,
          by
            intro c m
            have hqN : F.q c ∈ N := F.q_mem c
            exact hqN m,
          by
            intro x
            have hxDomain : (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ∈ Domain :=
              Submodule.mem_sup_right x.2
            have hTx_eq :
                (T ⟨x, hxDomain⟩ : Target) =
                  ⟨Eleft x, Submodule.mem_sup_right ⟨x, rfl⟩⟩ :=
              BHW.directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding_maps_right
                (d := d) hM hRz_orth hEleft_inj
                hEleft_orth_M hEleft_preserves x
            have hTx : ((T ⟨x, hxDomain⟩ : Target) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ∈ Qleft := by
              rw [congrArg Subtype.val hTx_eq]
              exact ⟨x, rfl⟩
            have hTxQ : ((T ⟨x, hxDomain⟩ : Target) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ∈ Qspan :=
              hQleft_le_Qspan hTx
            simpa [Qspan] using
              (show BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λfix
                  (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
                ((T ⟨x, hxDomain⟩ : Target) :
                  Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) from hΛfix ⟨x, hxDomain⟩) ▸ hTxQ,
          by
            intro x
            exact hRw_Q x.2⟩
      ```

      The pseudocode uses the proof-local helper
      `directSum_identity_sum_isotropicEmbedding` and its `preserves` /
      `maps_right` projections.  These are not new route assumptions: they are
      the finite linear map on `M ⊔ Rz` which is the identity on `M` and
      `Eleft` on `Rz`, with codomain `M ⊔ LinearMap.range Eleft`, not the
      whole maximal frame span.  The direct-sum decomposition is justified by
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_disjoint_of_nondegenerate_orthogonal`: an element
      in `M ∩ Rz` lies in `M` and is orthogonal to all of `M`, hence is zero
      by nondegeneracy of `M`; the same proof applies to
      `M ∩ LinearMap.range Eleft` after
      `BHW.span_frame_orthogonal_to_subspace` transports frame-span
      orthogonality to `M`.  The proof that the map preserves the bilinear
      form is exactly the block calculation using nondegeneracy of `M`,
      orthogonality of `Rz` and the frame span to `M`, and total isotropy of
      both residual blocks.  If the Lean implementation avoids naming this
      helper, it must still perform the same disjointness and block-pairing
      calculation before applying Witt extension.

      theorem BHW.exists_coefficients_of_mem_span_finite_frame
          {d s : Nat}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hv : v ∈ Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range q)) :
          ∃ a : Fin s -> ℂ, v = ∑ c : Fin s, a c • q c

      /-- After the selected nondegenerate spans have been matched by Witt
      extension, the low-rank discrepancy is contained in a finite totally
      isotropic residual frame in the orthogonal complement.  For `d = 3` this
      frame has length at most one, recovering Hall-Wightman's displayed
      vector `w`; for general `d` the frame length may be larger. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_alignedResidualFrame_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          ∃ (M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
            (Λ0 : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
            (ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
            (s : Nat)
            (aLeft aRight : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ)
            (q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M ∧
              (∀ i, ξ i ∈ M) ∧
              (∀ c (m : M),
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
                  0) ∧
              LinearIndependent ℂ q ∧
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ0 z =
                (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, aLeft i c * q c μ) ∧
              w = (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, aRight i c * q c μ) ∧
              ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ∧
              (∀ c c',
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0) ∧
              (∀ c i,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0)

      /-- Witt-complement input used by the low-rank contraction.  A finite
      totally isotropic residual frame in the orthogonal complement of the
      common base admits a dual isotropic frame, with the stated pairings,
      after possibly discarding zero residual directions in the normal-form
      construction.  This is the dimension-general replacement for the
      one-null-vector boost in the printed four-dimensional proof. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_residualFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n s : Nat}
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hM_nondeg :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M)
          (hξ_mem : ∀ i, ξ i ∈ M)
          (hq_orth_M :
            ∀ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (q c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_independent :
            LinearIndependent ℂ q) :
          ∃ qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ,
            (∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0) ∧
            (∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : ℂ) else 0) ∧
            (∀ c (m : M),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (qDual c) (m : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = 0) ∧
            (∀ c i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (ξ i) = 0)

      The dual-frame theorem must be proved through the same finite
      nondegenerate-space machinery as the Witt packet, not by choosing
      arbitrary ambient dual vectors.  The implementation-level support
      theorem is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicFrame_dualFrameIn
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {N : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hN :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N)
          {s : Nat}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hq_mem : ∀ c, q c ∈ N)
          (hq_independent : LinearIndependent ℂ q)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0) :
          ∃ qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ,
            (∀ c, qDual c ∈ N) ∧
            (∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0) ∧
            (∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
      ```

      Proof transcript for `complexMinkowski_isotropicFrame_dualFrameIn`:
      work with the restricted bilinear form on `N`.  Induct on `s`.
      For `s = 0`, return the empty frame.  For `s + 1`, put
      `q0 := q 0` and `qtail a := q (Fin.succ a)`.  Linear independence of
      `q` gives `q0 ≠ 0` and
      `IsCompl (ℂ ∙ q0) (span ℂ (Set.range qtail))` inside
      `Q := span ℂ (Set.range q)`.  Since the whole `q`-span is totally
      isotropic, `q0` lies in the restricted radical of `Q`.  Apply the
      radical-partner theorem inside the nondegenerate ambient `N` with this
      complement to obtain `p0 : N` such that
      `B q0 p0 = 1`, `B qtail a p0 = 0`, and `B p0 p0 = 0`.
      Let `H := span ℂ {q0, p0}`.  By the hyperbolic-plane split,
      `H` is nondegenerate and `Hᗮ` is nondegenerate in `N`.  The tail
      vectors lie in `Hᗮ` because they pair to zero with both `q0` and
      `p0`.  Apply the induction hypothesis to `qtail` as a frame in
      `Hᗮ`; the returned tail duals also lie in `Hᗮ`, hence pair to zero
      with `q0` and `p0`.  Assemble `qDual 0 := p0` and
      `qDual (Fin.succ a) := qtailDual a`.  The Kronecker equations are
      exactly the `p0` construction for the first row/column and the
      induction equations for the tail block.

      To apply this theorem to
      `complexMinkowski_isotropicDualFrame_of_residualFrame`, take
      `N := BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M`.  The previous
      support theorem gives nondegeneracy of `N`, `hq_orth_M` gives
      `hq_mem : ∀ c, q c ∈ N`, and the returned `qDual_mem` gives
      `qDual_orth` because membership in `N` is precisely orthogonality to
      `M`; since every `ξ i ∈ M`, this yields the displayed
      `qDual_orth` field.  Thus no independent choice of dual vectors outside
      the selected-span orthogonal complement is permitted.

      /-- The null boost fixing the selected span and contracting every vector
      in the totally isotropic residual frame.  The proof chooses a dual
      isotropic frame `qDual`, completes
      `selectedSpan ⊕ span q ⊕ span qDual` to an orthogonal Witt basis, and
      scales `q` by `exp (-t)` and `qDual` by `exp t`.  The dual scaling is
      part of the output so the proof that the family lies in
      `ComplexLorentzGroup d` is visible, not hidden inside a bare existence
      of maps.  In production declaration order, first prove the two support
      theorems displayed immediately below this public surface:
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` and
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContraction_partialIsometry`. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContractionFamily
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n s : Nat}
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hqDual_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
          (hq_dual :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0)
          (hqDual_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ∃ contract : ℝ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (∀ t i μ,
              (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1),
                (contract t).val μ ν * ξ i ν) = ξ i μ) ∧
            (∀ t c μ,
              (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1),
                (contract t).val μ ν * q c ν) =
                  Real.exp (-t) * q c μ) ∧
            (∀ t c μ,
              (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1),
                (contract t).val μ ν * qDual c ν) =
                  Real.exp t * qDual c μ)

      /-- Hall-Wightman's second and third remarks after Lemma 2,
      finite-frame version: if one coefficient choice along a finite totally
      isotropic frame orthogonal to the base lies in the ordinary extended
      tube, then the zero coefficient choice does too.  This does not assert
      any `ForwardTube` conclusion for the original base. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_base_mem_extendedTube_of_endpoint
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n

      /-- Hall-Wightman's first consequence of the second and third remarks
      after Lemma 2, finite-frame version.  Once one coefficient tuple along a
      totally isotropic frame lies in the ordinary extended tube, every
      coefficient tuple along the same frame lies in the ordinary extended
      tube, including the zero tuple.  The dual frame used later for the
      null-boost contraction is not part of this membership theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0)
          :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ∧
          ∀ b : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ,
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, b i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n

      /-- General finite-dimensional Witt extension for the complex Minkowski
      form.  Unlike the high-rank shortcut statement, the subspace here need
      not be nondegenerate: an isometry between two finite-dimensional
      subspaces of the ambient nondegenerate complex Minkowski space extends to
      a global complex Lorentz transformation.  This is the standard Witt
      lemma used by the low-rank contraction frame. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {M N : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (T : M ≃ₗ[ℂ] N)
          (hT :
            ∀ x y : M,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                ((T x : N) : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                ((T y : N) : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) :
          ∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ∀ x : M,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λ
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
              ((T x : N) : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)

      /-- The partial finite-frame isometry underlying the low-rank null
      boost.  Its domain is the span of the common base vectors, the residual
      isotropic frame, and the dual residual frame.  It fixes every `ξ i`,
      scales `q c` by `exp (-t)`, scales `qDual c` by `exp t`, and preserves
      the complex Minkowski form on that span. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContraction_partialIsometry
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {n s : Nat}
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hqDual_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
          (hq_dual :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0)
          (hqDual_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (ξ i) = 0)
          (t : ℝ) :
          ∃ K : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
          ∃ T : K ≃ₗ[ℂ] K,
          ∃ hξ : ∀ i, ξ i ∈ K,
          ∃ hq : ∀ c, q c ∈ K,
          ∃ hqDual : ∀ c, qDual c ∈ K,
            (∀ i,
              ((T ⟨ξ i, hξ i⟩ : K) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) = ξ i) ∧
            (∀ c,
              ((T ⟨q c, hq c⟩ : K) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
                fun μ => Real.exp (-t) * q c μ) ∧
            (∀ c,
              ((T ⟨qDual c, hqDual c⟩ : K) :
                Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
                fun μ => Real.exp t * qDual c μ) ∧
            (∀ x y : K,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                ((T x : K) : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                ((T y : K) : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))

      Proof transcript for the contraction family.  For each fixed `t`, put
      `X := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range ξ)`, `Q := Submodule.span ℂ
      (Set.range q)`, `Qd := Submodule.span ℂ (Set.range qDual)`, and
      `K := X ⊔ Q ⊔ Qd`.  The duality equations imply:

      1. `q` is linearly independent, because pairing
         `∑ c, a c • q c = 0` with `qDual c0` gives `a c0 = 0`.
      2. `qDual` is linearly independent by the same argument, using symmetry
         of `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner`.
      3. `Q ∩ X = ⊥`: an element of `Q ∩ X` pairs trivially with every
         `qDual c` by `hqDual_orth`, while the duality equation reads off its
         `q`-coordinates.
      4. `Qd ∩ (X ⊔ Q) = ⊥`: pair with every `q c` and use `hq_orth`,
         `hq_pair_zero`, and `hq_dual`.

      These four facts give a direct-sum coordinate decomposition of `K`.
      Define `T_t` on `K` by
      `x + qpart + qdpart ↦ x + Real.exp (-t) • qpart +
      Real.exp t • qdpart`.  The displayed pairings show that `T_t`
      preserves the complex Minkowski form on all pairs of generators:
      the `X`-`X` block is fixed, the `X`-`Q` and `X`-`Qd` blocks are zero,
      the `Q`-`Q` and `Qd`-`Qd` blocks are zero, and the `Q`-`Qd` block is
      multiplied by `Real.exp (-t) * Real.exp t = 1`.  The inverse is the
      same construction with `-t`, so this is a linear equivalence
      `K ≃ₗ[ℂ] K`.  This proves
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContraction_partialIsometry`.

      Then apply
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_subspaceIsometry` to this partial
      isometry for each `t`, and choose the returned Lorentz transformation
      as `contract t`.  The three output formulas of
      `BHW.complexMinkowski_isotropicContractionFamily` are obtained by
      applying the extension equality to the generators `ξ i`, `q c`, and
      `qDual c`.  No continuity of the chosen map `t ↦ contract t` is used
      later; all limits in the low-rank branch are proved from the explicit
      contracted configurations and the checked Mathlib scalar limit
      `Real.tendsto_exp_neg_atTop_nhds_zero`, followed by the continuous
      coercion `ℝ -> ℂ` and finite-sum continuity.

      Proof transcript for the tube-stability theorems.  The base-membership
      theorem is Hall-Wightman's second remark after Lemma 2.  Its conclusion
      is deliberately `ExtendedTube`, not `ForwardTube`: in complex null-frame
      coordinates, deleting the null residual can move the imaginary part of
      a forward-tube representative to the forward-cone boundary.  The proof
      is still finite-dimensional.  First transport the endpoint to a
      forward-tube representative, then handle one null residual direction at
      a time by Hall-Wightman's normal-form and scaling remarks.  The precise
      Hall-Wightman content is subtler than "delete the residual and stay in
      the forward tube": that
      statement is false.  If `q = e1 + i e2`, `η = T e0` is future timelike,
      and `ξ = iη + iR q` with `R > T`, then `ξ - iR q` is in the one-point
      forward tube and `q` is null and orthogonal to `ξ`, but `Im ξ =
      T e0 + R e1` is not timelike.  Hall-Wightman's second remark instead
      rewrites a forward-tube point with a null residual as
      `η + β q`, where `η` is another forward-tube point and the null vector
      has the standard real spacelike normal form relative to `η`.  The third
      remark then shows that all coefficient choices along this null vector
      are in the ordinary extended tube.  This is exactly the invariant
      conclusion needed downstream.

      The base-membership theorem should therefore be implemented through
      Hall-Wightman's second and third remarks in one null direction, finite
      coefficient replacement, and then Lorentz transport.  The dual frame is
      not part of this coefficient-freedom theorem and enters only later, in
      the simultaneous contraction family used for the singular analytic
      limit.  The nontrivial Hall-Wightman normal-form lemmas below assume
      `[NeZero n]`: for `n = 0` the finite-frame coefficient theorem is
      handled separately by the unique empty configuration, and no claim is
      made that an arbitrary complex null vector has spacelike normal form
      from vacuous tube data.

      ```lean
      /-- Normal form used in Hall-Wightman's second and third remarks after
      Lemma 2.  Either `q = 0`, or `q = α (u + i v)` with `u,v` real
      spacelike orthonormal vectors and every source vector `η i` orthogonal
      to both `u` and `v`.  With the repo's mostly-positive convention the
      spacelike normalizations are `u.u = 1` and `v.v = 1`; Hall-Wightman's
      printed `-1` signs come from the opposite convention. -/
      def BHW.HWNullResidualNormalForm
          (d n : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        q = 0 ∨
        ∃ (α : ℂ) (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ),
          α ≠ 0 ∧
          q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)) ∧
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1 ∧
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1 ∧
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0 ∧
          (∀ i,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0) ∧
          (∀ i,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0)

      def BHW.realNullCollinear
          (d : Nat) [NeZero d]
          (x y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ) : Prop :=
        ∃ (ℓ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ) (a b : ℝ),
          ℓ ≠ 0 ∧
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ ℓ = 0 ∧
          x = fun μ => a * ℓ μ ∧
          y = fun μ => b * ℓ μ

      theorem BHW.complexNull_iff_real_imag_equal_orthogonal
          [NeZero d]
          (q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (hq : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0) :
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun μ => (q μ).re) (fun μ => (q μ).re) =
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun μ => (q μ).im) (fun μ => (q μ).im) ∧
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun μ => (q μ).re) (fun μ => (q μ).im) = 0

      theorem BHW.real_imag_nonzero_of_complex_ne_zero
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hq : q ≠ 0) :
          (fun μ => (q μ).re) ≠ 0 ∨ (fun μ => (q μ).im) ≠ 0

      theorem BHW.realNullCollinear_commonVector
          [NeZero d]
          {x y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hcoll : BHW.realNullCollinear d x y)
          (hxy_ne : x ≠ 0 ∨ y ≠ 0) :
          ∃ ℓ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ,
            ℓ ≠ 0 ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ ℓ = 0 ∧
            (∃ a : ℝ, x = fun μ => a * ℓ μ) ∧
            (∃ b : ℝ, y = fun μ => b * ℓ μ)

      theorem BHW.realNullCollinear_scalar_ne_zero_of_nonzero_direction
          {qre qim ℓ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          {a b : ℝ}
          (hqre : qre = fun μ => a * ℓ μ)
          (hqim : qim = fun μ => b * ℓ μ)
          (hq_ne : qre ≠ 0 ∨ qim ≠ 0) :
          (a : ℂ) + Complex.I * (b : ℂ) ≠ 0 := by
        intro h
        have ha : a = 0 := by
          exact_mod_cast congrArg Complex.re h
        have hb : b = 0 := by
          have := congrArg Complex.im h
          simpa [ha] using this
        rcases hq_ne with hqre_ne | hqim_ne
        · exact hqre_ne (by ext μ; simp [hqre, ha])
        · exact hqim_ne (by ext μ; simp [hqim, hb])

      theorem BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero
          {ι : Type} [Fintype ι]
          (f : ι -> ℝ)
          (h : ∑ i, f i ^ 2 = 0) :
          ∀ i, f i = 0 := by
        intro i
        exact
          sq_eq_zero_iff.mp
            (Finset.sum_eq_zero_iff_of_nonneg
              (fun j _ => sq_nonneg (f j))).mp h i (Finset.mem_univ i)

      theorem BHW.realNull_abs_time_eq_spatial_norm
          [NeZero d]
          {ℓ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hℓ_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ ℓ = 0) :
          |ℓ 0| = Real.sqrt (∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ ^ 2) := by
        have hsq :
            ℓ 0 ^ 2 = ∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ ^ 2 := by
          simpa [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, BHW.minkowski_sum_decomp]
            using hℓ_null
        rw [← Real.sqrt_sq_eq_abs, hsq]
        exact (Real.sqrt_sq_eq_abs _).symm

      theorem BHW.realNullCollinear_orthogonal_forwardTube_differences
          [NeZero d]
          {ζ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {qre qim ℓ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (qre μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (qim μ : ℂ))
                (ζ i) = 0)
          (hq_ne : qre ≠ 0 ∨ qim ≠ 0)
          (hqre : ∃ a : ℝ, qre = fun μ => a * ℓ μ)
          (hqim : ∃ b : ℝ, qim = fun μ => b * ℓ μ) :
          ∀ k : Fin n,
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ
              (fun μ =>
                ((ζ k μ) -
                  (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                   else ζ ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ μ)).im) = 0 := by
        rcases hqre with ⟨a, hqre⟩
        rcases hqim with ⟨b, hqim⟩
        have hscalar_ne : (a : ℂ) + Complex.I * (b : ℂ) ≠ 0 :=
          BHW.realNullCollinear_scalar_ne_zero_of_nonzero_direction
            (qre := qre) (qim := qim) (ℓ := ℓ) hqre hqim hq_ne
        have hℓ_orth_point :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (ℓ μ : ℂ)) (ζ i) = 0 := by
          intro i
          have hqi := hq_orth i
          have hfactor :
              (fun μ => (qre μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (qim μ : ℂ)) =
              fun μ => ((a : ℂ) + Complex.I * (b : ℂ)) * (ℓ μ : ℂ) := by
            ext μ
            simp [hqre, hqim, mul_add, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
          rw [hfactor,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left] at hqi
          exact mul_eq_zero.mp hqi |>.resolve_left hscalar_ne
        intro k
        by_cases hk : k.val = 0
        · simpa [hk] using
            congrArg Complex.im (hℓ_orth_point k)
        · have hprev :=
            hℓ_orth_point ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩
          have hcurr := hℓ_orth_point k
          have hdiff :
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (ℓ μ : ℂ))
                (fun μ => ζ k μ - ζ ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ μ) = 0 := by
            simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right, hcurr, hprev]
          simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner,
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, hk] using
            congrArg Complex.im hdiff

      theorem BHW.realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone
          [NeZero d]
          {ℓ η : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hη : BHW.InOpenForwardCone d η)
          (hℓ_ne : ℓ ≠ 0)
          (hℓ_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ ℓ = 0) :
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ η ≠ 0 := by
        have hη_gap := BHW.inOpenForwardCone_iff_timePos_gapPos.mp hη
        have hη_spatial_lt :
            Real.sqrt (∑ i : Fin d, η i.succ ^ 2) < η 0 :=
          BHW.spatial_norm_lt_time hη
        have hℓ_time_abs :
            |ℓ 0| = Real.sqrt (∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ ^ 2) :=
          BHW.realNull_abs_time_eq_spatial_norm (d := d) hℓ_null
        have hℓ_time_ne : ℓ 0 ≠ 0 := by
          intro h0
          have hsp0 : ∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ ^ 2 = 0 := by
            simpa [h0, BHW.minkowski_sum_decomp] using hℓ_null
          have hsp_each : ∀ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ = 0 :=
            BHW.sum_sq_eq_zero.mp hsp0
          apply hℓ_ne
          ext μ
          cases μ using Fin.cases with
          | zero => exact h0
          | succ i => exact hsp_each i
        intro horth
        have hdot_eq :
            (∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ * η i.succ) = ℓ 0 * η 0 := by
          simpa [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner,
            BHW.minkowski_sum_decomp] using horth
        have hcs :
            |∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ * η i.succ| <=
              Real.sqrt (∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ ^ 2) *
              Real.sqrt (∑ i : Fin d, η i.succ ^ 2) :=
          Real.abs_sum_mul_le_sqrt_mul_sqrt _ _ _
        have habs_eq :
            |∑ i : Fin d, ℓ i.succ * η i.succ| = |ℓ 0| * η 0 := by
          rw [hdot_eq, abs_mul, abs_of_pos hη.1]
        have hle :
            |ℓ 0| * η 0 <=
              |ℓ 0| *
              Real.sqrt (∑ i : Fin d, η i.succ ^ 2) := by
          simpa [habs_eq, hℓ_time_abs] using hcs
        exact
          not_le_of_gt hη_spatial_lt
            (le_of_mul_le_mul_left hle (abs_pos.mpr hℓ_time_ne))

      theorem BHW.nonzero_realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone_differences
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          {ζ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {ℓ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hζ : ζ ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hℓ_ne : ℓ ≠ 0)
          (hℓ_null : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ ℓ = 0)
          (hℓ_orth :
            ∀ k : Fin n,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ
                (fun μ =>
                  ((ζ k μ) -
                    (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                     else ζ ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ μ)).im) = 0) :
          False := by
        let k : Fin n := 0
        have hkFT : BHW.InOpenForwardCone d
            (fun μ =>
              ((ζ k μ) -
                (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                 else ζ ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ μ)).im) :=
          hζ k
        exact
          BHW.realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone
            (d := d) hkFT hℓ_ne hℓ_null (hℓ_orth k)

      theorem BHW.realImag_null_not_collinear_to_spacelike_orthonormal
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {qre qim : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (h_ne : qre ≠ 0 ∨ qim ≠ 0)
          (hnull_real :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qre =
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qim qim ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qim = 0)
          (hnot_collinear : ¬ BHW.realNullCollinear d qre qim) :
          ∃ (α : ℂ) (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ),
            α ≠ 0 ∧
            (fun μ => (qre μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (qim μ : ℂ)) =
              fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)) ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1 ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1 ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0

      theorem BHW.forwardCone_timeOrientation_constant_on_timelike_segment
          [NeZero d]
          {γ : ℝ -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hγ_cont : Continuous γ)
          (hγ_timelike :
            ∀ s ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d (γ s) (γ s) < 0)
          (hγ_one_time : (γ 1) 0 > 0) :
          (γ 0) 0 > 0 := by
        by_contra hnot
        have h0_nonpos : (γ 0) 0 <= 0 := le_of_not_gt hnot
        have htime_path :
            ContinuousOn (fun s : ℝ => (γ s) 0) (Set.Icc 0 1) :=
          (continuous_apply 0).comp_continuousOn hγ_cont.continuousOn
        have hzero :
            ∃ s ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1, (γ s) 0 = 0 :=
          intermediate_value_Icc h0_nonpos (le_of_lt hγ_one_time)
            htime_path
        rcases hzero with ⟨s, hs, htime0⟩
        have hquad_nonneg :
            0 <= MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d (γ s) (γ s) := by
          -- With time component zero, the Minkowski square is a sum of
          -- spatial squares.
          simp [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, htime0,
            Fin.sum_univ_succ]
        exact not_lt_of_ge hquad_nonneg (hγ_timelike s hs)

      theorem BHW.hw_nullPlane_orthogonality_relation
          [NeZero d]
          {q ξ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {α : ℂ} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hq :
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (horth : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q ξ = 0) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) ξ =
            Complex.I *
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) ξ

      theorem BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector
          [NeZero d]
          {η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (huη :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0)
          (k : Fin n) :
          MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
            (fun μ =>
              (η k μ -
                (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                 else η ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ μ)).im) u = 0

      theorem BHW.imag_nullNormalForm_coefficients
          [NeZero d]
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {α γ : ℂ}
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hq :
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ))) :
          ∃ p r : ℝ,
            (fun μ => (γ * q μ).im) =
              fun μ => p * u μ + r * v μ

      /-- The real two-plane calculation in Hall-Wightman's equation (41).
      With the repo's mostly-positive convention, if `Y + p u + r v` is in
      the open forward cone, while `u,v` are spacelike orthonormal and
      orthogonal to `Y`, then `Y` is also in the open forward cone.  The path
      `s ↦ Y + s p u + s r v`, `0 <= s <= 1`, stays timelike because its
      Minkowski square is `Y.Y + s^2 (p^2 + r^2)`, which is bounded above by
      the negative value at `s = 1`; the time orientation cannot change along
      a continuous timelike path. -/
      theorem BHW.forwardCone_remove_spacelikeOrthogonal_twoPlane
          [NeZero d]
          {Y u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          {p r : ℝ}
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (hYu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y u = 0)
          (hYv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y v = 0)
          (hYpr :
            BHW.InOpenForwardCone d
              (fun μ => Y μ + p * u μ + r * v μ)) :
          BHW.InOpenForwardCone d Y := by
        have hsquare_decomp :
            ∀ s : ℝ,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => Y μ + s * p * u μ + s * r * v μ)
                (fun μ => Y μ + s * p * u μ + s * r * v μ)
              =
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y Y +
                s ^ 2 * (p ^ 2 + r ^ 2) := by
          intro s
          -- Expand bilinearly; cross terms vanish by `hYu`, `hYv`, `huv`.
          ring_nf
          simp [MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul,
            huu, hvv, huv, hYu, hYv,
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner_comm]
        have hneg_at_one :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y Y + (p ^ 2 + r ^ 2) < 0 := by
          simpa [BHW.InOpenForwardCone, hsquare_decomp 1] using hYpr.2
        have hYY_neg : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d Y Y < 0 := by
          nlinarith [sq_nonneg p, sq_nonneg r, hneg_at_one]
        have hpath_timelike :
            ∀ s ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1,
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => Y μ + s * p * u μ + s * r * v μ)
                (fun μ => Y μ + s * p * u μ + s * r * v μ) < 0 := by
          intro s hs
          rw [hsquare_decomp s]
          have hs_sq : s ^ 2 <= 1 := by nlinarith [hs.1, hs.2]
          nlinarith [sq_nonneg p, sq_nonneg r, hs_sq, hneg_at_one]
        have htime_pos :
            Y 0 > 0 :=
          BHW.forwardCone_timeOrientation_constant_on_timelike_segment
            (d := d)
            (γ := fun s μ => Y μ + s * p * u μ + s * r * v μ)
            (hγ_cont := by
              exact continuous_pi fun μ =>
                continuous_const.add
                  ((continuous_id.mul continuous_const).mul continuous_const).add
                    ((continuous_id.mul continuous_const).mul continuous_const))
            (hγ_timelike := hpath_timelike)
            (hγ_one_time := by simpa using hYpr.1)
        exact ⟨htime_pos, hYY_neg⟩

      /-- Algebraic split of one complex null vector.  If `q ≠ 0`,
      `B(q,q)=0`, and `q` is orthogonal to a forward-tube configuration in
      the sense needed by the second remark, then `q` is a nonzero scalar
      multiple of `u + i v` with real spacelike orthonormal `u,v`.  The
      excluded case is the Hall-Wightman lightlike-collinear alternative:
      a nonzero real null vector orthogonal to every strict forward-tube
      imaginary difference is impossible. -/
      theorem BHW.complexNullOrthogonal_forwardTube_spacelikeNormalForm
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ζ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hζ : ζ ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hq_ne : q ≠ 0)
          (hq_null : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (ζ i) = 0) :
          ∃ (α : ℂ) (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ),
            α ≠ 0 ∧
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)) ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1 ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1 ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0 := by
        let qre : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ := fun μ => (q μ).re
        let qim : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ := fun μ => (q μ).im
        have hnull_real :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qre =
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qim qim ∧
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d qre qim = 0 :=
          BHW.complexNull_iff_real_imag_equal_orthogonal
            (d := d) (q := q) hq_null
        have hqreim_ne : qre ≠ 0 ∨ qim ≠ 0 :=
          BHW.real_imag_nonzero_of_complex_ne_zero (q := q) hq_ne
        have hnot_lightlike_collinear :
            ¬ BHW.realNullCollinear d qre qim := by
          intro hcoll
          rcases BHW.realNullCollinear_commonVector hcoll hqreim_ne with
            ⟨ℓ, hℓ_ne, hℓ_null, hqre, hqim⟩
          have hℓ_orth_diffs :
              ∀ k : Fin n,
                MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d ℓ
                  (fun μ =>
                    ((ζ k μ) -
                      (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                       else ζ ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ μ)).im) = 0 :=
            BHW.realNullCollinear_orthogonal_forwardTube_differences
              (d := d)
              (by simpa [qre, qim] using hq_orth)
              hqreim_ne
              hqre hqim
          exact
            BHW.nonzero_realNull_not_orthogonal_to_forwardCone_differences
              (d := d) hζ hℓ_ne hℓ_null hℓ_orth_diffs
        rcases
          BHW.realImag_null_not_collinear_to_spacelike_orthonormal
            (d := d) hd hqreim_ne hnull_real hnot_lightlike_collinear with
          ⟨α, u, v, hα, hq_reim, huu, hvv, huv⟩
        refine ⟨α, u, v, hα, ?_, huu, hvv, huv⟩
        ext μ
        simpa [qre, qim] using congrFun hq_reim μ

      /-- Hall-Wightman's coefficient split in the second remark.  Once
      `q = α (u + i v)`, every vector `ξ i` orthogonal to `q` decomposes
      uniquely as `η i + β i q`, where `η i` is orthogonal to both real
      spacelike vectors `u` and `v`. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_secondRemark_coefficients_orthogonalTo_nullPlane
          [NeZero d]
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {α : ℂ} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hα : α ≠ 0)
          (hq :
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (ξ i) = 0) :
          ∃ (η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
            (β : Fin n -> ℂ),
            (∀ i μ, ξ i μ = η i μ + β i * q μ) ∧
            (∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0) ∧
            (∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0) := by
        let cu : (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
          fun x => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) x
        let cv : (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
          fun x => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) x
        let β : Fin n -> ℂ :=
          fun i => cu (ξ i) / α
        let η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          fun i μ => ξ i μ - β i * q μ
        refine ⟨η, β, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
        · intro i μ
          simp [η]
        · intro i
          have hcu_q : cu q = α := by
            simp [cu, hq, huu, huv]
          have hcv_q : cv q = Complex.I * α := by
            simp [cv, hq, hvv, huv, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
          simp [η, β, cu, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right, hcu_q, hα]
        · intro i
          have hcu_q : cu q = α := by
            simp [cu, hq, huu, huv]
          have hcv_q : cv q = Complex.I * α := by
            simp [cv, hq, hvv, huv, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
          have horth_i := hq_orth i
          -- The real and imaginary parts of `hq_orth i`, expanded with
          -- `hq`, give `cv (ξ i) = Complex.I * cu (ξ i)`.
          have hcv_relation : cv (ξ i) = Complex.I * cu (ξ i) :=
            BHW.hw_nullPlane_orthogonality_relation
              (d := d) hq huu hvv huv horth_i
          simp [η, β, cv, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right, hcv_q,
            hcv_relation, hα]

      /-- The tube part of Hall-Wightman's second remark after the coefficient
      split: if `ξ + a q` is in the forward tube and
      `ξ = η + β q` is the orthogonal-null-plane split, then `η` is in the
      forward tube.  This is exactly equation (41) applied to each successive
      imaginary difference. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_secondRemark_eta_mem_forwardTube
          [NeZero d]
          {ξ η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {a β : Fin n -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {α : ℂ} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hξ_split : ∀ i μ, ξ i μ = η i μ + β i * q μ)
          (hq :
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (huη :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0)
          (hvη :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0)
          (hξaq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + a i * q μ) ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n) :
          η ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
        intro k
        let prevη : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          if h : k.val = 0 then 0 else η ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩
        let prevξ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          if h : k.val = 0 then 0 else ξ ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩
        let δη : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ := fun μ => η k μ - prevη μ
        let γ : ℂ :=
          (β k + a k) -
            (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
             else β ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ +
                  a ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩)
        have hdiff :
            (fun μ =>
              ((ξ k μ + a k * q μ) -
                (if h : k.val = 0 then 0
                 else ξ ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ μ +
                      a ⟨k.val - 1, by omega⟩ * q μ)).im)
            =
            fun μ => (δη μ + γ * q μ).im := by
          ext μ
          simp [δη, prevη, hξ_split, γ, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc,
            sub_eq_add_neg, mul_add]
        have horth_u :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun μ => (δη μ).im) u = 0 :=
          BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector
            (d := d) huη k
        have horth_v :
            MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d
              (fun μ => (δη μ).im) v = 0 :=
          BHW.imag_difference_orthogonal_realVector
            (d := d) hvη k
        have hcone_with_plane :
            BHW.InOpenForwardCone d (fun μ => (δη μ + γ * q μ).im) := by
          simpa [BHW.ForwardTube, hdiff] using hξaq k
        rcases
          BHW.imag_nullNormalForm_coefficients
            (d := d) hq γ with
          ⟨p, r, himag_eq⟩
        have hremove :=
          BHW.forwardCone_remove_spacelikeOrthogonal_twoPlane
            (d := d) (Y := fun μ => (δη μ).im)
            (u := u) (v := v) (p := p) (r := r)
            huu hvv huv horth_u horth_v
            (by simpa [himag_eq] using hcone_with_plane)
        simpa [BHW.ForwardTube, δη, prevη] using hremove

      /-- Hall-Wightman's second remark after Lemma 2.  A forward-tube point
      with one orthogonal complex-null residual can be rewritten as a
      forward-tube point `η` plus another coefficient family along the same
      null vector, and in that representation the null vector has the standard
      real spacelike normal form.  This theorem does **not** say that the
      original base `ξ` is in the forward tube. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_secondRemark_forwardTube_singleNullResidual_normalForm
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {a : Fin n -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + a i * q μ) ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hq_null :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (ξ i) = 0) :
          ∃ (η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
            (β : Fin n -> ℂ),
            η ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ∧
            BHW.HWNullResidualNormalForm d n q η ∧
            (∀ i μ, ξ i μ = η i μ + β i * q μ) := by
        by_cases hq0 : q = 0
        · refine ⟨ξ, 0, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
          · simpa [hq0] using hξq
          · exact Or.inl hq0
          · intro i μ
            simp [hq0]
        · rcases
            BHW.complexNullOrthogonal_forwardTube_spacelikeNormalForm
              (d := d) hd hξq hq0 hq_null ?_ with
            ⟨α, u, v, hα, hq, huu, hvv, huv⟩
          · rcases
              BHW.hw_secondRemark_coefficients_orthogonalTo_nullPlane
                (d := d) (ξ := ξ) (q := q)
                hα hq huu hvv huv hq_orth with
              ⟨η, β, hξ_split, huη, hvη⟩
            have hηFT :
                η ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n :=
              BHW.hw_secondRemark_eta_mem_forwardTube
                (d := d) hξ_split hq huu hvv huv huη hvη hξq
            refine ⟨η, β, hηFT, ?_, hξ_split⟩
            exact Or.inr ⟨α, u, v, hα, hq, huu, hvv, huv, huη, hvη⟩
          · intro i
            -- `hξq` is the forward point, but `q` is orthogonal to `ξ`.
            -- The normal-form lemma only uses the orthogonality to the base
            -- vectors; the extra `a i q` term is null and orthogonal to `q`.
            simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right, hq_null,
              hq_orth i]

      /-- Hall-Wightman's third remark after Lemma 2.  In the standard null
      normal form, changing the coefficients of the null residual keeps the
      configuration in the ordinary extended tube. -/
      /-- Explicit complex rotation in the spacelike two-plane of the third
      remark.  Write `cu x = B(u,x)` and `cv x = B(v,x)`.  Since
      `B(u,u)=B(v,v)=1` and `B(u,v)=0`, the vector
      `x - cu x • u - cv x • v` is orthogonal to both `u` and `v`.  The
      operator below fixes that orthogonal complement and applies the
      two-by-two complex orthogonal matrix from Hall-Wightman equation (42)
      on the `u,v` plane. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation
          [NeZero d]
          (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ)
          (t : ℝ)
          (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
        let cu : ℂ :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) x
        let cv : ℂ :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) x
        fun μ =>
          x μ
            + cu * (((Real.cosh t : ℂ) - 1) * (u μ : ℂ) +
                Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : ℂ) * (v μ : ℂ))
            + cv * (-Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : ℂ) * (u μ : ℂ) +
                (((Real.cosh t : ℂ) - 1) * (v μ : ℂ)))

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_linear
          [NeZero d]
          (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ)
          (t : ℝ) :
          IsLinearMap ℂ (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t)

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_apply_orthogonal
          [NeZero d]
          {u v x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {ur vr : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          {t : ℝ}
          (hu :
            u = fun μ => (ur μ : ℂ))
          (hv :
            v = fun μ => (vr μ : ℂ))
          (hux : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d u x = 0)
          (hvx : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d v x = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) ur vr t x = x := by
        ext μ
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, hu, hv, hux, hvx]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_apply_u
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          {t : ℝ}
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
              (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) =
            fun μ =>
              (Real.cosh t : ℂ) * (u μ : ℂ) +
              Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : ℂ) * (v μ : ℂ) := by
        ext μ
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, huu, huv,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_apply_v
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          {t : ℝ}
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
              (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) =
            fun μ =>
              -Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : ℂ) * (u μ : ℂ) +
              (Real.cosh t : ℂ) * (v μ : ℂ) := by
        ext μ
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, hvv, huv,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_preserves_inner
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          {t : ℝ}
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (x y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t x)
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t y) =
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d x y := by
        let U : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ := fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)
        let V : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ := fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)
        let cu : (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
          fun z => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d U z
        let cv : (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
          fun z => BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d V z
        let xperp : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          fun μ => x μ - cu x * U μ - cv x * V μ
        let yperp : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          fun μ => y μ - cu y * U μ - cv y * V μ
        have hxperp_u : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d U xperp = 0 := by
          simp [xperp, cu, cv, U, V, huu, huv]
        have hxperp_v : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d V xperp = 0 := by
          simp [xperp, cu, cv, U, V, hvv, huv,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
        have hyperp_u : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d U yperp = 0 := by
          simp [yperp, cu, cv, U, V, huu, huv]
        have hyperp_v : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d V yperp = 0 := by
          simp [yperp, cu, cv, U, V, hvv, huv,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm]
        have hdecomp_x :
            x = fun μ => xperp μ + cu x * U μ + cv x * V μ := by
          ext μ
          simp [xperp, sub_eq_add_neg, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc]
        have hdecomp_y :
            y = fun μ => yperp μ + cu y * U μ + cv y * V μ := by
          ext μ
          simp [yperp, sub_eq_add_neg, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc]
        -- Expand both sides using the decompositions.  Orthogonal-complement
        -- cross terms vanish, and the two-plane block is preserved by
      ```
