        -- `(cosh t)^2 - (sinh t)^2 = 1`; the mixed `u,v` terms cancel.
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, hdecomp_x, hdecomp_y,
          hxperp_u, hxperp_v, hyperp_u, hyperp_v, U, V, huu, hvv, huv,
          Real.cosh_sq_sub_sinh_sq]
        ring

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_scale_null
          [NeZero d]
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {α : ℂ} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          {t : ℝ}
          (hq :
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t q =
            fun μ => (Real.exp t : ℂ) * q μ := by
        rw [hq]
        ext μ
        simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation, huu, hvv, huv,
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm, Complex.ofReal_exp,
          Real.exp_eq_cosh_add_sinh]
        ring

      theorem BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_preserving_toMatrix'_metric
          [NeZero d]
          (L :
            (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ->ₗ[ℂ]
              (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (hpres :
            ∀ x y,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (L x) (L y) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d x y) :
          ∀ μ ν : Fin (d + 1),
            ∑ α : Fin (d + 1),
              (minkowskiSignature d α : ℂ) *
                (LinearMap.toMatrix' L) α μ *
                (LinearMap.toMatrix' L) α ν =
            if μ = ν then (minkowskiSignature d μ : ℂ) else 0 := by
        intro μ ν
        have h := hpres (Pi.single μ (1 : ℂ)) (Pi.single ν (1 : ℂ))
        simpa [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner, LinearMap.toMatrix'_apply,
          Pi.single_apply, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm] using h

      theorem BHW.det_complexTwoPlaneRotation_of_adaptedBasis
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (t : ℝ)
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          (LinearMap.toMatrix'
            ({ toFun := BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
               map_add' := by
                 intro x y
                 ext μ
                 simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation,
                   BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
                   add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc, mul_add]
               map_smul' := by
                 intro c x
                 ext μ
                 simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation,
                   BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right,
                   mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc] } :
              (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ->ₗ[ℂ]
                (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))).det = 1

      /-
      Proof transcript for `det_complexTwoPlaneRotation_of_adaptedBasis`.
      From `huu = 1`, `hvv = 1`, and `huv = 0`, the complex vectors
      `U := fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)` and `V := fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)` are linearly
      independent.  Let `P := Submodule.span ℂ {U,V}` and
      `Pperp := LinearMap.BilinForm.orthogonal B P`, where `B` is
      `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d`.  The restricted form on `P` is the
      identity matrix, so `P` is nondegenerate and
      `IsCompl P Pperp` by
      `LinearMap.BilinForm.restrict_nondegenerate_iff_isCompl_orthogonal`.
      Choose an arbitrary basis of `Pperp` and concatenate it with `U,V`.
      The matrix of `complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation u v t` in this adapted
      basis is block diagonal with the identity on `Pperp` and the two-by-two
      block
      `!![(Real.cosh t : ℂ), -Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : ℂ);
          Complex.I * (Real.sinh t : ℂ), (Real.cosh t : ℂ)]`
      on `P`.  Its determinant is
      `(Real.cosh t : ℂ)^2 -
        (-Complex.I * Real.sinh t) * (Complex.I * Real.sinh t) = 1`,
      by `Real.cosh_sq_sub_sinh_sq`.  Finally use the standard
      change-of-basis determinant identity
      `LinearMap.det_toMatrix_changeBasis` to identify this determinant with
      `LinearMap.toMatrix'` in the coordinate basis.
      -/

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_det_one
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (t : ℝ)
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          (LinearMap.toMatrix'
            ({ toFun := BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
               map_add' := by
                 intro x y
                 ext μ
                 simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation,
                   BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
                   add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc, mul_add]
               map_smul' := by
                 intro c x
                 ext μ
                 simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation,
                   BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right,
                   mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc] } :
              (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ->ₗ[ℂ]
                (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))).det = 1 := by
        -- Extend the independent pair `(u,v)` to a complex basis adapted to
        -- `span {u,v} ⊕ span {u,v}ᗮ`.  In that basis the matrix is block
        -- diagonal: the complement block is the identity, and the plane block
        -- is
        --   [[cosh t, -I*sinh t],
        --    [I*sinh t,  cosh t]].
        -- Its determinant is `cosh^2 t - sinh^2 t = 1`; determinant is
        -- invariant under change of basis.
        exact
          BHW.det_complexTwoPlaneRotation_of_adaptedBasis
            (d := d) (t := t) huu hvv huv

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_to_complexLorentzGroup
          [NeZero d]
          {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (t : ℝ)
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          ∃ A : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (∀ x μ,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction A x μ =
                BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t x μ) := by
        let L : (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ->ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
          { toFun := BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation (d := d) u v t
            map_add' := by
              intro x y
              -- Expand the formula; `sourceComplexMinkowskiInner` is
              -- linear in the second argument.
              ext μ
              simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
                add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc, mul_add]
            map_smul' := by
              intro c x
              ext μ
              simp [BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right,
                mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc] }
        have hpres :
            ∀ x y,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (L x) (L y) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d x y :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_preserves_inner
            (d := d) huu hvv huv
        let M : Matrix (Fin (d + 1)) (Fin (d + 1)) ℂ :=
          LinearMap.toMatrix' L
        have hM_apply :
            ∀ x μ, (∑ ν, M μ ν * x ν) = L x μ := by
          intro x μ
          simpa [M] using
            LinearMap.toMatrix'_mulVec L x μ
        have hmetric :
            ∀ μ ν : Fin (d + 1),
              ∑ α : Fin (d + 1),
                (minkowskiSignature d α : ℂ) * M α μ * M α ν =
              if μ = ν then (minkowskiSignature d μ : ℂ) else 0 :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_preserving_toMatrix'_metric
            (d := d) L hpres
        have hdet : M.det = 1 :=
          BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_det_one
            (d := d) (t := t) huu hvv huv
        refine ⟨⟨M, hmetric, hdet⟩, ?_⟩
        intro x μ
        simpa [BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction, hM_apply, L]

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_twoPlaneComplexRotation_lorentz
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {α : ℂ} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hq :
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0) :
          ∃ A : ℝ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (∀ t (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) x = 0 ->
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) x = 0 ->
              ∀ μ, BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction (A t) x μ = x μ) ∧
            (∀ t μ,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction (A t) q μ =
                (Real.exp t : ℂ) * q μ) := by
        choose A hA_apply using
          fun t =>
            BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_to_complexLorentzGroup
              (d := d) (t := t) huu hvv huv
        refine ⟨A, ?_, ?_⟩
        · intro t x hux hvx μ
          rw [hA_apply]
          exact congrFun
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_apply_orthogonal
              (d := d) (ur := u) (vr := v) (t := t)
              (u := fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) (v := fun μ => (v μ : ℂ))
              rfl rfl hux hvx) μ
        · intro t μ
          rw [hA_apply]
          exact congrFun
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiTwoPlaneRotation_scale_null
              (d := d) (t := t) hq huu hvv huv) μ

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_nullPlaneScalingFamily
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {α : ℂ} {u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ}
          (hα : α ≠ 0)
          (hq :
            q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)))
          (huu : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1)
          (hvv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1)
          (huv : MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0)
          (huη :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0)
          (hvη :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0) :
          ∃ A : ℝ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (∀ t i μ, BHW.complexLorentzAction (A t) η i μ = η i μ) ∧
            (∀ t μ,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction (A t) q μ =
                (Real.exp t : ℂ) * q μ) := by
        classical
        -- On the complex span of `u,v`, use the matrix
        -- `u ↦ cosh t • u + I*sinh t • v`,
        -- `v ↦ -I*sinh t • u + cosh t • v`; it preserves the bilinear
        -- form because `cosh^2 - sinh^2 = 1`, and it sends
        -- `u + I v` to `exp t • (u + I v)`.
        -- On the orthogonal complement of this nondegenerate spacelike
        -- plane, use the identity.  Since every `η i` is orthogonal to both
        -- `u` and `v`, it lies in that fixed complement.
        choose A hA_fix_orth hA_scale hA_lorentz using
          BHW.complexMinkowski_twoPlaneComplexRotation_lorentz
            (d := d) hd hq huu hvv huv
        refine ⟨A, ?_, ?_⟩
        · intro t i μ
          exact hA_fix_orth t (η i) (huη i) (hvη i) μ
        · intro t μ
          exact hA_scale t μ

      theorem BHW.hw_thirdRemark_nullNormalForm_scalingFamily
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hη : η ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hnorm :
            ∃ (α : ℂ) (u v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℝ),
              α ≠ 0 ∧
              q = fun μ => α * ((u μ : ℂ) + Complex.I * (v μ : ℂ)) ∧
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u u = 1 ∧
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d v v = 1 ∧
              MinkowskiSpace.minkowskiInner d u v = 0 ∧
              (∀ i,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (fun μ => (u μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0) ∧
              (∀ i,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (fun μ => (v μ : ℂ)) (η i) = 0)) :
          ∃ A : ℝ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            (∀ t i μ, BHW.complexLorentzAction (A t) η i μ = η i μ) ∧
            (∀ t μ,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction (A t) q μ =
                (Real.exp t : ℂ) * q μ) := by
        rcases hnorm with
          ⟨α, u, v, hα, hq, huu, hvv, huv, huη, hvη⟩
        exact
          BHW.complexMinkowski_nullPlaneScalingFamily
            (d := d) hd hα hq huu hvv huv huη hvη

      /-- Openness of the forward tube at the base point in the third remark.
      For fixed coefficients `b`, the perturbation
      `η + exp(-t) b q` tends to `η` in the finite product topology, hence is
      eventually in the forward tube. -/
      theorem BHW.forwardTube_eventually_singleNullSmallCoefficients
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hη : η ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (b : Fin n -> ℂ)
          (q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          ∀ᶠ t : ℝ in atTop,
            (fun i μ => η i μ + Real.exp (-t) * b i * q μ) ∈
              BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
        have hopen : IsOpen (BHW.ForwardTube d n) :=
          BHW.isOpen_forwardTube (d := d) (n := n)
        have htend :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ℝ =>
                fun i μ => Real.exp (-t) * b i * q μ)
              atTop
              (nhds (0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) :=
          BHW.tendsto_singleResidual_exp_neg_zero
            (d := d) (n := n) b q
        have hto :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ℝ =>
                fun i μ => η i μ + Real.exp (-t) * b i * q μ)
              atTop (nhds η) := by
          simpa using htend.const_add η
        exact hto.eventually (hopen.mem_nhds hη)

      theorem BHW.hw_thirdRemark_nullNormalForm_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {η : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hη : η ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hnorm : BHW.HWNullResidualNormalForm d n q η) :
          ∀ b : Fin n -> ℂ,
            (fun i μ => η i μ + b i * q μ) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro b
        rcases hnorm with hq0 | ⟨α, u, v, hα, hq, huu, hvv, huv, huη, hvη⟩
        · simpa [hq0] using BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hη
        · rcases
            BHW.hw_thirdRemark_nullNormalForm_scalingFamily
              (d := d) hd (η := η) (q := q) hη
              ⟨α, u, v, hα, hq, huu, hvv, huv, huη, hvη⟩ with
            ⟨A, hA_fix_η, hA_scale_q⟩
          have hsmall :
              ∀ᶠ t : ℝ in atTop,
                (fun i μ => η i μ + Real.exp (-t) * b i * q μ) ∈
                  BHW.ForwardTube d n :=
            BHW.forwardTube_eventually_singleNullSmallCoefficients
              (d := d) hd hη b q
          rcases hsmall.exists with ⟨t, htFT⟩
          have hA_eq :
              BHW.complexLorentzAction (A t)
                (fun i μ => η i μ + Real.exp (-t) * b i * q μ) =
              (fun i μ => η i μ + b i * q μ) := by
            ext i μ
            simp [BHW.complexLorentzAction, hA_fix_η, hA_scale_q,
              mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
          exact
            hA_eq ▸
              BHW.complexLorentzAction_mem_extendedTube
                (d := d) n (A t)
                (BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube htFT)

      /-- One-null-vector coefficient freedom, forward representative version:
      if one coefficient choice along an orthogonal null residual is in the
      forward tube, every coefficient choice is in the ordinary extended
      tube. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_singleIsotropicResidual_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube_of_forwardEndpoint
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {a : Fin n -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξa :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + a i * q μ) ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n)
          (hq_null :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (ξ i) = 0) :
          ∀ b : Fin n -> ℂ,
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + b i * q μ) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        rcases BHW.hw_secondRemark_forwardTube_singleNullResidual_normalForm
            (d := d) hd hξa hq_null hq_orth with
          ⟨η, β, hηFT, hnorm, hξ_eq⟩
        intro b
        have hη_coeff :
            (fun i μ => η i μ + (β i + b i) * q μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          BHW.hw_thirdRemark_nullNormalForm_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd hηFT hnorm (fun i => β i + b i)
        simpa [hξ_eq, add_mul, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc] using
          hη_coeff

      /-- One-null-vector coefficient freedom, arbitrary extended-tube
      endpoint version, obtained by transporting to a forward-tube
      representative and back. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_singleIsotropicResidual_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {a : Fin n -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξa :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + a i * q μ) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_null :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q q = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q (ξ i) = 0) :
          ∀ b : Fin n -> ℂ,
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + b i * q μ) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro b
        rcases Set.mem_iUnion.mp hξa with ⟨Λ, y, hyFT, hy_eq⟩
        let ξ0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ⁻¹ ξ
        let q0 : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λ⁻¹ q
        have hy_as_coeff :
            y = fun i μ => ξ0 i μ + a i * q0 μ := by
          apply BHW.complexLorentzAction_cancel_left (Λ := Λ)
          simpa [ξ0, q0, BHW.complexLorentzAction_add,
            BHW.complexLorentzAction_smul_vector] using hy_eq.symm
        have hq0_null :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q0 q0 = 0 :=
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_complexLorentzVectorAction
            (d := d) Λ⁻¹ q q ▸ hq_null
        have hq0_orth :
            ∀ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d q0 (ξ0 i) = 0 := by
          intro i
          simpa [ξ0, q0] using
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_complexLorentzVectorAction
              (d := d) Λ⁻¹ q (ξ i) ▸ hq_orth i
        have hforward_all :
            (fun i μ => ξ0 i μ + b i * q0 μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          BHW.hw_singleIsotropicResidual_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube_of_forwardEndpoint
            (d := d) hd
            (ξ := ξ0) (a := a) (q := q0)
            (by simpa [hy_as_coeff] using hyFT)
            hq0_null hq0_orth b
        have htransport :
            BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ
              (fun i μ => ξ0 i μ + b i * q0 μ) =
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + b i * q μ) := by
          simp [ξ0, q0, BHW.complexLorentzAction_add,
            BHW.complexLorentzAction_smul_vector,
            BHW.complexLorentzAction_inv]
        simpa [htransport] using
          BHW.complexLorentzAction_mem_extendedTube
            (d := d) n Λ hforward_all

      /-- Finite-frame coefficient freedom.  The proof replaces coefficients
      one frame vector at a time, using the one-null-vector theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube_finset
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ι : Type} [DecidableEq ι]
          {S : Finset ι}
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {a : Fin n -> ι -> ℂ}
          {q : ι -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c in S, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c ∈ S, ∀ c' ∈ S,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c ∈ S, ∀ i,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ∀ b : Fin n -> ι -> ℂ,
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c in S, b i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        classical
        revert hξq hq_pair_zero hq_orth
        refine Finset.induction_on S ?base ?step
        · intro hξ hpair horth b
          simpa using hξ
        · intro c S hc hIH hξcS hpair horth b
          let ξS : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
            fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c' in S, a i c' * q c' μ
          have hc_null :
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c) = 0 :=
            hpair c (by simp [hc]) c (by simp [hc])
          have hc_orth_ξS :
              ∀ i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξS i) = 0 := by
            intro i
            have hsum :
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c)
                  (fun μ => ∑ c' in S, a i c' * q c' μ) = 0 := by
              -- Expand by linearity in the second variable; every summand is
              -- zero by `hpair c _ c' _`.
              simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_right,
                hpair c (by simp [hc])]
            simp [ξS, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
              horth c (by simp [hc]) i, hsum]
          have hreplace_c :
              (fun i μ => ξS i μ + b i c * q c μ) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
            BHW.hw_singleIsotropicResidual_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
              (d := d) hd
              (ξ := ξS) (a := fun i => a i c) (q := q c)
              (by
                simpa [ξS, Finset.sum_insert, hc, add_comm, add_left_comm,
                  add_assoc] using hξcS)
              hc_null hc_orth_ξS (fun i => b i c)
          let ξSb : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
            fun i μ => ξ i μ + b i c * q c μ
          have horth_tail :
              ∀ c' ∈ S, ∀ i,
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c') (ξSb i) = 0 := by
            intro c' hc' i
            have hqc'qc : BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c') (q c) = 0 :=
              hpair c' (by simp [hc, hc']) c (by simp [hc])
            simp [ξSb, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right,
              horth c' (by simp [hc, hc']) i, hqc'qc]
          have htail_start :
              (fun i μ => ξSb i μ + ∑ c' in S, a i c' * q c' μ) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
            simpa [ξS, ξSb, add_comm, add_left_comm, add_assoc] using
              hreplace_c
          have htail :=
            hIH htail_start
              (fun c' hc' c'' hc'' => hpair c' (by simp [hc, hc'])
                c'' (by simp [hc, hc'']))
              horth_tail
              b
          simpa [ξSb, Finset.sum_insert, hc, add_comm, add_left_comm,
            add_assoc] using htail

      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_base_mem_extendedTube_of_endpoint_nonempty
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        by
          have hall :=
            BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube_finset
              (d := d) hd (S := (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin s)))
              (ξ := ξ) (a := a) (q := q) hξq
              (by intro c _ c' _; exact hq_pair_zero c c')
              (by intro c _ i; exact hq_orth c i)
              (fun _ _ => 0)
          simpa using hall

      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube_nonempty
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ∧
          ∀ b : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ,
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, b i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        have hall :=
          BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube_finset
            (d := d) hd (S := (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin s)))
            (ξ := ξ) (a := a) (q := q) hξq
            (by intro c _ c' _; exact hq_pair_zero c c')
            (by intro c _ i; exact hq_orth c i)
        constructor
        · simpa using hall (fun _ _ => 0)
        · intro b
          simpa using hall b

      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ∧
          ∀ b : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ,
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, b i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        by_cases hn : n = 0
        · subst hn
          have hξ : ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d 0 := by
            simpa [Subsingleton.elim
              (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ξ] using hξq
          constructor
          · exact hξ
          · intro b
            simpa [Subsingleton.elim
              (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, b i c * q c μ) ξ] using hξ
        · haveI : NeZero n := ⟨hn⟩
          exact
            BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube_nonempty
              (d := d) hd hξq hq_pair_zero hq_orth

      theorem BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_base_mem_extendedTube_of_endpoint
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        (BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
          (d := d) hd hξq hq_pair_zero hq_orth).1
      ```

      The two non-mechanical theorems in this coefficient-freedom block are
      exactly Hall-Wightman's second and third remarks.  For the second
      remark, write `q = u + i v`.  From `q.q = 0`, `u.u = v.v` and
      `u.v = 0`; the lightlike-collinear case is excluded because a nonzero
      real null vector orthogonal to the strict forward-cone imaginary part
      would lie in the positive definite orthogonal complement of a timelike
      vector.  Thus, after rescaling, `q = α (w1 + i w2)` with real spacelike
      orthonormal `w1,w2`.  Expanding each source vector into the
      `w1,w2` plane and its orthogonal complement, the equations `q.ξ_i = 0`
      force the coefficients into a multiple of `q`; subtracting that
      multiple gives the forward-tube vector `η_i` orthogonal to `w1,w2`.
      The forward-cone inequality is checked by continuously decreasing the
      two transverse real coefficients in Hall-Wightman's displayed equation
      (41); the time orientation cannot flip inside the open cone component.
      For the third remark, the complex Lorentz transformation that is the
      identity on the orthogonal complement of `span {w1,w2}` and acts by
      `w1 ↦ w1 cos(iθ) - w2 sin(iθ)`,
      `w2 ↦ w1 sin(iθ) + w2 cos(iθ)` fixes every `η_i` and scales `q` by
      `exp(θ)`.  Openness of `ForwardTube` near `η` puts
      `η + exp(-θ) b q` in the forward tube for large `θ`; applying this
      Lorentz transformation witnesses `η + b q ∈ ExtendedTube d n`.

      The finite-frame all-coefficients theorem above is the membership
      theorem.  It is not proved from the dual-frame contraction packet, and
      it does not use openness of the ordinary extended tube at the base.
      The dual frame is still load-bearing later: it constructs the explicit
      null-boost family used to identify endpoint values with the common
      singular limit.  The topology needed for that later limit is only the
      finite-coordinate convergence of the contracted residuals:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_base
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          (a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ)
          (q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ℝ =>
              fun i μ =>
                ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c μ)
            atTop (nhds ξ) := by
        have hsmall :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ℝ =>
                fun i μ => ∑ c : Fin s,
                  Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c μ)
              atTop
              (nhds (0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) :=
          BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_zero
            (a := a) (q := q)
        simpa using hsmall.const_add ξ

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicContraction_membership_tendsto_base
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0) :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ∧
          (∀ t,
            (fun i μ =>
              ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ℝ =>
              fun i μ =>
                ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c μ)
            atTop (nhds ξ) := by
        rcases BHW.hw_isotropicFrame_allCoefficients_mem_extendedTube
            (d := d) hd hξq hq_pair_zero hq_orth with
          ⟨hξ_ET, hall⟩
        constructor
        · exact hξ_ET
        constructor
        · intro t
          exact hall (fun i c => Real.exp (-t) * a i c)
        · exact BHW.tendsto_isotropicResidual_exp_neg_base
            (d := d) hd a q
      ```

      /-- Hall-Wightman's tube lemma after Lemma 2: every contracted
      residual-frame configuration remains in the ordinary extended tube and
      converges to the base configuration.  The dual-frame contraction family
      is used in the subsequent value-equality step, where these contracted
      configurations are identified as complex-Lorentz transforms of the
      original endpoint. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicContraction_staysIn_extendedTube
          [NeZero d] [NeZero n]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {ξ : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {s : Nat}
          {a : Fin n -> Fin s -> ℂ}
          {q qDual : Fin s -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          {contract : ℝ -> ComplexLorentzGroup d}
          (hξq :
            (fun i μ => ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hq_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (q c') = 0)
          (hqDual_pair_zero :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (qDual c') = 0)
          (hq_dual :
            ∀ c c',
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (qDual c') =
                if c = c' then (1 : ℂ) else 0)
          (hq_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (q c) (ξ i) = 0)
          (hqDual_orth :
            ∀ c i, BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (qDual c) (ξ i) = 0)
          (hcontract_fix_ξ :
            ∀ t i μ,
              (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val μ ν * ξ i ν) = ξ i μ)
          (hcontract_scale_q :
            ∀ t c μ,
              (∑ ν : Fin (d + 1), (contract t).val μ ν * q c ν) =
                Real.exp (-t) * q c μ) :
          ξ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ∧
          (∀ t,
            (fun i μ =>
              ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c μ) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
          Tendsto
            (fun t : ℝ =>
              fun i μ =>
                ξ i μ + ∑ c : Fin s, Real.exp (-t) * a i c * q c μ)
            atTop (nhds ξ) := by
        exact
          BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicContraction_membership_tendsto_base
            (d := d) hd hξq hq_pair_zero hq_orth

      /-- The singular alternative in Hall-Wightman Lemma 2, packaged as the
      actual limiting data used in the proof.  In the high scalar-rank
      alternative the output is just a complex Lorentz orbit equality.  In the
      low-rank alternative the data remain geometric: both endpoint
      configurations have Lorentz-orbit curves in the ordinary extended tube
      which contract to the same base configuration. -/
      structure BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData
          (d n : Nat)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) where
        base : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        curve_left : ℝ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        curve_right : ℝ -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        base_mem : base ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        curve_left_mem : ∀ t, curve_left t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        curve_right_mem : ∀ t, curve_right t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        curve_left_tendsto_base : Tendsto curve_left atTop (nhds base)
        curve_right_tendsto_base : Tendsto curve_right atTop (nhds base)
        curve_left_orbit :
          ∀ t, ∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            curve_left t = BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z
        curve_right_orbit :
          ∀ t, ∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            curve_right t = BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ w

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm d n z w

      theorem BHW.hw_lowRank_isotropicNormalForm_to_contractionData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (N : BHW.HWLowRankIsotropicNormalForm d n z w) :
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w

      /-- Full-complex-orthogonal orbit data needed in the full-rank
      Hall-Wightman branch when the same-Gram frame map has determinant
      `-1`.  This is the geometric half of the improper-component gate above:
      the analytic branch law may consume it only together with the
      source-backed full orthochronous/improper invariance theorem. -/
      structure BHW.HWSameSourceGramImproperOrbitData
          (d n : Nat)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) where
        A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d
        det_neg : BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A = -1
        maps :
          w = BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzAction A z

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 2 in the exact form used by the branch law:
      equal source Gram data give either a determinant-`1` complex Lorentz
      orbit equality, an improper full-complex-orthogonal orbit equality, or
      the singular null-contraction data from the paper.  The middle
      alternative is mandatory in full scalar rank; omitting it silently
      assumes parity/improper invariance. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_fiberAlternative
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hgram :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          (∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            w = BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) ∨
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramImproperOrbitData d n z w ∨
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 2, high scalar-rank case.  This deliberately uses
      the scalar-product rank predicate above.  It is not the repo's
      `SourceComplexGramRegularAt`, which is a local source-map regularity
      predicate used elsewhere for Gram-image charts. -/
      def BHW.sourceCoefficientEval
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          (Fin n -> ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
        { toFun := fun a μ => ∑ i : Fin n, a i * z i μ
          map_add' := by
            intro a b
            ext μ
            simp [add_mul, Finset.sum_add_distrib]
          map_smul' := by
            intro c a
            ext μ
            simp [mul_assoc, Finset.mul_sum] }

      def BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap
          (n : Nat)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) :
          (Fin n -> ℂ) →ₗ[ℂ] (Fin n -> ℂ) :=
        { toFun := fun a j => ∑ i : Fin n, a i * G i j
          map_add' := by
            intro a b
            ext j
            simp [add_mul, Finset.sum_add_distrib]
          map_smul' := by
            intro c a
            ext j
            simp [mul_assoc, Finset.mul_sum] }

      def BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel
          (n : Nat)
          (G : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) :
          Submodule ℂ (Fin n -> ℂ) :=
        LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n G)

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (a : Fin n -> ℂ)
          (j : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a j =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a) (z j) := by
        have hEval :
            BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a =
              (∑ i : Fin n, a i • z i) := by
          ext μ
          simp [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
        calc
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a j
              =
            ∑ i : Fin n,
              a i * BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (z i) (z j) := by
                simp [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram_apply_eq_complexInner]
          _ =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (∑ i : Fin n, a i • z i) (z j) := by
                rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_left]
          _ =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a) (z j) := by
                rw [hEval]

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_single
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (j : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z
              ((Pi.single j 1 : Fin n -> ℂ)) =
            z j := by
        ext μ
        simp only [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval, LinearMap.coe_mk,
          AddHom.coe_mk]
        rw [Finset.sum_eq_single j]
        · simp
        · intro k _ hkj
          simp [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hkj]
        · intro hj
          exact False.elim (hj (Finset.mem_univ j))

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_eq_zero_iff_eval_pair_eval_eq_zero
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (a : Fin n -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a = 0 ↔
            ∀ b : Fin n -> ℂ,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a)
                (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z b) = 0 := by
        constructor
        · intro h b
          have hcoord :
              ∀ j,
                BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a j = 0 :=
            fun j => congrFun h j
          have hEvalB :
              BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z b =
                ∑ j : Fin n, b j • z j := by
            ext μ
            simp [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
          rw [hEvalB]
          rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_right]
          simp [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) n z a, hcoord]
        · intro h
          ext j
          rw [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) n z a j]
          have hj := h (Pi.single j (1 : ℂ))
          simpa [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_single (d := d) n z j] using hj

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_pair_eq_sum_gram
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (a b : Fin n -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a)
              (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z b) =
            ∑ j : Fin n,
              b j *
                BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a j := by
        have hEvalB :
            BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z b =
              ∑ j : Fin n, b j • z j := by
          ext μ
          simp [BHW.sourceCoefficientEval, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul]
        rw [hEvalB]
        rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sum_smul_right]
        apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
        intro j _
        rw [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram
          (d := d) n z a j]
        ring

      theorem BHW.sourceCoefficientEval_ker_le_gramKernel
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z) ≤
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) := by
        intro a ha
        change
          BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) a = 0
        ext j
        rw [BHW.sourceCoefficientGramMap_apply_sourceMinkowskiGram
          (d := d) n z a j]
        rw [show BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z a = 0 from ha]
        simp [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner]

      /-- The left-adjoint map of the complex Minkowski form restricted to a
      finite source-vector span. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) :
          M →ₗ[ℂ] (M →ₗ[ℂ] ℂ) where
        toFun x :=
          { toFun := fun y =>
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
            map_add' := by
              intro y z
              exact BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_right
                d (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                  (z : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
            map_smul' := by
              intro c y
              exact BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_right
                d c (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                  (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) }
        map_add' := by
          intro x y
          ext z
          exact BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_add_left
            d (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (z : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
        map_smul' := by
          intro c x
          ext y
          exact BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_smul_left
            d c (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)

      /-- Radical of the complex Minkowski form restricted to a finite
      source-vector span. -/
      def BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) : Submodule ℂ M :=
        LinearMap.ker (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiLeftMap d M)

      /-- Rank of the restricted complex Minkowski form on `M`, expressed as
      `finrank M - finrank radical`. -/
      def BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank
          (d : Nat)
          (M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) : Nat :=
        Module.finrank ℂ M -
          Module.finrank ℂ (BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRadical d M)

      /-- The rank of the scalar Gram matrix of a source configuration equals
      the rank of the complex Minkowski form restricted to the span generated
      by that configuration.  This is the quotient-rank bridge between the
      coefficient Gram kernel and the geometry of the source-vector span. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_eq_restrictedMinkowskiRank_range
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
            BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))

      /-- If the restricted form on the source-vector span is degenerate, the
      scalar Gram rank is below Hall-Wightman's direct-orbit threshold
      `min d n`.  The proof uses both facts: the span is generated by `n`
      vectors, and a proper subspace of the `(d+1)`-dimensional nondegenerate
      ambient space has dimension at most `d`. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank_lt_orbitThreshold_of_range_degenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (hdeg :
            ¬ BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))) :
          BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) <
            min d n

      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_range_nondegenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z))

      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_eval_ker_eq_gramKernel
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z) :
          LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z) =
            BHW.sourceCoefficientGramKernel n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)

      theorem BHW.hw_highRank_sourceCoefficientEval_ker_eq_of_sameSourceGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z) =
            LinearMap.ker (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w)

      /-- The well-defined span isometry in Hall-Wightman's high-rank branch.
      The map is induced from the common coefficient quotient; it is not the
      informal assignment `z i ↦ w i` until the coefficient kernels have been
      proved equal. -/
      structure BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData
          (d n : Nat)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) where
        M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
        N : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
        M_eq_range :
          M = LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z)
        N_eq_range :
          N = LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n w)
        M_nondegenerate :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M
        N_nondegenerate :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d N
        z_mem : ∀ i, z i ∈ M
        w_mem : ∀ i, w i ∈ N
        T : M ≃ₗ[ℂ] N
        T_preserves :
          ∀ x y : M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (T x : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
                (T y : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
        T_z :
          ∀ i, T ⟨z i, z_mem i⟩ = ⟨w i, w_mem i⟩

      noncomputable def BHW.hw_highRank_spanIsometryData_of_sameSourceGram
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hzRank : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w

      theorem BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryData_sourceGram_eq
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w) :
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w := by
        ext i j
        have hzi := S.T_z i
        have hzj := S.T_z j
        calc
          BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z i j
              =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (⟨z i, S.z_mem i⟩ : S.M)
              (⟨z j, S.z_mem j⟩ : S.M) := by
                rfl
          _ =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (S.T ⟨z i, S.z_mem i⟩ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (S.T ⟨z j, S.z_mem j⟩ : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) := by
                rw [S.T_preserves]
          _ =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (⟨w i, S.w_mem i⟩ : S.N)
              (⟨w j, S.w_mem j⟩ : S.N) := by
                rw [hzi, hzj]
          _ = BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w i j := by
                rfl

      theorem BHW.hwHighRankSpanIsometryData_domain_nondegenerate
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d S.M :=
        S.M_nondegenerate

      The full-rank determinant ratio is not an additional analytic input.
      It is the determinant of the unique linear isometry carrying one
      ordered full frame of `z` to the corresponding ordered full frame of
      `w`.  The proof must expose the uniqueness step, because this is what
      makes the ratio independent of the chosen nonzero full frame.

      Lean-shaped proof transcript for the full-frame ratio:

      ```lean
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis
          (d n : Nat)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0) :
          Basis (Fin (d + 1)) ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
        let M := BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ι z
        let hM : IsUnit M.transpose.det := by
          rw [Matrix.det_transpose]
          exact isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr hι
        (Pi.basisFun ℂ (Fin (d + 1))).map
          (Matrix.toLinearEquiv' (M.transpose) hM)

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis_apply
          (d n : Nat)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0)
          (a : Fin (d + 1)) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis d n ι z hι a = z (ι a) := by
        ext μ
        simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis, BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix,
          Matrix.toLinearEquiv'_apply, Matrix.toLin'_apply,
          Matrix.mulVec_single_one]

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_sameGram_fullFrame
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι w ≠ 0 := by
        have hsel :
            BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ι z) =
              BHW.sourceFullFrameGram d
                (BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ι w) := by
          ext a b
          simpa [BHW.sourceFullFrameGram,
            BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix, BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram]
            using congrFun (congrFun hgram (ι a)) (ι b)
        intro hwzero
        have hdet_eq := congrArg Matrix.det hsel
        rw [BHW.sourceFullFrameGram_det_eq,
          BHW.sourceFullFrameGram_det_eq, hwzero] at hdet_eq
        have hzsq_ne :
            (BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z)^2 ≠ 0 :=
          pow_ne_zero 2 hι
        have hmetric_ne :
            BHW.minkowskiMetricDet d ≠ 0 :=
          (BHW.minkowskiMetricDet_isUnit d).ne_zero
        exact
          (mul_ne_zero hmetric_ne hzsq_ne)
            (by simpa using hdet_eq.symm)

      noncomputable def BHW.sourceFullFrameMap
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0) :
          (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
        let hιw :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_sameGram_fullFrame
            (d := d) d n hgram ι hι
        (BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis d n ι z hι).equiv
          (BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis d n ι w hιw)
          (Equiv.refl _)

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameMap_apply_selected
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0)
          (a : Fin (d + 1)) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι (z (ι a)) =
            w (ι a) := by
        simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameMap,
          BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis_apply, Basis.equiv_apply]

      theorem BHW.bilinForm_eq_of_basis_values
          {ι U : Type*}
          [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
          [AddCommGroup U] [Module ℂ U]
          (B : LinearMap.BilinForm ℂ U)
          (b : Basis ι ℂ U)
          {L : U ≃ₗ[ℂ] U}
          (hbasis : ∀ a b',
            B (L (b a)) (L (b b')) = B (b a) (b b')) :
          ∀ x y, B (L x) (L y) = B x y := by
        intro x y
        have hL :
            B (L x) (L y) =
              ((b.map L).repr (L x)).sum fun i xi =>
                ((b.map L).repr (L y)).sum fun j yj =>
                  xi • yj • B ((b.map L) i) ((b.map L) j) := by
          exact
            (LinearMap.sum_repr_mul_repr_mul
              (b.map L) (b.map L) (B := B) (L x) (L y)).symm
        have h0 :
            B x y =
              (b.repr x).sum fun i xi =>
                (b.repr y).sum fun j yj =>
                  xi • yj • B (b i) (b j) := by
          exact
            (LinearMap.sum_repr_mul_repr_mul
              b b (B := B) x y).symm
        rw [hL, h0]
        simp [Basis.map, hbasis]

      theorem BHW.basis_pairing_zero_ext
          {ι U : Type*}
          [Fintype ι]
          [AddCommGroup U] [Module ℂ U]
          (B : LinearMap.BilinForm ℂ U)
          (b : Basis ι ℂ U)
          {u : U}
          (h : ∀ a, B (b a) u = 0) :
          ∀ v, B v u = 0 := by
        let f : U →ₗ[ℂ] ℂ :=
          { toFun := fun v => B v u
            map_add' := by intro x y; simp
            map_smul' := by intro c x; simp }
        have hf : f = 0 := by
          apply b.ext
          intro i
          simp [f, h i]
        intro v
        simpa [f] using congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe hf) v

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameMap_preserves_inner
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0) :
          ∀ x y,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι x)
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι y) =
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d x y := by
        let bZ := BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis d n ι z hι
        let E := BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι
        have hbasis :
            ∀ a b,
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                (E (bZ a)) (E (bZ b)) =
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d (bZ a) (bZ b) := by
          intro a b
          simp [E, bZ, BHW.sourceFullFrameMap_apply_selected,
            BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis_apply,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram, hgram]
        exact
          BHW.bilinForm_eq_of_basis_values
            (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d) bZ hbasis

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameMap_apply_all
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0)
          (i : Fin n) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι (z i) = w i := by
        let E := BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι
        have hpres :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMap_preserves_inner
            (d := d) d n hgram ι hι
        let hιw :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_ne_zero_of_sameGram_fullFrame
            (d := d) d n hgram ι hι
        let bW := BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis d n ι w hιw
        apply BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_left_nonDegenerate d
        intro v
        -- Reduce `v` to the basis `bW`.  On a basis vector `w (ι a)`,
        -- subtract the two pairings and use `hpres` plus `hgram`.
        have hz :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              v (E (z i) - w i) = 0 :=
          BHW.basis_pairing_zero_ext
            (BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiBilinForm d) bW (fun a => by
            calc
              BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (bW a) (E (z i) - w i)
                  =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (E (z (ι a))) (E (z i)) -
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (w (ι a)) (w i) := by
                    simp [bW, BHW.sourceFullFrameBasis_apply,
                      BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_sub_right,
                      BHW.sourceFullFrameMap_apply_selected]
              _ =
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (z (ι a)) (z i) -
                BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
                  (w (ι a)) (w i) := by
                    rw [hpres]
              _ = 0 := by
                    simpa [BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram]
                      using congrFun (congrFun hgram (ι a)) i) v
        rw [BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner_comm d
          (E (z i) - w i) v]
        exact hz

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix_linearEquivAction
          (d n : Nat)
          (L : (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ι (fun i => L (z i)) =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix d n ι z *
              (LinearMap.toMatrix
                (Pi.basisFun ℂ (Fin (d + 1)))
                (Pi.basisFun ℂ (Fin (d + 1))) L.toLinearMap).transpose := by
        ext a μ
        simp [BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix, Matrix.mul_apply,
          LinearMap.toMatrix_apply, Basis.repr_self]

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_linearEquivAction
          (d n : Nat)
          (L : (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≃ₗ[ℂ] (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι (fun i => L (z i)) =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z *
              LinearMap.det L.toLinearMap := by
        unfold BHW.sourceFullFrameDet
        rw [BHW.sourceFullFrameMatrix_linearEquivAction]
        simp [Matrix.det_mul, Matrix.det_transpose,
          LinearMap.det_toMatrix]

      theorem BHW.det_sourceFullFrameMap_eq_ratio
          [NeZero d]
          (d n : Nat)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0) :
          LinearMap.det
              (BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι).toLinearMap =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι w /
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z := by
        let E := BHW.sourceFullFrameMap d n hgram ι hι
        have hact :=
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_linearEquivAction d n E ι z
        have hall : (fun i => E (z i)) = w := by
          ext i
          exact BHW.sourceFullFrameMap_apply_all d n hgram ι hι i
        rw [hall] at hact
        exact (eq_div_iff hι).mpr hact.symm

      ```

      /-- Independence of the full-frame determinant ratio in the full scalar
      rank case.  If two configurations have the same source Gram matrix and
      one ordered full frame of `z` is nonzero, then every nonzero ordered
      full frame gives the same ratio `det_w / det_z`.  This is the finite
      determinant form of the unique full-ambient same-Gram frame map. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_ratio_eq_of_sameSourceGram_fullRank
          (d n : Nat)
          [NeZero d]
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hfull :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = d + 1)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι κ : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0)
          (hκ : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n κ z ≠ 0) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι w /
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n κ w /
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n κ z

      /-- Determinant of the unique ambient same-Gram frame map in the full
      scalar-rank case.  The total definition chooses any nonzero ordered
      full source frame of `z` when one exists and uses the determinant ratio
      there; the preceding ratio-independence theorem proves that the value is
      independent of this choice under the full-rank same-Gram hypotheses.
      Outside the full-rank case the value is a harmless default and is never
      used by the orientation predicate except through the left disjunct. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.HWFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet
          (d n : Nat)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) : ℂ :=
        if h :
            ∃ ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n,
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0 then
          let ι := Classical.choose h
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι w /
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z
        else
          1

      theorem BHW.hwFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet_eq_det_ratio_of_fullFrame
          (d n : Nat)
          [NeZero d]
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hfull :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = d + 1)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (hι : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z ≠ 0) :
          BHW.HWFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet d n z w =
            BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι w /
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z := by
        classical
        unfold BHW.HWFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet
        have hnonempty :
            ∃ κ : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n,
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n κ z ≠ 0 := ⟨ι, hι⟩
        rw [dif_pos hnonempty]
        let κ := Classical.choose hnonempty
        have hκ : BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n κ z ≠ 0 :=
          Classical.choose_spec hnonempty
        exact
          (BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_ratio_eq_of_sameSourceGram_fullRank
            d n hfull hgram κ ι hκ hι).symm

      /-- Orientation compatibility for extending a high-rank same-Gram span
      isometry to the determinant-`1` complex Lorentz group.  This is
      automatic when the source span is a proper nondegenerate subspace and a
      complementary sign can be chosen; in full ambient rank it says exactly
      that the unique full-frame isometry has determinant `1`. -/
      def BHW.HWSameSourceGramSOOrientationCompatible
          (d n : Nat)
          (z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) : Prop :=
        BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1 ∨
        BHW.HWFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet d n z w = 1

      /-- The determinant-`1` Witt extension theorem in exactly the form
      consumed by the Hall-Wightman high-rank branch.  The ordinary Witt
      extension gives a full complex-orthogonal ambient isometry.  This
      strengthened form records the determinant correction: below full
      ambient rank, change the sign on a one-dimensional orthogonal
      complement if necessary; in full ambient rank, use the determinant
      equality stored in `HWSameSourceGramSOOrientationCompatible`. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_detOne_of_sourceSpan
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w)
          (hSO :
            BHW.HWSameSourceGramSOOrientationCompatible d n z w) :
          ∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ∀ i,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λ (z i) = w i

      /-- The same high-rank span isometry without the determinant-`1`
      condition.  It returns either the proper extension or the explicitly
      improper full-complex-orthogonal orbit.  This is the ordinary
      Hall-Wightman scalar-product alternative; theorem 2's active oriented
      branch consumes the proper side after proving `hSO`. -/
      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_or_improper_of_sourceSpan
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w) :
          (∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ∀ i,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λ (z i) = w i) ∨
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramImproperOrbitData d n z w

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowski_wittExtension_full_of_sourceSpan
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w) :
          ∃ A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ∀ i,
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A (z i) =
                w i

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_to_complexLorentzGroup_of_det_one
          [NeZero d]
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (hdet : BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A = 1) :
          ∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            ∀ v,
              BHW.complexLorentzVectorAction Λ v =
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A v

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_det_eq_frameMapDet_of_fullRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hfull :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) = d + 1)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (hA :
            ∀ i,
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A (z i) =
                w i) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A =
            BHW.HWFullRankSameGramFrameMapDet d n z w

      theorem BHW.sourceFullFrameDet_fullComplexLorentzAction
          [NeZero d]
          (A : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (n : Nat)
          (ι : Fin (d + 1) ↪ Fin n)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι
              (fun i =>
                BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A (z i)) =
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A *
              BHW.sourceFullFrameDet d n ι z

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_det_neg_reflection_fixing_sourceSpan
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w)
          (hproper :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1) :
          ∃ R : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d,
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet R = -1 ∧
            ∀ x : S.M,
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction R x = x

      theorem BHW.sourceSpan_orthogonalComplement_nontrivial_of_proper
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (S : BHW.HWHighRankSpanIsometryData d n z w)
          (hproper :
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) < d + 1) :
          Nontrivial
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d S.M)

      theorem BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank_eq_finrank_of_nondegenerate
          (d : Nat)
          {M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hM : BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.restrictedMinkowskiRank d M = Module.finrank ℂ M

      theorem BHW.exists_nonisotropic_in_nondegenerate_subspace
          [NeZero d]
          (M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          [Nontrivial M]
          (hM :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          ∃ u : M,
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≠ 0

      theorem BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule_nondegenerate
          [NeZero d]
          (M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (hM :
            BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d M) :
          BHW.ComplexMinkowskiNondegenerateSubspace d
            (BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)

      def BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≠ 0) :
          BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection_det
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≠ 0) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet
              (BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection u hu) = -1

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection_fix_subspace
          [NeZero d]
          {M : Submodule ℂ (Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (u : BHW.complexMinkowskiOrthogonalSubmodule d M)
          (hu :
            BHW.sourceComplexMinkowskiInner d
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
              (u : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≠ 0)
          (x : M) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction
              (BHW.fullComplexLorentzReflection u hu) x = x

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_det
          [NeZero d]
          (A B : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul A B) =
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet A *
              BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzDet B

      theorem BHW.fullComplexLorentz_mul_vectorAction
          [NeZero d]
          (A B : BHW.HallWightmanFullComplexLorentzGroup d)
          (v : Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzMul A B) v =
            BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction A
              (BHW.hallWightmanFullComplexLorentzVectorAction B v)

      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzOrbit : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (hSO :
            BHW.HWSameSourceGramSOOrientationCompatible d n z w) :
          ∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            w = BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z

      /-- Correct high scalar-rank orbit alternative with determinant
      orientation exposed.  In scalar rank exactly `d`, the determinant can be
      repaired on the one-dimensional orthogonal complement and the left
      alternative holds.  In scalar rank `d + 1`, the full-frame map may have
      determinant `-1`; then the right alternative records the improper
      full-complex-orthogonal orbit which must be discharged by the
      Hall-Wightman improper-component invariance gate. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_regular_orbit_or_improper
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzOrbit : BHW.HWSourceGramOrbitRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          (∃ Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d,
            w = BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) ∨
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramImproperOrbitData d n z w

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemma 2, singular scalar-rank case.  This is the
      residual-frame contraction normal form of the paper: after reducing by a
      complex Lorentz frame, the discrepancy lies in a totally isotropic
      subspace orthogonal to the source span, and a one-parameter complex
      Lorentz family contracts that subspace. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singular_contractionData
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hzLow : BHW.HWSourceGramLowRankAt d n z)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) :
          BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w

      /-- Lemma 1 / local repo support: the ordinary extended-tube
      continuation is holomorphic and invariant under complex Lorentz
      transformations.  The theorem-2-facing variant carries both forward-tube
      holomorphy and forward-tube complex Lorentz invariance; the extended
      invariance is not available from `hF_cinv` alone. -/
      theorem BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = F z)
          (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
          {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.extendF F (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
            BHW.extendF F z

      /-- Lemma 2, singular/low-rank case: equal source Gram data have the
      Hall-Wightman residual-frame normal form: after one complex Lorentz
      transform the discrepancy is contained in a totally isotropic subspace
      orthogonal to the source span, together with the one-parameter complex
      Lorentz contraction used in the paper.  The output is stated directly as
      value equality because the proof is a continuity/limit argument, not a
      single orbit equality. -/
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = F z)
          {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hgram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
          (hsingular :
            BHW.HWSameSourceGramSingularContractionData d n z w) :
          BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w
      ```

      Scratch-checked topology core for the singular-limit theorem.  The hard
      Hall-Wightman input is exactly `hsingular`; after that the proof is
      continuity of `extendF F` on `ExtendedTube d n`, complex Lorentz
      invariance on each orbit curve, and uniqueness of limits in `ℂ`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hw_sameSourceGram_singularLimit_extendF_eq ... := by
        have hExtend_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          (BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv).continuousOn
        have hleft_val :
            ∀ t, BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t) =
              BHW.extendF F z := by
          intro t
          rcases hsingular.curve_left_orbit t with ⟨Λ, hΛ⟩
          rw [hΛ]
          exact BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv Λ hz
        have hright_val :
            ∀ t, BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t) =
              BHW.extendF F w := by
          intro t
          rcases hsingular.curve_right_orbit t with ⟨Λ, hΛ⟩
          rw [hΛ]
          exact BHW.extendF_complexLorentzInvariant_of_cinv
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv Λ hw
        have hleft_lim :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ℝ => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_left t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          (hExtend_cont.continuousWithinAt hsingular.base_mem).tendsto.comp
            (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.mpr
              ⟨hsingular.curve_left_tendsto_base,
                Eventually.of_forall hsingular.curve_left_mem⟩)
        have hright_lim :
            Tendsto
              (fun t : ℝ => BHW.extendF F (hsingular.curve_right t))
              atTop (nhds (BHW.extendF F hsingular.base)) :=
          (hExtend_cont.continuousWithinAt hsingular.base_mem).tendsto.comp
            (tendsto_nhdsWithin_iff.mpr
              ⟨hsingular.curve_right_tendsto_base,
                Eventually.of_forall hsingular.curve_right_mem⟩)
        have hz_lim :
            Tendsto (fun _ : ℝ => BHW.extendF F z)
