      `min d n` and not the later `SourceComplexGramRegularAt` predicate.  This
      keeps the Hall-Wightman PDE-span lemma separate from both the Lemma-2
      orbit alternative and the regular-source-map chart machinery.

      Lean-shaped proof of
      `BHW.hallWightman_selectedScalarFunction_to_fullGramChart`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_selectedScalarFunction_to_fullGramChart
          ... := by
        -- The theorem hypotheses include
        -- `hprod : C.Ucoord = Set.prod Us Ua` and
        -- `hUs_open : IsOpen Us`.
        let p0 := (C.coord ⟨z0, C.z0_mem⟩).1
        have hp0 : p0 ∈ C.Ucoord :=
          (C.coord ⟨z0, C.z0_mem⟩).2
        have hp0_prod : p0 ∈ Set.prod Us Ua := by
          simpa [p0, hprod] using hp0
        have hp0_Us : p0.1 ∈ Us := by
          exact hp0_prod.1
        let U0 : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) :=
          C.U0 ∩ {Z | C.scalarCoord Z ∈ Us}
        let Ψ : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
          fun Z => Ψs (C.scalarCoord Z)
        have hZ0U0 :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 ∈ U0 := by
          exact ⟨C.gram_z0_mem, by
            simpa [p0, C.sourceGram_selected ⟨z0, C.z0_mem⟩] using
              hp0_Us⟩
        have hU0_open : IsOpen U0 :=
          C.U0_open.inter
            (hUs_open.preimage C.scalarCoord_diff.continuous)
        have hΨ_diff : DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 := by
          have hscalar_into :
              C.scalarCoord '' U0 ⊆ Us := by
            intro u hu
            rcases hu with ⟨Z, hZU0, rfl⟩
            exact hZU0.2
          exact hΨs_diff.comp
            C.scalarCoord_diff.differentiableOn hscalar_into
        exact ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZ0U0, Set.inter_subset_left,
          hΨ_diff, by
            intro Z hZ
            rfl⟩
      ```

      The intermediate `hp0_prod` is necessary in Lean: before rewriting
      `C.Ucoord` by `hprod`, a proof `hp0 : p0 ∈ C.Ucoord` is not a product
      membership proof and has no `.1` projection.  The same pattern should be
      used anywhere a membership proof in `C.Ucoord` is projected into scalar
      and auxiliary coordinates.

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeriesChart_branch_eq_of_sameGram
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Uvec : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          {U0 O : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          {Ψ : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hΨ_branch_vec :
            ∀ z, z ∈ Uvec ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈ U0 ->
                Ψ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) =
                  BHW.extendF F z)
          (hUvec_sub : Uvec ⊆ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hO_sub_image :
            O ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Uvec)
          {w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hwU :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈
              (U0 ∩ O) ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) :
          Ψ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w

      Lean-shaped proof of the branch-law transport helper:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_powerSeriesChart_branch_eq_of_sameGram
          ... := by
        rcases hO_sub_image ⟨hwU.1.2, hwU.2⟩ with
          ⟨z, hzUvec, hzGram⟩
        have hzET : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := hUvec_sub hzUvec
        have hzU0 :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈ U0 := by
          simpa [hzGram] using hwU.1.1
        calc
          Ψ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w)
              = Ψ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z) := by
                  rw [hzGram]
          _ = BHW.extendF F z :=
                  hΨ_branch_vec z hzUvec hzU0
          _ = BHW.extendF F w :=
                  hBranch hzET hw hzGram
      ```

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 5--7, source-coordinate form: at a
      non-exceptional scalar point there are `sourceGramExpectedDim d n`
      functionally independent scalar products, and the invariant extended-tube
      branch is a convergent power series in those scalar products. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ}
          (hZU : Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z) :
          ∃ U0 Ψ,
            IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
            DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
            U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ∧
            (∀ w, w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈ U0 ->
                Ψ (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                  BHW.extendF F w)

      /-- Branch-defined scalar value on the ordinary source Gram image,
      stated on the subtype carrying the proof of membership in `S'_n`.
      This is the mathematically honest object: it chooses an ordinary
      extended-tube realization of the given scalar point and evaluates the
      single-valued BHW continuation.  The branch law is the separate theorem
      proving this definition does not depend on the chosen realization. -/
      noncomputable def BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn
          (d n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (Z : {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ //
            Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n}) : ℂ :=
        BHW.extendF F (Classical.choose Z.2)

      theorem BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq
          [NeZero d]
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w)
          {w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ}
          (hw : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
          BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn d n F
              ⟨BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w, ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩⟩ =
            BHW.extendF F w

      theorem BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq ... := by
        classical
        have hZ :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩
        have hchoose_mem :
            Classical.choose hZ ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hZ).1
        have hchoose_gram :
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Classical.choose hZ) =
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w :=
          (Classical.choose_spec hZ).2
        change BHW.extendF F (Classical.choose hZ) =
          BHW.extendF F w
        exact hBranch hchoose_mem hw hchoose_gram

      /-- Hall-Wightman Lemmas 1--7 before the final removability step:
      the scalar germ defined by the branch law is single-valued, continuous,
      and locally bounded on `S'_n`. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          (F : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
          (hF_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ F (BHW.ForwardTube d n))
          (hF_cinv :
            ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              F (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = F z)
          (hBranch :
            ∀ {z w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ},
              z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z =
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ->
              BHW.extendF F z = BHW.extendF F w) :
          ∃ phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
            (∀ w, w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) = BHW.extendF F w) ∧
            ContinuousOn phi (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) ∧
            BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)

      Lean-shaped proof of continuity and local boundedness.  This proof must
      not call `hallWightman_localScalarChart_at`, because the exceptional
      chart theorem consumes continuity/local boundedness.  Instead it uses
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 directly: every sufficiently near scalar-variety
      point has a realization in an arbitrarily small ordinary extended-tube
      neighborhood of the chosen realization.  The initial subtype value and
      branch-equality proof have been scratch-checked against
      `SourceExtension.lean`: `Classical.choose hZ` has fields
      `(Classical.choose_spec hZ).1 : _ ∈ ExtendedTube d n` and
      `(Classical.choose_spec hZ).2 : sourceMinkowskiGram d n _ = Z`, so the
      branch law rewrites the chosen representative to any other realization.
      The exported proof-local helper
      `BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq` packages exactly this
      well-definedness calculation; it is not a replacement for the branch
      law, because it consumes `hBranch`.  The total function `phi` below is a
      theorem-local totalization needed only because the existing continuity
      and germ APIs take total functions on the ambient scalar coordinate
      space.  Its `else 0` branch is never mathematical content and must not
      be exported as a production scalar representative.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded ... := by
        classical
        -- Totalize the subtype-defined scalar value only for this ambient
        -- function API.  All later uses are restricted to `S'_n`.
        let phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
          fun Z =>
            if hZ : Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n then
              BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn d n F ⟨Z, hZ⟩
            else 0
        have hphi_branch :
            ∀ w, w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ->
              phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w) =
                BHW.extendF F w := by
          intro w hw
          have hZ :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩
          simpa [phi, hZ] using
            BHW.sourceScalarValueOfExtendFOn_branch_eq
              (d := d) n F hBranch hw
        have hU_sub :
            BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ⊆
              BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_subset_sourceComplexGramVariety
            (d := d) (n := n)
        have hExt_holo :
            DifferentiableOn ℂ (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv
        have hExt_cont :
            ContinuousOn (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          hExt_holo.continuousOn
        have hcont :
            ContinuousOn phi (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.continuousOn_of_open_nhdsWithin_control <| by
            intro Z hZU T hT_open hphiZT
            rcases hZU with ⟨z0, hz0, rfl⟩
            have hz0_value :
                phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) =
                  BHW.extendF F z0 :=
              hphi_branch z0 hz0
            have hExt_in_T : BHW.extendF F z0 ∈ T := by
              simpa [hz0_value] using hphiZT
            rcases BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative
                (d := d) hd n hz0 with
              ⟨ζ0, hζ0, hζ0Gram, hζ0span⟩
            have hζ0_value :
                phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ζ0) =
                  BHW.extendF F ζ0 :=
              hphi_branch ζ0 hζ0
            have hExtζ_in_T : BHW.extendF F ζ0 ∈ T := by
              have hsame :
                  phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ζ0) =
                    phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) := by
                rw [hζ0Gram]
              simpa [hζ0_value, hz0_value] using
                hsame.symm ▸ hphiZT
            rcases
              BHW.continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds
                hExt_cont hζ0 hT_open hExtζ_in_T with
              ⟨Vsrc, hVsrc_open, hζ0Vsrc, hVsrc_sub_T⟩
            let Vtube := Vsrc ∩ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
            have hVtube_open : IsOpen Vtube :=
              hVsrc_open.inter (BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n))
            have hζ0Vtube : ζ0 ∈ Vtube := ⟨hζ0Vsrc, hζ0⟩
            have hVtube_sub : Vtube ⊆ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := Set.inter_subset_right
            let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ζ0)
            have hspan :
                Module.finrank ℂ
                  (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ζ0)) =
                r := by
              simpa [r, hζ0Gram] using hζ0span
            rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
                (d := d) hd n hζ0 hspan
                hVtube_open hζ0Vtube hVtube_sub with
              ⟨O, hO_open, hZO, hO_image⟩
            exact ⟨O, hO_open, by simpa [hζ0Gram] using hZO, by
              intro G hGO hGdomain
              have hGvar : G ∈ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
                hU_sub hGdomain
              rcases hO_image ⟨hGO, hGvar⟩ with
                ⟨z, hzVtube, hGz⟩
              have hzET : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := hzVtube.2
              have hphiG : phi G = BHW.extendF F z := by
                simpa [hGz] using hphi_branch z hzET
              simpa [hphiG] using hVsrc_sub_T z hzET hzVtube.1⟩
        have hbdd :
            BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) := by
          intro Z hZU
          rcases hZU with ⟨z0, hz0, rfl⟩
          let C : ℝ := ‖BHW.extendF F z0‖ + 1
          have hC_nonneg : 0 <= C := by positivity
          have hC_pos : ‖BHW.extendF F z0‖ < C := by linarith
          have hNorm_open : IsOpen {c : ℂ | ‖c‖ < C} :=
            isOpen_lt continuous_norm continuous_const
          rcases BHW.hwLemma3_extendedTube_adaptedRankRepresentative
              (d := d) hd n hz0 with
            ⟨ζ0, hζ0, hζ0Gram, hζ0span⟩
          have hζ_bound : ‖BHW.extendF F ζ0‖ < C := by
            have hsame_value :
                BHW.extendF F ζ0 = BHW.extendF F z0 := by
              exact hBranch hζ0 hz0 hζ0Gram
            simpa [hsame_value] using hC_pos
          rcases
            BHW.continuousOn_openDomain_preimage_nhds
              hExt_cont hζ0 hNorm_open hζ_bound with
            ⟨Vsrc, hVsrc_open, hζ0Vsrc, hVsrc_bound⟩
          let Vtube := Vsrc ∩ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
          have hVtube_open : IsOpen Vtube :=
            hVsrc_open.inter (BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n))
          have hζ0Vtube : ζ0 ∈ Vtube := ⟨hζ0Vsrc, hζ0⟩
          have hVtube_sub : Vtube ⊆ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := Set.inter_subset_right
          let r := BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
            (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n ζ0)
          have hspan :
              Module.finrank ℂ
                (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n ζ0)) =
              r := by
            simpa [r, hζ0Gram] using hζ0span
          rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
              (d := d) hd n hζ0 hspan
              hVtube_open hζ0Vtube hVtube_sub with
            ⟨O, hO_open, hZO, hO_image⟩
          exact ⟨O, hO_open, by simpa [hζ0Gram] using hZO, C,
            hC_nonneg, by
              intro G hG
              have hGvar : G ∈ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
                hU_sub hG.2
              rcases hO_image ⟨hG.1, hGvar⟩ with
                ⟨z, hzVtube, hGz⟩
              have hzET : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := hzVtube.2
              have hphiG : phi G = BHW.extendF F z := by
                simpa [hGz] using hphi_branch z hzET
              exact le_of_lt (by
                simpa [hphiG] using hVsrc_bound z hzET hzVtube.1)⟩
        exact ⟨phi, hphi_branch, hcont, hbdd⟩
      ```

      /-- Determinantal description of the exceptional locus in the
      scalar-product variety: locally it is the analytic subset cut out by the
      maximal scalar minors.  This is the input needed by the removable
      singularity theorem. -/
      theorem BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
            (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
            {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z}

      /-- Normal analytic-space removable theorem in the exact form consumed
      by the Hall-Wightman exceptional-rank chart.  The input gives local
      ambient representatives on the maximal-rank locus of a relatively open
      source-variety domain and continuity/local boundedness on the full
      domain.  Since the exceptional locus is an analytic subvariety of the
      normal source Gram variety, the representatives extend across it as
      analytic-space germs. -/
      theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hU_rel : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U)
          (hphi_cont : ContinuousOn phi U)
          (hphi_bdd : BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi U)
          (hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z})
          (hMaxCharts :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ U ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)) :
          ∀ Z, Z ∈ U ->
            ∃ U0 Ψ,
              IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
              DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
              U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U ∧
              Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)

      /-- Hall-Wightman's final removable-singularity step: a scalar germ that
      is holomorphic on the non-exceptional part of a scalar neighborhood and
      continuous/locally bounded on the whole neighborhood extends to an
      ambient holomorphic representative at an exceptional scalar point. -/
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (n : Nat)
          {phi : (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
          (hphi_cont :
            ContinuousOn phi (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n))
          (hphi_bdd :
            BHW.LocallyBoundedOn phi
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n))
          (hExceptionalAnalytic :
            BHW.IsAnalyticSubvarietyIn
              (BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
              {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z})
          (hMaxCharts :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n))
          {Z : Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ}
          (hZU : Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n)
          (hRank : BHW.HWSourceGramExceptionalRank d n Z) :
          ∃ U0 Ψ,
            IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
            DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
            U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆
              BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ∧
            Set.EqOn phi Ψ
              (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof of the exceptional removable chart:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at ... := by
        have hU_rel :
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
              (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
          BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen (d := d) hd n
        exact
          BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank
            (d := d) hd n hU_rel hphi_cont hphi_bdd
            hExceptionalAnalytic hMaxCharts Z hZU
      ```

      Proof transcript for
      `BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank`: this theorem must
      be a direct consumer of the named normal analytic-space Riemann theorem
      `BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension`, not a second
      removability black box.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourceGramVariety_removableAlongExceptionalRank ... := by
        have hregular :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ U ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro Z hZU hMax
          rcases hMaxCharts Z hZU hMax with
            ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, hEq⟩
          exact ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, hEq⟩
        have hMaxDense :
            U ⊆ closure
              (U ∩ {Z | BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z}) :=
          BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_relOpen_subset_closure_inter_maxRank
            (d := d) (n := n) hU_rel
        have hall :=
          BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension
            (d := d) hd n hU_rel hphi_cont hphi_bdd
            hExceptionalAnalytic hMaxDense hregular
        intro Z hZU
        exact hall Z hZU
      ```

      The mathematical input behind
      `BHW.sourceGramVariety_normal_riemannExtension` is exactly the normal
      analytic-space Riemann extension theorem recorded earlier in this
      section.  By `BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety_normal`, each relatively open
      piece of the source Gram variety is normal.  The exceptional set is the
      analytic subvariety cut out by maximal scalar minors via
      `BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety`; its complement is
      the maximal-rank locus.  On that complement the `hMaxCharts` fields give
      a structure-sheaf section represented by ambient holomorphic functions,
      and chart compatibility is equality on the source variety.  Continuity
      and local boundedness of `phi` on all of `U` provide the boundedness
      hypothesis for the normal-space extension theorem.  The normal Riemann
      theorem itself uses the relative-open witness for `U` to choose the
      returned neighborhood inside the source domain, so the subset field
      `U0 ∩ sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆ U` is part of the theorem output
      rather than a later shrink hidden in this removable wrapper.

      The max-rank chart theorem is the direct formalization of
      Hall-Wightman Lemma 5 together with Lemmas 6--7: choose a realization
      `z0 ∈ ExtendedTube` of `Z`; maximal scalar rank gives the required
      number `sourceGramExpectedDim d n` of functionally independent scalar
      products; the implicit-function theorem expresses the remaining vector
      variables analytically in terms of those scalar products and auxiliary
      variables; invariance kills the auxiliary derivatives, so the branch is
      a convergent power series in scalar products alone.  The result is the
      ambient scalar chart `Ψ`.

      Lean-shaped proof of `BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at`:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at ... := by
        rcases hZU with ⟨z0, hz0, rfl⟩
        have hz0Rank : BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt d n z0 := by
          simpa [BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRankAt] using hRank
        have hExt_holo :
            DifferentiableOn ℂ (BHW.extendF F) (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
          BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n F hF_holo hF_cinv
        have hSpan :
            BHW.SourceScalarDifferentialsSpanInvariantPDE d n z0 :=
          BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_scalarDifferentials_span_PDE
            (d := d) hd n hz0Rank
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_powerSeries_from_PDE_span
            (d := d) hd n F hz0 hz0Rank hF_holo hF_cinv hExt_holo hSpan with
          ⟨Uvec, U0, Ψ,
            hUvec_open, hz0Uvec, hUvec_sub,
            hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hΨ_branch_vec⟩
        -- Hall-Wightman Lemma 3 shrinks the scalar chart so every nearby
        -- scalar-variety point has an extended-tube realization inside `Uvec`.
        have hspan_adapted :
            Module.finrank ℂ
              (LinearMap.range (BHW.sourceCoefficientEval d n z0)) =
            BHW.sourceGramMatrixRank n
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) :=
          BHW.hwSourceGramMaxRankAt_span_finrank_eq_sourceGramMatrixRank
            (d := d) hd n hz0Rank
        rcases BHW.hwLemma3_adapted_sourceGram_localVectorRealization
            (d := d) hd n hz0 hspan_adapted
            hUvec_open hz0Uvec hUvec_sub with
          ⟨O, hO_open, hZO, hO_sub_image⟩
        let Uscalar := U0 ∩ O
        exact ⟨Uscalar, Ψ, hU0_open.inter hO_open,
          ⟨hZU0, hZO⟩, hΨ_diff.mono (Set.inter_subset_left),
          by
            intro G hG
            rcases hO_sub_image ⟨hG.1.2, hG.2⟩ with
              ⟨z, hzUvec, rfl⟩
            exact ⟨z, hUvec_sub hzUvec, rfl⟩,
          by
            intro w hw hwUscalar
            have hwU_helper :
                BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈
                  (U0 ∩ O) ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
              ⟨hwUscalar, ⟨w, rfl⟩⟩
            exact BHW.hallWightman_powerSeriesChart_branch_eq_of_sameGram
              (d := d) hd n F hBranch hΨ_branch_vec hUvec_sub hO_sub_image
              hw hwU_helper⟩
      ```

      The final helper in the last line is not a new source theorem: it is the
      branch-law transport step.  Given `sourceMinkowskiGram w ∈ O`, Lemma 3
      returns `z ∈ Uvec` with the same Gram; `hΨ_branch_vec` identifies
      `Ψ (sourceMinkowskiGram z)` with `extendF F z`, and `hBranch` identifies
      `extendF F z` with `extendF F w`.

      The exceptional theorem is the final paragraph of Hall-Wightman's proof.
      First use the same-source-Gram branch law to define the scalar germ
      `phi` on `S'_n`.  Lemmas 1--7 give its single-valuedness, continuity, and
      local boundedness.  On the complement of the exceptional determinantal
      locus, `hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at` supplies ambient
      holomorphic representatives.  Since the exceptional locus is locally an
      analytic subvariety of the source Gram variety, the removable
      singularity theorem extends the scalar representative through `Z`.
      This is the precise mathematical content of
      `hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at`.

      The compatible germ-atlas theorem must remember which local chart was
      obtained at each scalar point and must prove its `overlap_eq` field by
      the Hall-Wightman power-series/removable-germ uniqueness argument.  Do
      not replace that field by a generic predicate saying that arbitrary
      ambient holomorphic extensions agree when they agree on the
      rank-bounded Gram variety; that statement is false in general and would
      be assumption-smuggling.

      Lean-shaped proof of the chart split after these subfacts:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        exact BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
          (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hZU hRank

      theorem BHW.hallWightman_exceptionalRank_localScalarChart_at ... := by
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_scalarGerm_continuous_locallyBounded
            (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch with
          ⟨phi, hphi_branch, hphi_cont, hphi_bdd⟩
        have hMaxCharts :
            ∀ Z, Z ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ->
              BHW.HWSourceGramMaxRank d n Z ->
              ∃ U0 Ψ,
                IsOpen U0 ∧ Z ∈ U0 ∧
                DifferentiableOn ℂ Ψ U0 ∧
                U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n ⊆
                  BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n ∧
                Set.EqOn phi Ψ
                  (U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n) := by
          intro W hWU hWmax
          rcases BHW.hallWightman_maxRank_powerSeriesChart_at
              (d := d) hd n F hF_holo hF_cinv hBranch hWU hWmax with
            ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hWU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, hΨ_branch⟩
          exact ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hWU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, by
            intro G hG
            have hGdomain : G ∈ BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n :=
              hU0_sub hG
            rcases hGdomain with ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩
            exact (hphi_branch w hw).trans (hΨ_branch w hw hG.1).symm⟩
        rcases BHW.hallWightman_removableScalarChart_at
            (d := d) hd n hphi_cont hphi_bdd
            (BHW.hwSourceGramExceptionalRank_isAnalyticSubvariety d n)
            hMaxCharts hZU hRank with
          ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, hphi_eq⟩
        exact ⟨U0, Ψ, hU0_open, hZU0, hΨ_diff, hU0_sub, by
          intro w hw hwU0
          have hG : BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n w ∈
              U0 ∩ BHW.sourceComplexGramVariety d n :=
            ⟨hwU0, ⟨w, rfl⟩⟩
          exact (hphi_eq hG).symm.trans (hphi_branch w hw)⟩
      ```

      Do not add a separate production descent-core forwarding theorem merely
      to package the local facts into the descent theorem.  The active Lean
      target is the direct
      proof of `sourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn_extendF_descent` above, where
      the branch-defined `Phi`, max-rank charts, and exceptional removable
      chart are in the same proof term.  If the implementation uses
      `HallWightmanScalarGermAtlas` internally for organization, the atlas
      construction should be inlined or kept private; it is not an additional
      public theorem surface on the route.

      This is the mathematically complete Hall-Wightman output needed by the
      corrected source representative.  Hall-Wightman Lemmas 4--7 supply the
      local power-series/implicit-function expansions in scalar products; the
      overlap compatibility comes from the same branch law above, and the
      singular points use the bounded/continuous extension and removable
      singularity step in the proof of Theorem I.  The proof never promotes
      equality on the scalar variety to equality on a full ambient open set.

      Once this local source predicate is available on every point of
      `closure E`, the compact common-germ theorem is a finite gluing theorem:

      The two source-supplier lemmas used by the adjacent OS45 packet are
      identity-route lemmas, not a general arbitrary-`ρ` theorem.  The
      Figure-2-4 selector below only constructs the active identity OS45
      common edge and the adjacent swap branch over that same edge.  A later
      generic common-boundary theorem may consume explicit source-germ
      hypotheses for any branch label, but the Figure-2-4 supplier itself must
      stay at the exact active surface:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hVcl : closure V ⊆ Ufig) :
          ∀ x ∈ closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)

      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_swap_ordered_closure :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ)
          (hV_figPath_closure :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              let y :=
                BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
              ∃ (Γ Δ : unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
                Continuous Γ ∧
                Continuous Δ ∧
                Γ (0 : unitInterval) = (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∧
                Γ (1 : unitInterval) = Q.symm (BHW.realEmbed y) ∧
                (∀ t, Γ t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
                (∀ t, Δ t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
                (∀ t,
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Δ t) =
                    BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (Γ t))))
          (hVcl : closure V ⊆ Ufig) :
          ∀ x ∈ closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩)
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)
      ```

      Both displayed suppliers should factor through the same one-branch
      source theorem.  This theorem is the exact OS I §4.5/BHW input needed
      upstream of EOW:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_oneBranch_sourcePatch
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (U : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hU_open : IsOpen U)
          (hU_ordered :
            ∀ x ∈ U,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hU_horiz_bhw :
            ∀ x ∈ U,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0U : x0 ∈ U)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n β
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0)) :
          BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
            (d := d) OS lgc n β
            (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) β x0)
      ```

      Implementation restriction: this arbitrary-`β` theorem is only an
      assembly target because it consumes `hScalar`.  The scalar packet is the
      real mathematical source input.  The current theorem-2 route needs only
      two public scalar-packet suppliers: `β = 1` and
      `β = Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩`.  If the generic theorem is
      implemented before the full arbitrary-permutation Hall-Wightman source
      theorem, it must remain this private-style assembly lemma consuming the
      explicit scalar source packet returned by the identity or adjacent scalar
      source suppliers, not a theorem claiming that `hU_open`, `hU_ordered`,
      and `hU_horiz_bhw` alone prove arbitrary permutation source
      compatibility.  Jost and ordinary extended-tube fields live upstream in
      the scalar-packet suppliers; once `hScalar` is present, this assembly
      step does not need to carry them.

      Mathematical content of this one-branch theorem:

      1. Put `Q := os45QuarterTurnCLE` and
         `Wβ z := permAct β.symm (Q.symm z)`.
      2. Use `hU_ordered x0 hx0U` and the checked
         `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered` to put the
         horizontal base point in `os45ACRBranchDomain β`.  Use
         `hU_horiz_bhw x0 hx0U` to put the same base point in
         `os45PulledRealBranchDomain β`.  Both are open domains, so shrink to
         a chart neighborhood contained in their intersection.  On that
         neighborhood, `Q.symm z ∈ ForwardTube` and `Wβ z ∈ ExtendedTube`.
      3. On this neighborhood the BHW-side representative is definitionally
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z)`.
      4. The ACR-side equality

         ```lean
         extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z) =
           bvt_F OS lgc n (Wβ z)
         ```

         on `os45ACRBranchDomain β` is the genuine one-branch BHW
         source/edge theorem: the OS-II ACR branch and the Hall-Wightman BHW
         branch are two continuations of the same forward-tube germ through the
         local Streater-Wightman Figure-2-4 real environment.  It may use the
         one-branch scalar-product representative and local source uniqueness
         for the branch `β`, but it may not use adjacent branch equality,
         `AdjacentOSEOWDifferenceEnvelope`, final `bvt_W` locality, or global
         PET branch independence.
      5. Package the branch-specific neighborhood and the two agreement fields
         as `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`.

      The identity supplier applies this theorem not to bare `Ufig`, but to
      the ordered open patch

      ```lean
      Uid := Ufig ∩
        EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
      ```

      with `β = 1` and `x0 = x`.  For a closure point
      `x ∈ closure V`, this is an open neighborhood of `x`; the required
      membership in `Uid` is obtained from `hVcl` and
      `hV_ordered_closure`.  Apply
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id` with `V := Uid`: the Jost,
      ordinary extended-tube, and identity horizontal pulled-domain hypotheses
      are the corresponding fields of `hUfig_source` restricted along
      `Uid ⊆ Ufig`.  Then apply the generic one-branch source-patch assembly
      with the same `Uid` and the resulting scalar packet.  No extra shrink is
      needed at this layer unless a later compactness consumer explicitly asks
      for one; the branch-BV compact shrink is a separate downstream step.

      For the adjacent supplier, do not try to use the original `V` as the
      scalar source patch for a boundary point `x ∈ closure V`.  First choose a
      local open patch around that closure point in the original identity
      coordinates:

      ```lean
      Ux := Ufig ∩
        EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) ∩
        {u | (fun k => u (τ k)) ∈
          EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ} ∩
        {u | BHW.realEmbed
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) u) ∈
          BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ}
      ```

      The point `x` lies in `Ux` by `hVcl`, `hV_ordered_closure`,
      `hV_swap_ordered_closure`, and the closure-level adjacent pulled-domain
      field supplied by the source patch selector.  Openness comes from
      `hUfig_open`, ordered-sector openness, continuity of
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint`/`realEmbed`, and
      `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.  The pointwise
      `hfig_x0` input for the adjacent scalar source theorem is exactly
      `hV_figPath_closure x hx`.

      The adjacent scalar theorem is applied on `Ux` and returns a scalar packet
      at the identity common edge

      ```lean
      τ := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
      y0 := BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
        (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
      hScalar_y0 :
        BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket (d := d) OS lgc n τ y0
      ```

      The one-branch source-patch assembly theorem is not applied to `Ux`
      directly, because its ordered input for branch `τ` is an ordered
      `τ`-configuration.  Apply it to the relabelled patch

      ```lean
      Uτ := {u | (fun k => u (τ k)) ∈ Ux}
      ```

      The relabelled patch transport is part of the theorem, not an informal
      convention.  This preimage formulation is the Lean-facing definition; it
      is equivalent to the image formulation
      `{u | ∃ v ∈ Ux, u = fun k => v (τ k)}` because `τ` is an adjacent swap.
      For the base point,

      ```lean
      let xτ : NPointDomain d n := fun k => x (τ k)
      have hxτUτ : xτ ∈ Uτ := by
        change (fun k => xτ (τ k)) ∈ Ux
        simpa [xτ, τ] using hxUx
      ```

      For a general `u ∈ Uτ`, put `v := fun k => u (τ k)`.  Then
      `hvUx : v ∈ Ux` is exactly `hu`, and

      ```lean
      have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
        ext k μ
        simp [v, τ]
      ```

      The transported source-patch fields are then:

      - openness of `Uτ`, by continuity of the finite permutation map
        `u ↦ fun k => u (τ k)` and the openness of `Ux`;
      - Jost membership, from the `Ufig` Jost field applied to `v` and
        `BHW.jostSet_permutation_invariant`;
      - branch-`τ` ordered-sector membership, exactly the swapped ordered field
        in the definition of `Ux`;
      - ordinary extended-tube membership of `u`, from the adjacent ET field of
        `hUfig_source` applied to `v`;
      - horizontal pulled-BHW membership, by rewriting `u` with `hu_eq` and then
        rewriting `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint τ (v ∘ τ)` to
        `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 v` using
        `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`, then applying the
        adjacent horizontal field of `hUfig_source` to `v`.

      The Lean-facing transport transcript is:

      ```lean
      have hperm_cont :
          Continuous (fun u : NPointDomain d n => fun k => u (τ k)) := by
        exact continuous_pi (fun k =>
          continuous_pi (fun μ =>
            (continuous_apply μ).comp (continuous_apply (τ k))))

      have hUτ_open : IsOpen Uτ := by
        simpa [Uτ] using hUx_open.preimage hperm_cont

      have hUτ_jost : ∀ u ∈ Uτ, u ∈ BHW.JostSet d n := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hvJ : v ∈ BHW.JostSet d n := hUx_jost v hvUx
        have hτJ : (fun k => v (τ k)) ∈ BHW.JostSet d n :=
          BHW.jostSet_permutation_invariant (d := d) (n := n) τ hvJ
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hτJ

      have hUτ_ordered :
          ∀ u ∈ Uτ,
            u ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hv_ordered :
            (fun k => v (τ k)) ∈
              EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ :=
          hUx_swap_ordered v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hv_ordered

      have hUτ_ET :
          ∀ u ∈ Uτ, BHW.realEmbed u ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hvET :
            BHW.realEmbed (fun k => v (τ k)) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
          hUx_swap_ET v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        simpa [hu_eq] using hvET

      have hUτ_horiz :
          ∀ u ∈ Uτ,
            BHW.realEmbed
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ u) ∈
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ := by
        intro u hu
        let v : NPointDomain d n := fun k => u (τ k)
        have hvUx : v ∈ Ux := by simpa [Uτ, v] using hu
        have hbase := hUx_horiz v hvUx
        have hu_eq : u = fun k => v (τ k) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [v, τ]
        have hedge :
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ u =
              BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) v := by
          rw [hu_eq]
          simpa [τ] using
            BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
              (d := d) (n := n) i hi (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) v
        simpa [hedge] using hbase
      ```

      The basepoint rewrite must be explicit.  Put

      ```lean
      have hedge_eq :
        BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ xτ = y0 := by
        simpa [τ, xτ, y0] using
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
            (d := d) (n := n) i hi (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x

      have hScalar_relabelled :
        BHW.OS45OneBranchScalarGramEqPacket
          (d := d) OS lgc n τ
          (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ xτ) := by
        simpa [hedge_eq] using hScalar_y0
      ```

      Applying the one-branch theorem with `β = τ`, `U = Uτ`, and `x0 = xτ`
      consumes `hScalar_relabelled` and yields the adjacent source germ at
      `os45CommonEdgeRealPoint τ xτ`.  The final displayed supplier rewrites
      this predicate back to `y0` by the same `hedge_eq`.  This is only
      basepoint transport in the source-germ predicate; it is not equality
      between the identity and adjacent branch values.

      Their proof is not an extra route, but it also must not silently promote
      domain geometry into branch-value equality.  The fields of
      `hUfig_source` say only that the selected Figure-2-4 patch is Jost, that
      the identity and adjacent real configurations lie in the ordinary
      extended tube, and that the two OS45 horizontal chart points lie in the
      corresponding pulled BHW domains.  These fields are enough to choose
      neighborhoods and to evaluate the two formulas.  They do **not** prove
      that the formulas agree.

      For the identity supplier, after `β = 1`, the branch-specific pulled BHW
      argument is definitionally `Q.symm z`.  For the adjacent supplier, the
      proof must work on the relabelled ordered configuration `x ∘ τ`, apply
      the same one-branch OS I §4.5 / BHW source construction there with the
      pointwise Figure-2-4 path supplied by `hV_figPath_closure x hx`, and
      then transport the result back to the identity common edge by
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq` and
      `BHW.os45QuarterTurnConfig_reindexed_realBranch_eq`.  It must not prove
      or assume equality between the identity and adjacent BHW branches.

      Lean implementation rule: `os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent`
      may call only the branch-local OS I §4.5/BHW source construction for the
      relabelled one-branch packet, plus the geometric reindexing identities.
      If a proof calls the downstream
      `BHW.os45AdjacentScalarGerm_of_OSII_Figure24`, the downstream
      `BHW.bvt_F_adjacent_extendF_edgeDistribution_eq_of_osEOWDifferenceEnvelope`,
      `hallWightman_source_permutedBranch_compatibility_of_distributionalAnchor`,
      `BHW.permutedExtendedTube_singleValued_of_forwardTube_symmetry`, or a
      corollary of them, it has skipped Slot 1 and is off the OS-II route.

      Fork compatibility guard for the whole branch-local source block.  The
      theorem surfaces
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_oneBranch_sourcePatch`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_sourcePatch`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_sourceInput`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_id`,
      `BHW.os45OneBranchScalarGramEq_sourceInput_adjacent`,
      `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalSourceEq_of_BHWJost`, and
      `BHW.os45SPrime_figure24LocalBranchCompatibility_of_BHWJost`
      are pure-Gram theorem surfaces.  They may consume
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData` only after the pure-Gram source gate
      has been closed by a proved or explicitly sourced
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F`, including the missing
      full-component/improper Hall-Wightman input.  Since the strict OS §4.5
      source decision is now closed on the proper-complex/oriented
      representative, none of these pure-Gram names may be implemented as
      compatibility wrappers around the oriented theorem.  The whole block
      must be restated with the oriented source API before Lean:

      The oriented packet shape is the earlier
      `BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket` display in the
      source-decision guard; do not introduce a second incompatible structure
      with the same name.  Its branch-value consumer has the same role as the
      pure-Gram `os45OneBranchACRBHWAgreement_of_scalarGramEq`, but with the
      oriented invariant in every scalar slot:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedSourceEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n β y0) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let Wβ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm (Q.symm z)
          ∃ Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            IsOpen Uy ∧
            BHW.realEmbed y0 ∈ Uy ∧
            Uy ⊆ BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β ∩
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β ∧
            Set.EqOn
              (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z))
              (fun z =>
                BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n β z)
              Uy
      ```

      The oriented consumer is Lean-ready only after the following algebraic
      reduction is made explicit.  The branch label in the pulled BHW
      representative is `β.symm`, so the oriented invariant of the pulled
      point is rewritten by
      `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
      (d := d) (n := n) β.symm`; the scalar
      equality packet is then applied to the unpermuted quarter-turn
      invariant.  Reversing `β` and `β.symm`, or putting
      `sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (Wβ z)` directly into the packet
      field, would double-count the branch permutation.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedScalarEq
          [NeZero d]
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
          (hUy_acr :
            Uy ⊆ BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hUy_bhw :
            Uy ⊆ BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β)
          (hOrient :
            let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
            ∀ z, z ∈ Uy ->
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n β.symm
                  (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                    (Q.symm z))) =
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (Q.symm z))) :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          let Wβ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm (Q.symm z)
          Set.EqOn
            (fun z => BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z))
            (fun z =>
              BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n β z)
            Uy
      ```

      Proof transcript for the oriented reduction:

      1. For `z ∈ Uy`, put `u := Q.symm z` and
         `w := Wβ z = BHW.permAct β.symm u`.
         From `hUy_acr hz`, unfolded through `os45ACRBranchDomain`, obtain
         `hu_ft : u ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n`.  From `hUy_bhw hz`, unfolded
         through `os45PulledRealBranchDomain`, obtain
         `hw_et : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n`.  Set
         `hu_et := BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft`.
      2. Apply the oriented representative twice:
         `(hRep.branch_eq w hw_et).symm` rewrites the pulled BHW branch to
         `hRep.Phi (sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w)`, and
         `hRep.branch_eq u hu_et` rewrites the unpermuted scalar value back
         to `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u`.
      3. Rewrite the pulled invariant using the exact finite permutation
         lemma:
         ```lean
         BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w =
           BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n β.symm
             (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u)
         ```
         by `BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
         (d := d) (n := n) β.symm u`.
      4. Apply `hOrient z hz` to pass from the permuted oriented invariant to
         the unpermuted one.  This is the only scalar equality used in the
         oriented consumer.
      5. Since `u ∈ ForwardTube`, use
         `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` with the checked
         `bvt_F_holomorphic` and
         `bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` inputs, transported
         through `BHW_forwardTube_eq`, to rewrite
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u` to `bvt_F OS lgc n u`.
      6. Unfold `BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative`; it is
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w`.  The checked total permutation symmetry
         `bvt_F_perm OS lgc n β.symm u` gives
         `bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u`, so the final step uses this
         equality in the reverse direction.

      Lean-oriented calculation:

      ```lean
      intro z hz
      let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
      let u : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ := Q.symm z
      let w : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
        BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm u
      have hu_ft : u ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain, Q, u] using hUy_acr hz
      have hw_et : w ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
        simpa [BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain, Q, u, w] using hUy_bhw hz
      have hu_et : u ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
        BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hu_ft
      have hbranch_w :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w =
            hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w) :=
        (hRep.branch_eq w hw_et).symm
      have horient_w :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w =
            BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n β.symm
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u) := by
        simpa [w] using
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant_permAct
            d n β.symm u
      have hbranch_u :
          hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u) =
            BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u :=
        hRep.branch_eq u hu_et
      have hF_holo_BHW :
          DifferentiableOn ℂ (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
        simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
          bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
      have hF_restricted_BHW :
          ∀ (Λ : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
            (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
            z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
            bvt_F OS lgc n
              (fun k μ => ∑ ν, (Λ.val.val μ ν : ℂ) * z k ν) =
            bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
        intro Λ z hz
        exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
          (d := d) OS lgc n Λ z
          ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) ▸ hz)
      have hext_u :
          BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u = bvt_F OS lgc n u :=
        BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
          hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW u hu_ft
      have hperm_u :
          bvt_F OS lgc n w = bvt_F OS lgc n u := by
        simpa [w, BHW.permAct] using bvt_F_perm OS lgc n β.symm u
      calc
        BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) w
            = hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n w) := hbranch_w
        _ = hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n β.symm
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u)) := by
          rw [horient_w]
        _ = hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n u) := by
          simpa [Q, u] using hOrient z hz
        _ = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) u := hbranch_u
        _ = bvt_F OS lgc n u := hext_u
        _ = BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative (d := d) OS lgc n β z := by
          simpa [BHW.os45ACRBranchRepresentative, Q, u, w] using
            hperm_u.symm
      ```

      The proof of
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedSourceEq`
      is then only the unpacking of the packet plus this algebraic reduction:

      ```lean
      rcases hScalar with
        ⟨hRep, Wscal, Uy, hW_relOpen, hUy_open, hy0Uy,
          hUy_sub, hUy_scalar, hW_double, hPhi_eq⟩
      have hUy_acr :
          Uy ⊆ BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β := by
        intro z hz
        exact (hUy_sub hz).1
      have hUy_bhw :
          Uy ⊆ BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) β := by
        intro z hz
        exact (hUy_sub hz).2
      have hOrient :
          let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
          ∀ z, z ∈ Uy ->
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n β.symm
                (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
                  (Q.symm z))) =
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (Q.symm z)) := by
        intro z hz
        exact hPhi_eq (hUy_scalar z hz)
      refine ⟨Uy, hUy_open, hy0Uy, hUy_sub, ?_⟩
      exact
        BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedScalarEq
          (d := d) OS lgc n β hRep Uy hUy_acr hUy_bhw hOrient
      ```

      The fields `hW_relOpen` and `hW_double` are intentionally unused in the
      last unpacking step: their role is upstream, where the identity or
      adjacent source supplier proves the equality `hPhi_eq` by a
      source-variety identity theorem on the oriented double domain.  They
      must remain in the packet so the producer has no hidden analytic
      continuation assumption.

      This oriented ACR agreement is not yet the horizontal branch-germ
      predicate consumed by the common-boundary envelope.  The envelope
      deliberately consumes the representation-free predicate
      `OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt`, because after a one-branch source
      theorem has identified the ACR formula and the pulled BHW formula, the
      EOW step should not remember whether that identification came from the
      pure-Gram or oriented scalar packet.  On the active route, however, the
      only admissible producer of that neutral predicate is the oriented one:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (β : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hScalar :
            BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
              (d := d) OS lgc n β y0) :
          BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
            (d := d) OS lgc n β y0 := by
        classical
        let Q := BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)
        let Wβ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ->
            Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          fun z => BHW.permAct (d := d) β.symm (Q.symm z)
        obtain ⟨Uy, hUy_open, hy0Uy, hUy_sub, hACR_eq⟩ :=
          BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedACRBHWAgreement_of_orientedSourceEq
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n β y0 hScalar
        have hPulled_mem :
            ∀ z ∈ Uy, Wβ z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
          intro z hz
          exact
            BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain_pulled_mem
              (d := d) (n := n) β z (hUy_sub hz).2
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn ℂ (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_cinv_BHW :
            ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro Λ z hz hΛz
          exact
            bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
              (d := d) OS lgc n Λ z
              ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) ▸ hz)
              ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) ▸ hΛz)
        have hExtend_cont :
            DifferentiableOn ℂ
              (fun z =>
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (Wβ z))
              Uy := by
          have hExtend_holo :
              DifferentiableOn ℂ (BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n))
                (BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :=
            BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
              hF_holo_BHW hF_cinv_BHW
          exact
            hExtend_holo.comp_differentiableOn
              (BHW.differentiable_permAct_comp_quarterTurn_symm
                (d := d) (n := n) β)
              hPulled_mem
        refine ⟨Uy, hUy_open, ?_, hPulled_mem, hExtend_cont, ?_, ?_⟩
        · simpa [y0] using hy0Uy
        · intro z hz hz_acr
          exact hACR_eq hz
        · intro z hz hz_bhw
          -- The pulled BHW representative is definitionally the same
          -- branch-specific `extendF` formula on its pulled branch domain.
          simpa [BHW.os45BHWBranchRepresentative, BHW.os45PulledRealBranch,
            Wβ, Q] using
            (BHW.os45PulledRealBranchRepresentative_eq_extendF
              (d := d) OS lgc n β z hz_bhw).symm
      ```

      The helper names in this proof are part of the required implementation
      surface.  `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain_pulled_mem` is the domain
      projection saying that membership in the pulled BHW branch domain means
      the branch-specific argument `Wβ z` lies in `ExtendedTube`.
      `BHW.differentiable_permAct_comp_quarterTurn_symm` is the finite
      continuous-linear differentiability of
      `z ↦ permAct β.symm (Q.symm z)`.  The representative rewrite
      `BHW.os45PulledRealBranchRepresentative_eq_extendF` is definitional
      bookkeeping for `os45BHWBranchRepresentative`; it must not contain any
      source equality, adjacent branch comparison, PET independence, EOW
      output, or final locality.

      There is no generic placeholder structure called
      `OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceInput` in the production route.  The active
      Figure-2-4 suppliers are the two concrete one-branch packet producers,
      and they must build the oriented scalar packet before the neutral
      branch-germ predicate is exposed:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalOrientedSourceGermAt_of_figure24_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hUfig_source :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hVcl : closure V ⊆ Ufig) :
          ∀ x ∈ closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)

      theorem BHW.os45BranchHorizontalOrientedSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (Ufig V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hUfig_open : IsOpen Ufig)
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_nonempty : V.Nonempty)
          (hV_connected : IsConnected V)
          (hV_sourcePatch :
            V ⊆ BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch (d := d) n i hi)
          (hUfig_source :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig, BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) ∧
            (∀ x ∈ Ufig,
              BHW.realEmbed
                (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x) ∈
                BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ))
          (hV_ordered_closure :
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hV_swap_ordered_closure :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ)
          (hV_orientedPath_closure :
            ∀ x ∈ closure V,
              BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField (d := d) n i hi x)
          (hVcl : closure V ⊆ Ufig) :
          ∀ x ∈ closure V,
            BHW.OS45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt
              (d := d) OS lgc n (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩)
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x)
      ```

      Their proofs are short only after the scalar packet suppliers have done
      the real work.  The identity supplier calls
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_id` at
      `β = 1` after deriving the base point's identity ACR and pulled-BHW
      domain memberships.  The adjacent supplier first constructs the
      oriented scalar representative, obtains the upstream
      `OS45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq` compact Wick packet for the selected
      Figure-2-4 patch, and combines that compact packet with
      the closure-level oriented path field `hV_orientedPath_closure` through
      `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq`.
      Only that seed/path packet is then passed to
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent`, together with
      the adjacent ACR/pulled-domain memberships and the quarter-turn scalar
      membership supplied on the closure by
      `hPath.quarterTurn_mem_closure`.  The supplier finally
      transports the base point back with
      `BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq`.  Both suppliers finish
      by calling
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq`.  If either
      scalar packet supplier is not available, the supplier theorem is not
      Lean-ready; there is no abstract Figure-2-4 input record that may be
      filled trivially.

      Implementation transcript for the adjacent supplier:

      ```lean
      intro x hx
      let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
      let y0 :=
        BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n)
          (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x
      let hRep :=
        BHW.sourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n
      let hCompact :=
        BHW.os45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq_of_OSI45
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi
      have hV_sub_seed :
          let hChart :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart_of_figure24
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep
          let hSeed :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hChart hCompact
          V ⊆ hSeed.V := by
        -- `hSeed.V` is the canonical Figure-2-4 source patch; the theorem
        -- surface carries exactly this inclusion as `hV_sourcePatch`.
        simpa [BHW.OS45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq,
          BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch_eq_canonical] using hV_sourcePatch
      have hQuarterTurn_mem_closure :
          let hChart :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarizationChart_of_figure24
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep
          let hSeed :=
            BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hChart hCompact
          ∀ x ∈ closure V,
            BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
              ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
                (BHW.realEmbed
                  (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint
                    (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) x))) ∈ hSeed.Wscal :=
        BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedQuarterTurnMem_closure_of_figure24Path
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hCompact
          V hV_open hV_nonempty hV_connected hV_sub_seed
          hV_orientedPath_closure
      let hPath :=
        BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hCompact
          V hV_open hV_nonempty hV_sub_seed
          hQuarterTurn_mem_closure hV_orientedPath_closure
      have hy_acr_swapped :
          BHW.realEmbed
              (BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ
                (fun k => x (τ k))) ∈
            BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ :=
        BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered
          (d := d) (n := n) τ
          (hV_swap_ordered_closure x hx)
      have hedge_eq :
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint (d := d) (n := n) τ
              (fun k => x (τ k)) = y0 := by
        simpa [τ, y0] using
          BHW.os45CommonEdgeRealPoint_adjacent_swap_eq
            (d := d) (n := n) i hi 1 x
      have hy_acr :
          BHW.realEmbed y0 ∈
            BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ := by
        simpa [hedge_eq] using hy_acr_swapped
      have hy_bhw :
          BHW.realEmbed y0 ∈
            BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n) τ := by
        exact (hUfig_source.2.2.2.2 x (hVcl hx))
      have hy_scalar :
          BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n
            ((BHW.os45QuarterTurnCLE (d := d) (n := n)).symm
              (BHW.realEmbed y0)) ∈ hPath.seed.Wscal := by
        simpa [y0] using hPath.quarterTurn_mem_closure x hx
      have hScalar :
          BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n τ y0 :=
        BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi hRep hPath y0
          hy_acr hy_bhw hy_scalar
      simpa [τ, y0] using
        BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq
          (d := d) hd OS lgc n τ y0 hScalar
      ```

      The identity supplier is the same construction with no compact Wick
      seed.  For `x ∈ closure V`, set `y0 := os45CommonEdgeRealPoint 1 x`,
      obtain `hy_acr` from
      `BHW.os45CommonEdge_mem_acrBranchDomain_of_ordered 1
      (hV_ordered_closure x hx)`, obtain `hy_bhw` from the identity pulled
      branch field in `hUfig_source` and `hVcl hx`, call
      `BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_id` with the same
      `hRep`, and finally call
      `BHW.os45BranchHorizontalSourceGermAt_of_orientedSourceEq`.  No
      adjacent equality, EOW output, or PET branch-independence theorem is
      used in either supplier.

      The two oriented packet suppliers themselves must be local source-input
      theorems, not consequences of the downstream branch-germ predicate.  In
      both cases the chart neighborhood is pulled back from an ambient open
      representative of a relatively open oriented scalar set, then
      intersected with the two branch domains:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_one
          (d n : Nat)
          (G : BHW.SourceOrientedGramData d n) :
          BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) G = G := by
        apply BHW.SourceOrientedGramData.ext
        · simpa [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram] using
            BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram_one n G.gram
        · funext ι
          have hι :
              ι.trans (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)).toEmbedding = ι := by
            ext a
            simp
          simp [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram, hι]

      theorem BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain_one_eq
          (d n : Nat) :
          BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain d n
              (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) =
            BHW.sourceOrientedExtendedTubeDomain d n := by
        ext G
        simp [BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain,
          BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_one]

      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_id
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat)
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceOrientedScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          (y0 : NPointDomain d n)
          (hy_acr :
            BHW.realEmbed y0 ∈
              BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
          (hy_bhw :
            BHW.realEmbed y0 ∈
              BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
                (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) :
          BHW.OS45OneBranchOrientedSourceEqPacket
            (d := d) OS lgc n (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) y0
      ```

      Proof transcript for the identity supplier.  Choose
      `Wscal := hRep.U`.  Destructure `hRep.U_relOpen` as
      `⟨O, hO_open, hU_eq_rel⟩`.  Let
      `scalarMap z :=
        BHW.sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant d n (Q.symm z)`, and define

      ```lean
      let Uy : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
        (scalarMap ⁻¹' O) ∩
          BHW.os45ACRBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) ∩
          BHW.os45PulledRealBranchDomain (d := d) (n := n)
            (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
      ```

      The set `Uy` is open by
      `BHW.continuous_sourceOrientedMinkowskiInvariant`,
      continuity of `Q.symm`, `BHW.isOpen_os45ACRBranchDomain`, and
      `BHW.isOpen_os45PulledRealBranchDomain`.  The base point belongs to
      `Uy`: `hy_acr` unfolds to
      `Q.symm (realEmbed y0) ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n`, hence the oriented
      scalar value lies in `hRep.U` by `hRep.U_eq` and
      `BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube`; `hU_eq_rel` gives membership in
      `O`, while `hy_acr` and `hy_bhw` give the two branch-domain fields.
      For arbitrary `z ∈ Uy`, the same ACR-domain argument puts
      `Q.symm z` in the forward tube, hence
      `scalarMap z ∈ hRep.U = Wscal`.  The double-domain field follows from
      `BHW.sourceOrientedDoublePermutationDomain_one_eq` and `hRep.U_eq`.
      The equality field is the identity simplification:

      ```lean
      have hEq_id :
          Set.EqOn
            (fun G =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram d n
                  (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) G))
            hRep.Phi
            hRep.U := by
        intro G hG
        simp [BHW.sourcePermuteOrientedGram_one]
      ```

      The adjacent supplier consumes the oriented `S'_n` seed/path package,
      because that package is the first object that knows both the adjacent
      oriented double domain and the Figure-2-4 path provenance.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent
          [NeZero d]
