         real-intersection formulation applies.  This is a distributional
         real-environment uniqueness theorem; it does not assert pointwise
         equality of final Wightman boundary distributions and it is not
         Streater-Wightman Theorem 3-6.

         Expanded source transcript for the last line:

         ```lean
         have D :
             BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp :=
           BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45
             (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
             hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
             φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
         -- The producer above is the source proof, not a wrapper:
         -- * use (4.1) to put the compact zero-diagonal Schwinger test in
         --   the ordered OS-I difference-variable branch;
         -- * use (4.12) to identify its Fourier-Laplace transform with
         --   the forward-tube analytic branch represented by `bvt_F`;
         -- * use (4.14) and `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube`
         --   to obtain the Lorentz-invariant analytic branch needed by BHW;
         -- * apply BHW to obtain the single-valued adjacent extended-tube
         --   branch for the deterministic Figure-2-4 lift;
         -- * use the Figure-2-4 real Jost neighborhood and
         --   `OS.E3_symmetric` for the compact test `ψZ` to prove the
         --   common real-boundary distribution input for Jost/Ruelle
         --   uniqueness.
         -- It must fill `D.jr.ordinary_holo`, `D.jr.adjacent_holo`,
         -- `D.jr.ordinary_lorentzInvariant`,
         -- `D.jr.adjacent_lorentzInvariant`,
         -- `D.jr.jostPatch_realEmbed_mem`, `D.jr.realBoundary_eq`,
         -- `D.jr.lift_mem_of_support`, `D.jr_lift_eq`, and
         -- `D.adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.
         -- This theorem may be kept as the single honest frontier, but it may
         -- not be replaced by a public theorem that simply repackages the
         -- compact conclusion below.
         have huniq :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
               =
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.jr.adjacentBranch
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x :=
           by
             have huniq0 :=
               BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
                 (d := d) hd n φ hφ_comp D.jr
             simpa [D.jr_lift_eq] using huniq0
         have hord :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
               =
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x := by
           exact integral_congr_ae (by
             filter_upwards with x
             by_cases hx : x ∈ hChart.V0
             · rw [D.ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift x hx]
             · have hφx : φ x = 0 := by
                 exact
                   (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                     (fun hxSupp => hx (hφ_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
               simp [hφx])
         exact hord.trans
           (huniq.trans D.adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger)
         ```

         The displayed `OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData` and
         `jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary` names are proof-doc
         labels for genuine source content.  If they are introduced in Lean,
         they must be private implementation carriers or standard
         Jost/Ruelle analytic theorems with the fields shown here; they must
         not be public wrappers around
         `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.

         The implementation-level shape of these labels is now fixed.  The
         OS-I §4.5 source proof may use the following private data carrier to
         remember the analytic branches before the compact equality is
         extracted.  This carrier is not a substitute for the canonical-lift
         theorem: its producer is exactly the source proof from equations
         (4.1), (4.12), and (4.14), Euclidean symmetry of the compact
         zero-diagonal test, Bargmann-Hall-Wightman continuation, and the
         Figure-2-4 Jost real-boundary comparison.

         ```lean
         /-- OS-free compact Jost/Ruelle uniqueness input.  This is the
         genuine generic analytic theorem surface: it mentions only the two
         analytic branches, their common real Jost boundary distribution, and
         the compact lift against which the resulting branch identity is
         integrated.  It carries no OS axioms, no scalar representative, no
         PET/EOW/locality term, and no `bvt_W`. -/
         structure BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ)) where
           Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
           Ω_open : IsOpen Ω
           Ω_connected : IsConnected Ω
           lift : NPointDomain d n -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
           lift_mem_of_support :
             ∀ x, x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               lift x ∈ Ω
           jostPatch : Set (NPointDomain d n)
           jostPatch_open : IsOpen jostPatch
           jostPatch_nonempty : jostPatch.Nonempty
           jostPatch_jost :
             ∀ x, x ∈ jostPatch -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n
           jostPatch_realEmbed_mem :
             ∀ x, x ∈ jostPatch -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ Ω
           ordinaryBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ
           adjacentBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ
           ordinary_holo :
             DifferentiableOn ℂ ordinaryBranch Ω
           adjacent_holo :
             DifferentiableOn ℂ adjacentBranch Ω
           ordinary_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
               (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
               z ∈ Ω ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ Ω ->
                 ordinaryBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                   ordinaryBranch z
           adjacent_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
               (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
               z ∈ Ω ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ Ω ->
                 adjacentBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                   adjacentBranch z
           realBoundary_eq :
             ∀ χ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
               HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ jostPatch ->
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     ordinaryBranch (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
                   =
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x

         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (D :
               BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n φ hφ_comp) :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.ordinaryBranch (D.lift x) * φ x
               =
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 D.adjacentBranch (D.lift x) * φ x

         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_realPatch_eqOn_of_distributionEq
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (D :
               BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n φ hφ_comp) :
             Set.EqOn
               (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                 D.ordinaryBranch (BHW.realEmbed x))
               (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                 D.adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x))
               D.jostPatch

         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_branch_eqOn_connectedDomain
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (n : Nat)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (D :
               BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n φ hφ_comp) :
             Set.EqOn D.ordinaryBranch D.adjacentBranch D.Ω

         structure BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0) where
           τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
           φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n
           ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n
           φZ_def :
             φZ =
               ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                 (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                 (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
                 φ hφ_supp⟩
           ψZ_def :
             ψZ =
               permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
           jr :
             BHW.JostRuelleCompactBoundaryData hd n φ hφ_comp
           jr_lift_eq :
             ∀ x, jr.lift x =
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)
          ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift :
            ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
              jr.ordinaryBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) =
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
          adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger :
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                jr.adjacentBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) *
                  φ x
              =
            OS.S n ψZ
         ```

         The OS-specific producer must itself be decomposed before Lean.  It
         is acceptable as one private frontier only if its proof term visibly
         contains the following source packets; otherwise it is just a
         renamed compact theorem.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_ordered :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
             (hV_swap_ordered :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0) :
             BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp

         /-- Domain and lift geometry for the canonical Figure-2-4 compact
         branch.  `Ω` is the connected ordinary-extended-tube BHW/Jost branch
         domain containing both the real Jost patch and every canonical lift
         point over `tsupport φ`.  The subset field is load-bearing: it is
         what lets the ordinary branch be the restriction of
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)` rather than a second analytic function. -/
         structure BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0) where
           Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
           Ω_open : IsOpen Ω
           Ω_connected : IsConnected Ω
           Ω_sub_extendedTube : Ω ⊆ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
           jostPatch_open : IsOpen hChart.V0 := hChart.V0_open
           jostPatch_nonempty : hChart.V0.Nonempty := ⟨x0, hChart.x0_mem⟩
           jostPatch_jost :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n :=
             fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)
           realEmbed_mem :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ Ω
           lift_mem_of_V0 :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) ∈ Ω
           lift_mem_of_support :
             ∀ x, x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) ∈ Ω :=
             fun x hx => lift_mem_of_V0 x (hφ_supp hx)

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_jostRuelleDomainData_of_chart
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp

         Lean-shaped proof.  This packet should not be treated as an
         additional OS-I source theorem.  The connected analytic domain can
         be chosen to be the ordinary extended tube itself:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_jostRuelleDomainData_of_chart
             ... :
             BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp := by
           refine
             { Ω := BHW.ExtendedTube d n
               Ω_open := BHW.isOpen_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)
               Ω_connected := BHW.isConnected_extendedTube (d := d) (n := n)
               Ω_sub_extendedTube := by
                 intro z hz
                 exact hz
               realEmbed_mem := ?_
               lift_mem_of_V0 := ?_ }
           · intro x hx
             exact hV_ET x (hChart.V0_sub hx)
           · intro x hx
             exact hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx (0 : unitInterval)
         ```

         The swapped real-embedding hypothesis `hV_swapET` is intentionally
         still on the surrounding canonical-boundary surface, because the
         adjacent source-patch and compact Figure-2-4 packet need it.  This
         domain constructor itself uses only `hV_ET` and
         `hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube`; it must not infer extended-tube
         membership from bare `JostSet` membership and must not introduce a
         second analytic branch domain by choice.

         /-- Ordinary branch data.  This branch is definitionally the BHW
         ordinary continuation `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)` restricted to
         `DΩ.Ω`; it is not a second analytic function. -/
         structure BHW.OS45Figure24OrdinaryBranchData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp) where
           ordinaryBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
             BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
           ordinaryBranch_def :
             ordinaryBranch = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) := rfl
           ordinary_holo :
             DifferentiableOn ℂ ordinaryBranch DΩ.Ω
           ordinary_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ Λ z, z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
                 ordinaryBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                   ordinaryBranch z
           ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
               ordinaryBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) =
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                   (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_ordinaryBranchData_of_bvt_F
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24OrdinaryBranchData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp DΩ

         /-- Adjacent OS-I branch data.  This is where equations (4.1),
         (4.12), and (4.14) are consumed: (4.1) gives the compact Euclidean
         zero-diagonal boundary functional, (4.12) identifies its
         Fourier-Laplace analytic branch, and (4.14) supplies the Lorentz/BHW
         continuation used on the adjacent Figure-2-4 branch. -/
         structure BHW.OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (φZ ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp) where
           adjacentBranch :
             (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ
           adjacent_holo :
             DifferentiableOn ℂ adjacentBranch DΩ.Ω
           adjacent_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ Λ z, z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
                 adjacentBranch (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                   adjacentBranch z
           adjacent_realBoundary_eq_ordinary :
             ∀ χ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
               HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0 ->
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
                   =
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
           adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 adjacentBranch (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) *
                   φ x
               =
             OS.S n ψZ

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBranchData_of_OSI45
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_ordered :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
             (hV_swap_ordered :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (φZ ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (hφZ_def :
               φZ =
                 ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                   (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                   (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
                   φ hφ_supp⟩)
             (hψZ_def :
               ψZ =
                 permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n)
                   (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩).symm φZ)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
               φZ ψZ DΩ

         Implementation transcript for the adjacent branch packet.  The
         theorem above is Lean-ready only after its proof is decomposed into
         the following real mathematical subclaims; a proof term that simply
         chooses an arbitrary `adjacentBranch` has not formalized OS I §4.5.

         1. **Zero-diagonal orientation.**  Define
            `τ := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩`,
            `φZ := ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap ...⟩`,
            and
            `ψZ := permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz τ.symm φZ`.  The checked
            compact Euclidean edge theorem is called with `ρ = 1`; normalize
            its swapped-order hypothesis by `Equiv.swap_inv` and `mul_one`
            before passing `hV_swap_ordered`.  If the proof is oriented
            through `φZ`, use exactly one `OS.E3_symmetric` call to rewrite
            `OS.S n φZ = OS.S n ψZ`.
         2. **OS-I initial adjacent tube branch.**  Equations `(4.1)` and
            `(4.12)` identify the zero-diagonal Euclidean edge distribution
            with the boundary value of the corrected OS-II Wightman analytic
            continuation on the adjacent permuted tube.  In the Lean route,
            `(4.12)` is not imported through the false OS-I Lemma 8.8; it is
            the already repaired `bvt_F`/`OSLinearGrowthCondition` object.
            The branch is therefore the OS-I adjacent permuted-tube branch
            selected by the Figure-2-4 orientation, not
            `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value` applied directly at
            `permAct τ (wick x)`.
         3. **Lorentz/BHW continuation.**  Equation `(4.14)` supplies
            Lorentz covariance of the Wightman distribution, hence the
            complex-Lorentz invariance hypothesis for the adjacent tube
            branch.  The Bargmann-Hall-Wightman continuation then extends
            this adjacent branch single-valuedly along the connected
            Figure-2-4/Jost domain `DΩ.Ω`.  This is the field
            `adjacent_holo` plus `adjacent_lorentzInvariant`; it also
            transports the OS-I real Jost boundary equality against every
            compact test supported in `hChart.V0`, which is stored as
            `adjacent_realBoundary_eq_ordinary`.  This real-boundary field is
            not recoverable from one lift-pairing equality and cannot be
            replaced by the ordinary branch packet or by a scalar
            representative.
         4. **Canonical-lift compact pairing.**  The lift-pairing field
            `adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` is obtained by
            continuing the compact boundary identity from the adjacent Wick
            edge along the selected Figure-2-4 branch to
            `x ↦ hChart.adjLift x 0`.  The only compact-test input is the
            checked Euclidean edge equality for `ψZ`, with support restricted
            by `hφ_supp`; no raw pointwise adjacent-Wick comparison, local
            source equality, PET independence, EOW envelope, or final
            `bvt_W` locality may appear in this proof.
         5. **Source-patch PET-overlap branch difference.**  The compact
            packet consumed by the oriented real seed is not an integral of
            raw `extendF` at the half-time common edge, and it is not obtained
            by identifying either real source branch separately with a
            Schwinger functional.  The producer must prove the direct compact
            branch-difference theorem
            `BHW.os45Figure24_sourcePatch_pairing_eq_swappedSourcePatch_of_OSI45`.
            Its proof uses the Wick compact equality from the checked
            Euclidean edge theorem, then the direct OS-I/BHW-Jost
            source-patch difference carrier to continue the zero branch
            difference to the real source trace
            `extendF (realEmbed x) = extendF (realEmbed (x ∘ τ))` under the
            same compact test.  It must not call the downstream
            `BHW.os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope`.

         Paper-level OS-I transcript for these five subclaims.  The local
         scan of `references/Reconstruction theorem I.pdf` fixes the exact
         role of the three cited equations in the implementation:

         * Equation `(4.1)` is only the ordered-sector passage from the
           Euclidean Schwinger distribution to the translation-reduced
           difference-variable distribution `S_{n-1}`.  It is consumed after
           the compact test has been made zero-diagonal and supported in the
           Figure-2-4 Jost patch.  In Lean this is the checked
           `bvt_euclidean_restriction`/zero-diagonal orientation layer, not a
           pointwise adjacent Wick identity.
         * Equation `(4.12)` is the Fourier-Laplace construction of the
           Wightman momentum distribution from the reduced `S` distribution.
           On this branch it must be read through the OS-II corrected
           `bvt_F`/`OSLinearGrowthCondition` construction; it may not import
           the false OS-I Lemma 8.8 continuation route.  The theorem surface
           therefore has to identify the adjacent permuted-tube branch with
           the corrected `bvt_F` boundary branch selected by the compact test.
         * Equation `(4.14)` is the infinitesimal Lorentz covariance equation
           `X_{ij} W_n = 0`.  Its Lean consumer is a complex-Lorentz
           invariance field for the adjacent analytic branch on the connected
           Figure-2-4/Jost domain, parallel in content to
           `bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube` for the ordinary
           branch.  It is not a scalar-source equality and not final
           locality.
         * OS-I §4.5 then says that Euclidean symmetry plus `(4.1)`, `(4.12)`,
           and `(4.14)` gives the symmetric analytic continuation into the
           adjacent/permuted tube, and only after that applies the
           Bargmann-Hall-Wightman theorem to continue single-valuedly to the
           complex Lorentz orbit domain.  The local theorem here is exactly
           the adjacent transposition/compact-test specialization of that
           paragraph.

         A Lean implementation of
         `BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBranchData_of_OSI45` should therefore be
         split through the following private support surfaces, or inline
         proofs with exactly this content:

         ```lean
         /-- The compact OS-I `(4.1)`/`(4.12)` boundary datum for the
         adjacent transposition before BHW continuation.  This carrier is
         proof-local: it records the corrected Fourier-Laplace branch on the
         adjacent permuted forward-tube side and its compact boundary pairing
         with the zero-diagonal test `ψZ`. -/
         structure BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n) where
           τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
           V_ordered :
             ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
               x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1
           V_swap_ordered :
             ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
               (fun k => x (τ k)) ∈
                 EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) τ
           Ωτ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
           Ωτ_sector_eq :
             Ωτ = BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n τ
           Ωτ_open : IsOpen Ωτ
           permutedForward_subset_Ωτ :
             BHW.PermutedForwardTube d n τ ⊆ Ωτ
           wick_adjacent_mem :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
               (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) ∈ Ωτ
           branchτ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ
           branchτ_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ branchτ Ωτ
           branchτ_eq_correctedExtendF :
             ∀ z, z ∈ Ωτ ->
               branchτ z =
                 BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (fun k => z (τ k))
           branchτ_boundary_pairing :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 branchτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) * φ x
               =
             OS.S n ψZ

         /-- The `(4.14)` Lorentz-covariant adjacent branch before its BHW
         extension to the Figure-2-4/Jost domain.  The carrier extends the
         preceding compact boundary branch by adding the complex-Lorentz
         invariance needed by Bargmann-Hall-Wightman. -/
         structure BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ) where
           branchτ_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
               (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
               z ∈ Dinit.Ωτ ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ Dinit.Ωτ ->
                 Dinit.branchτ (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                   Dinit.branchτ z

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentInitialBoundary_of_OSI45_4_1_4_12
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ordered :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
             (hV_swap_ordered :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                     (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (φZ ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (hφZ_def :
               φZ =
                 ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                   (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                   (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
                   φ hφ_supp⟩)
             (hψZ_def :
               ψZ =
                 permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n)
                   (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩).symm φZ) :
             BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentLorentzInvariant_of_OSI45_4_14
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ) :
             BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBHWContinuation_to_JostDomain
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (φZ ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp)
             (Dlor :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit) :
             BHW.OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp φZ ψZ DΩ
         ```

         The two displayed `OS45Adjacent...` data names are proof-local
         carriers for genuine OS-I §4.5 content.  They must record boundary
         support, the fact that `Ωτ` is exactly
         `BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n τ`, the corrected
         Fourier-Laplace `bvt_F` branch after the coordinate relabelling
         `z ↦ fun k => z (τ k)`, and the Lorentz covariance needed before BHW
         continuation.  The field `branchτ_eq_correctedExtendF` is the guard
         against arbitrary analytic data: holomorphy of `branchτ` must be
         obtained by composing the ordinary corrected `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc
         n)` with the finite coordinate permutation, and the boundary-pairing
         field must then identify that selected branch with the OS-I
         `(4.1)`/`(4.12)` compact distribution.  The final continuation
         theorem must construct the `adjacentBranch` on `DΩ.Ω`, prove its
         holomorphy and invariance there, and transport the compact pairing
         from the adjacent permuted edge to the deterministic canonical lift.
         These carriers must not be implemented as `True`, as arbitrary
         chosen functions, or as wrappers around
         `OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData`; if no lower-level theorem with this
         content exists, these surfaces remain the adjacent compact source
         frontier.

         Lean-shaped proof transcript for the first two carrier constructors:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentInitialBoundary_of_OSI45_4_1_4_12
             ... := by
           let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
             Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
           let Ωτ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
             BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n τ
           let branchτ :
               (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
             fun z =>
               BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (fun k => z (τ k))
           have hF_holo_BHW :
               DifferentiableOn ℂ (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                 (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
             simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
               bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
           have hF_cinv_BHW :
               ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
                 (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
                 z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
                 BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
                   bvt_F OS lgc n
                     (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                   bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
             exact bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
               (d := d) OS lgc n
           have hΩ_open : IsOpen Ωτ := by
             simpa [Ωτ] using
               BHW.isOpen_permutedExtendedTubeSector
                 (d := d) (n := n) τ
           have hPFT : BHW.PermutedForwardTube d n τ ⊆ Ωτ := by
             intro z hz
             change (fun k => z (τ k)) ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
             exact BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube
               (d := d) (n := n) hz
           have hwick : ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
               (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) ∈ Ωτ := by
             intro x hx
             have hxord :
                 x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector
                   (d := d) (n := n) 1 :=
               hV_ordered x (hChart.V0_sub hx)
             exact hPFT
               (wick_mem_permutedForwardTube_of_ordered
                 (d := d) (n := n) (π := τ) x hxord)
           have hbranch_holo :
               DifferentiableOn ℂ branchτ Ωτ := by
             exact
               (BHW.extendF_holomorphicOn n (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                 hF_holo_BHW hF_cinv_BHW).comp_differentiableOn
                   (differentiable_permNPoint τ).differentiableOn
                   (by intro z hz; simpa [Ωτ,
                     BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector] using hz)
           have hbranch_eq :
               ∀ z, z ∈ Ωτ ->
                 branchτ z =
                   BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                     (fun k => z (τ k)) := by
             intro z hz; rfl
           have hpair :
               ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   branchτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) * φ x
                 =
               OS.S n ψZ := by
             have hφZ :
                 OS.S n φZ =
                   ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     bvt_F OS lgc n
                       (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x := by
               simpa [hφZ_def] using
                 bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n φZ
             have hE3 : OS.S n φZ = OS.S n ψZ := by
               subst φZ
               subst ψZ
               refine OS.E3_symmetric (n := n) (σ := τ.symm) _ _ ?_
               intro x
               simp [permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz, τ]
             calc
               ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   branchτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) * φ x
                   =
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   bvt_F OS lgc n
                     (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x := by
                     -- Pointwise integrand rewrite using `τ * τ = 1`,
                     -- `hV_ordered`, and
                     -- `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube`.
                     exact integral_congr_ae
                       (ae_of_all _ fun x => by
                         simp [branchτ, τ, Equiv.swap_inv,
                           BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube])
               _ = OS.S n φZ := hφZ.symm
               _ = OS.S n ψZ := hE3
           exact
             { τ := τ
               Ωτ := Ωτ
               Ωτ_sector_eq := rfl
               Ωτ_open := hΩ_open
               V_ordered := hV_ordered
               V_swap_ordered := by
                 intro x hx
                 simpa [τ] using hV_swap_ordered x hx
               permutedForward_subset_Ωτ := hPFT
               wick_adjacent_mem := hwick
               branchτ := branchτ
               branchτ_holo := hbranch_holo
               branchτ_eq_correctedExtendF := hbranch_eq
               branchτ_boundary_pairing := hpair }

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentLorentzInvariant_of_OSI45_4_14
             ... (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ) := by
           have hF_holo_BHW :
               DifferentiableOn ℂ (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                 (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
             simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
               bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
           have hF_cinv_BHW :
               ∀ (Λ : ComplexLorentzGroup d)
                 (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
                 z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
                 BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
                   bvt_F OS lgc n
                     (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) =
                   bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
             exact bvt_F_complexLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
               (d := d) OS lgc n
           refine { branchτ_lorentzInvariant := ?_ }
           intro Λ z hz hΛz
           rw [Dinit.branchτ_eq_correctedExtendF _ hΛz,
             Dinit.branchτ_eq_correctedExtendF _ hz]
           have hperm :
               (fun k =>
                   (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) (Dinit.τ k)) =
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ
                 (fun k => z (Dinit.τ k)) := by
             simp [BHW.complexLorentzAction]
           rw [hperm]
           exact
             BHW.extendF_complex_lorentz_invariant n
               (bvt_F OS lgc n) hF_holo_BHW hF_cinv_BHW Λ
               (fun k => z (Dinit.τ k))
               (by simpa [Dinit.Ωτ_sector_eq,
                 BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector] using hz)
         ```

         The displayed `wick_mem_permutedForwardTube_of_ordered` and
         `differentiable_permNPoint` names may be implemented as private
         one-line topology helpers if no exact declaration already exists:
         the first is just the definition of `BHW.PermutedForwardTube` after
         `τ * τ = 1`, and the second is continuity/differentiability of a
         finite coordinate permutation.  The final integral-congruence line
         is deliberately recorded at theorem-surface level: the production
         proof must rewrite the pointwise integrand by the same displayed
         steps and then call the repository's standard integral congruence
         lemma, rather than introducing a new analytic shortcut.

         A later implementation may package these subclaims as private
         helpers, but only if the helpers expose this content: an initial
         adjacent permuted-tube boundary theorem, a Lorentz/BHW continuation
         theorem on `DΩ.Ω`, and the resulting canonical-lift compact pairing.
         Those helpers are still source mathematics, not forwarding wrappers.

         The theorem surface for
         `BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBranchData_of_OSI45` must fill three
         provenance fields: holomorphic/Lorentz continuation, real Jost
         boundary equality, and the fixed canonical-lift compact pairing.  A
         packet with only `adjacent_holo`, `adjacent_lorentzInvariant`, and
         one lift-pairing field is too weak: the later real-boundary theorem
         would be false for an arbitrary analytic branch.  The source
         comparison is therefore stored directly as
         `adjacent_realBoundary_eq_ordinary`.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBranchData_of_OSI45
             ... := by
           classical
           let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
             Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
           have Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ :=
             BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentInitialBoundary_of_OSI45_4_1_4_12
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
               hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
               φZ ψZ hφZ_def hψZ_def
           have Dlor :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit :=
             BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentLorentzInvariant_of_OSI45_4_14
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit
           exact
             BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBHWContinuation_to_JostDomain
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
               hV_ET hV_swapET hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
               φZ ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor
         ```

         The final continuation constructor is the local implementation-facing
         form of OS I §4.5 after `(4.1)/(4.12)` and `(4.14)` have been
         isolated.  It may be implemented as a private theorem, but its proof
         term must visibly construct the adjacent branch on `DΩ.Ω`, prove the
         real-boundary comparison for every compact `χ` supported in
         `hChart.V0`, and prove the fixed lift pairing for `φ`.

         ```lean
         structure BHW.OS45AdjacentBHWJostContinuationData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp)
             (Dlor :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit)
             where
           Badj : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ
           Badj_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ Badj DΩ.Ω
           Badj_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ Λ z, z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
                 Badj (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = Badj z
           Badj_realBoundary_eq_ordinary :
             ∀ χ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
               HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0 ->
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
                   =
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     Badj (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
           Badj_liftPairing :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 Badj (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
               =
             OS.S n ψZ

         theorem BHW.os45_bhwJostContinuation_adjacentPermutedBranch
             ...
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp)
             (Dlor :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit) :
             BHW.OS45AdjacentBHWJostContinuationData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor
         ```

         The adjacent-branch record is then assembled from this continuation
         result:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_adjacentBHWContinuation_to_JostDomain
             ... : BHW.OS45Figure24AdjacentBranchData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp φZ ψZ DΩ := by
           classical
           let C :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentBHWJostContinuationData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor :=
             BHW.os45_bhwJostContinuation_adjacentPermutedBranch
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
               hV_ET hV_swapET hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
               ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor
           exact
             { adjacentBranch := C.Badj
               adjacent_holo := C.Badj_holo
               adjacent_lorentzInvariant := C.Badj_lorentzInvariant
               adjacent_realBoundary_eq_ordinary := by
                 intro χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
                 exact C.Badj_realBoundary_eq_ordinary χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
               adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger :=
                 C.Badj_liftPairing }
         ```

         The displayed
         `BHW.os45_bhwJostContinuation_adjacentPermutedBranch` is not a
         license for a new axiom.  It is a genuine OS-I/BHW producer: start
         with `Dinit.branchτ` on the adjacent permuted tube, use
         `Dlor.branchτ_lorentzInvariant` for the BHW continuation, follow the
         deterministic Figure-2-4/Jost continuation path into `DΩ.Ω`, and
         transport distributional boundary values along that path.  Its
         `Badj_realBoundary_eq_ordinary` output is consumed by generic
         Jost/Ruelle uniqueness; it cannot be recovered from that uniqueness
         theorem without circularity.

         Implementation transcript for
         `BHW.os45_bhwJostContinuation_adjacentPermutedBranch`.  The proof is
         one OS-I §4.5 specialization, but its internal obligations are fixed:

         1. **Continue the branch.**  Apply the local
            Bargmann-Hall-Wightman/Jost continuation theorem to the
            Lorentz-invariant branch `Dinit.branchτ` on
            `Dinit.Ωτ = BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n Dinit.τ`.  The
            continuation domain is exactly `DΩ.Ω`, not an arbitrary connected
            component.  The output branch `Badj` is characterized by
            agreement with `Dinit.branchτ` on the selected adjacent
            permuted-tube edge and by analytic continuation along the
            deterministic Figure-2-4 path.
         2. **Holomorphy and Lorentz invariance.**  `Badj_holo` is the
            holomorphy output of the BHW continuation on `DΩ.Ω`.
            `Badj_lorentzInvariant` is transported from
            `Dlor.branchτ_lorentzInvariant` along the same continuation; it is
            not re-proved from the ordinary branch.
         3. **Real-boundary comparison.**  For every compact
            `χ : SchwartzNPoint d n` with `tsupport χ ⊆ hChart.V0`, apply the
            OS-I §4.5 real Jost boundary comparison to the two continued
            branches.  This gives the exact distributional field
            `Badj_realBoundary_eq_ordinary χ hχ_comp hχ_supp`:

            ```lean
            ∫ x, BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x =
              ∫ x, Badj (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
            ```

            The proof uses the same `(4.1)/(4.12)/(4.14)` branch selection,
            the local real Jost patch `hChart.V0`, and compact-test
            distributional equality.  It must not use generic Jost/Ruelle
            uniqueness, because this field is an input to that theorem.
         4. **Canonical-lift pairing.**  Transport
            `Dinit.branchτ_boundary_pairing` from the adjacent Wick edge to
            the deterministic canonical lift `x ↦ hChart.adjLift x 0` along
            the selected Figure-2-4 continuation.  This proves
            `Badj_liftPairing`; the only compact-test symmetry used here is
            the zero-diagonal `ψZ` equality already stored in `Dinit`.

         Lean-shaped body of the source theorem:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45_bhwJostContinuation_adjacentPermutedBranch
             ... := by
           classical
           obtain
             ⟨Badj, hBadj_agrees_edge, hBadj_holo, hBadj_inv,
               hBadj_realBoundary, hBadj_lift⟩ :=
             BHW.os45_BHWJost_continue_adjacentBranch_from_OSI45
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET
               hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor
           exact
             { Badj := Badj
               Badj_holo := hBadj_holo
               Badj_lorentzInvariant := hBadj_inv
               Badj_realBoundary_eq_ordinary := by
                 intro χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
                 exact hBadj_realBoundary χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
               Badj_liftPairing := hBadj_lift }
         ```

         The displayed
         `BHW.os45_BHWJost_continue_adjacentBranch_from_OSI45` is the actual
         standard-paper theorem to formalize here.  It is not allowed to
         mention source scalar representatives, EOW envelopes, PET
         monodromy, `bvt_W`, or final locality.  Its proof is exactly OS I
         §4.5 plus the local BHW continuation theorem: equations `(4.1)` and
         `(4.12)` identify the adjacent compact boundary branch, `(4.14)`
         gives Lorentz covariance, BHW/Jost continuation carries the branch
         to the Figure-2-4 domain, and the two compact consequences above are
         read off by analytic continuation of distributions.

         To keep this paper theorem implementation-level rather than
         slogan-level, split it one more time through a local continuation
         carrier.  This carrier is the Figure-2-4 specialization of the OS I
         §4.5 sentence: E2 plus `(4.1)`, `(4.12)`, `(4.14)` gives a symmetric
         analytic branch on the adjacent/permuted tube, and the
         Bargmann-Hall-Wightman theorem continues that branch through the
         proper complex Lorentz/Jost domain.  The carrier is local: it names
         only the selected adjacent transposition, selected chart, selected
         compact test, and deterministic canonical lift.

         ```lean
         structure BHW.OS45BHWJostLocalContinuationData
             [NeZero d]
             (hd : 2 <= d)
             (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
             (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
             (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
             (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
             (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
             (hV_ET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_swapET :
               ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
                 BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                   BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
             (hChart :
               BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0)
             (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
             (hφ_comp :
               HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
             (hφ_supp :
               tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0)
             (ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp)
             (Dlor :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit)
             where
           ΩJ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
           ΩJ_open : IsOpen ΩJ
           ΩJ_connected : IsConnected ΩJ
           ΩJ_sub_bhwJostHull :
             ΩJ ⊆
               BHW.os45BHWJostHull d n Dinit.τ
                 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 (Dinit.τ k)))
           DΩ_sub_ΩJ : DΩ.Ω ⊆ ΩJ
           Ωτ_sub_ΩJ : Dinit.Ωτ ⊆ ΩJ
           adjacentEdge_mem_ΩJ :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
               (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k))) ∈ ΩJ
           adjacentEdge_mem_Ωτ :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
               (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k))) ∈ Dinit.Ωτ :=
             Dinit.wick_adjacent_mem
           realPatch_mem_ΩJ :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ ΩJ
           lift_mem_ΩJ_of_support :
             ∀ x, x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) ∈ ΩJ
           WJ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ
           WJ_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ WJ ΩJ
           WJ_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ Λ z, z ∈ ΩJ ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ ΩJ ->
                 WJ (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = WJ z
           WJ_agrees_Ωτ :
             ∀ z, z ∈ Dinit.Ωτ -> WJ z = Dinit.branchτ z
           WJ_agrees_initialEdge :
             ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
               WJ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k))) =
                 Dinit.branchτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k))) := by
             intro x hx
             exact WJ_agrees_Ωτ
               (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k)))
               (adjacentEdge_mem_Ωτ x hx)
           WJ_restrict_DΩ_holo :
             DifferentiableOn ℂ WJ DΩ.Ω := WJ_holo.mono DΩ_sub_ΩJ
           WJ_restrict_DΩ_lorentzInvariant :
             ∀ Λ z, z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
               BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ DΩ.Ω ->
                 WJ (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = WJ z := by
             intro Λ z hz hΛz
             exact WJ_lorentzInvariant Λ z (DΩ_sub_ΩJ hz) (DΩ_sub_ΩJ hΛz)
           WJ_realBoundary_eq_ordinary :
             ∀ χ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
               HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0 ->
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
                   =
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     WJ (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
           WJ_liftPairing :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 WJ (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
               =
             OS.S n ψZ
         ```

         The symbol
         `BHW.os45BHWJostHull d n Dinit.τ
           (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 (Dinit.τ k)))`
         is a local proof-doc name for the proper-complex Lorentz/Jost path
         component of the identity and `Dinit.τ` adjacent tube used in
         OS I §4.5.  A Lean implementation may
         spell it as an existing local BHW hull if one is already available,
         but it may not replace it by global PET branch independence.  The
         required geometric facts are exactly the fields above: the hull
         contains the whole initial adjacent tube `Dinit.Ωτ`, the adjacent
         Wick edge, the real Jost patch, `DΩ.Ω`, and the canonical lift over
         `tsupport φ`, and it is connected on this local Figure-2-4
         component.  The continued branch must agree with `Dinit.branchτ` on
         all of `Dinit.Ωτ`; agreement only on the selected edge is too weak to
         be the BHW analytic-continuation input.

         The actual lower theorem is then:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45_BHWJostLocalContinuationData_from_OSI45
             ...
             (hV_open : IsOpen V)
             (hV_adjLift_ET :
               let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
                 Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> ∀ t,
                 BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                   (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x t ∈
                 BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_sourcePatch :
               V ⊆ BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch (d := d) n i hi)
             (hV_orientedPath_closure :
               ∀ x ∈ closure hChart.V0,
                 BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField (d := d) n i hi x)
             (Dinit :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeBoundaryFunctional
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ)
             (DΩ :
               BHW.OS45Figure24JostRuelleDomainData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp)
             (Dlor :
               BHW.OS45AdjacentPermutedTubeLorentzInvariantBranch
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit) :
             BHW.OS45BHWJostLocalContinuationData
               hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor
         ```

         Its proof is not allowed to be a wrapper around
         `OS45AdjacentBHWJostContinuationData`.  It is the place where the
         paper theorem is formalized:

         * use `(4.1)` and `(4.12)` to identify `Dinit.branchτ` as the
           Fourier-Laplace/Wightman branch selected by the permuted
           zero-diagonal compact test `ψZ`;
         * use `(4.14)` through `Dlor.branchτ_lorentzInvariant` to meet the
           BHW theorem's complex-Lorentz covariance hypothesis;
         * construct the local proper-complex/Jost hull and prove the
           membership fields for the whole initial tube `Dinit.Ωτ`, the
           adjacent Wick edge, real Jost patch, `DΩ.Ω`, and
           `hChart.adjLift · 0`;
         * obtain `WJ`, `WJ_holo`, and `WJ_lorentzInvariant` by the
           Bargmann-Hall-Wightman continuation theorem on that local connected
           hull;
         * prove `WJ_agrees_Ωτ` as the branch-normalization condition in the
           BHW theorem, then derive `WJ_agrees_initialEdge` from
           `Dinit.wick_adjacent_mem`;
         * prove `WJ_realBoundary_eq_ordinary` by equality of the two
           boundary-value distributions on the real Jost patch, not by generic
           Jost/Ruelle uniqueness;
         * prove `WJ_liftPairing` by applying the same continued branch to
           the fixed compact lift family and using the initial compact
           boundary pairing `Dinit.branchτ_boundary_pairing`.

         For production implementation, this lower theorem should not be
         attacked as one large proof.  The proof-doc-complete split is the
         following five local support surfaces.  These are all inside the same
         OS I §4.5/BHW theorem; none is a public theorem-2 shortcut.

         The hull in the first support surface must be a concrete path
         component, not an unnamed connected open set.  Use the proper-complex
         Lorentz saturation of the identity extended tube and the selected
         adjacent permuted tube:

         ```lean
         def BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient
             (d n : Nat) (τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
             Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
           {z | ∃ Λ u,
             u ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n ∧
             z = BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ u} ∪
           {z | ∃ Λ u,
             u ∈ BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n τ ∧
             z = BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ u}

         def BHW.os45BHWJostHull
             (d n : Nat) (τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
             (zbase : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
             Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
           pathComponentIn (BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient d n τ) zbase
         ```

         In the local carrier choose

         ```lean
         let zτ0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
           fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 (Dinit.τ k))
         ```

         and set `ΩJ := BHW.os45BHWJostHull d n Dinit.τ zτ0`.  The proof
         then has no hidden component choice.  The needed topology API is
         exact: `JoinedIn`, `JoinedIn.refl`, `JoinedIn.trans`,
         `JoinedIn.mono`, `JoinedIn.somePath`, and `JoinedIn.somePath_mem`
         are in `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.PathConnected`;
         the checked local helper
         `BHW.isOpen_pathComponentIn_of_isOpen_normed` supplies the open
         path-component fact in the finite-dimensional normed source
         configuration spaces; `IsOpen.isConnected_iff_isPathConnected` is in
         `Mathlib.Topology.Connected.LocPathConnected`; `Path.trans` and
         `Path.trans_range` are in `Mathlib.Topology.Path`.

         The path-component proof must be split into these finite pieces:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient_open
             [NeZero d] (hd : 2 <= d) (n : Nat)
             (τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)) :
             IsOpen (BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient d n τ)

         theorem BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient_mem_identity
             (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
             (hz : z ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n) :
             z ∈ BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient d n τ

         theorem BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient_mem_adjacent
             (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
             (hz : z ∈ BHW.permutedExtendedTubeSector d n τ) :
             z ∈ BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient d n τ

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_joined_adjacentWick_to_adjLift0
             ...
             (x : NPointDomain d n) (hx : x ∈ hChart.V0) :
             JoinedIn (BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient d n Dinit.τ)
               (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k)))
               (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))

         theorem BHW.os45Figure24_joined_adjLift0_to_realPatch
             ...
             (x : NPointDomain d n) (hx : x ∈ hChart.V0) :
             JoinedIn (BHW.os45BHWJostAmbient d n Dinit.τ)
               (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
               (BHW.realEmbed x)
         ```

         The first joined theorem uses
         `BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse`,
         `hChart.adjLift_def`, `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero`, and a
         path in the proper complex Lorentz group from `1` to `Λinv`; the
         image path is `t ↦ complexLorentzAction (Λpath t) zraw`.  The second
         joined theorem uses the deterministic Figure-2-4 path
         `t ↦ hChart.adjLift x t`, its extended-tube membership, and the
         endpoint calculation supplied by the chart's `figPath_closure`
         field.  Both paths live in the ambient because the ambient contains
         the identity tube and the adjacent tube together with their
         proper-complex Lorentz images.  They do not use PET branch
         independence, scalar representatives, or final locality.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45_BHWJostHullData_of_figure24
             ... :
             ∃ ΩJ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
               IsOpen ΩJ ∧
               IsConnected ΩJ ∧
               ΩJ ⊆
                 BHW.os45BHWJostHull d n Dinit.τ
                   (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 (Dinit.τ k))) ∧
               Dinit.Ωτ ⊆ ΩJ ∧
               DΩ.Ω ⊆ ΩJ ∧
               (∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 ->
                 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k))) ∈ ΩJ) ∧
               (∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ ΩJ) ∧
               (∀ x, x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
                 hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) ∈ ΩJ)

         theorem BHW.os45_BHWJostBranch_onLocalHull_of_OSI45
             ...
             {ΩJ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
             (hΩ_open : IsOpen ΩJ)
             (hΩ_conn : IsConnected ΩJ)
             (hΩ_hull :
               ΩJ ⊆
                 BHW.os45BHWJostHull d n Dinit.τ
                   (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 (Dinit.τ k))))
             (hΩτ : Dinit.Ωτ ⊆ ΩJ) :
             ∃ WJ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ,
               DifferentiableOn ℂ WJ ΩJ ∧
               (∀ Λ z, z ∈ ΩJ ->
                 BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ ΩJ ->
                   WJ (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = WJ z) ∧
               (∀ z, z ∈ Dinit.Ωτ -> WJ z = Dinit.branchτ z)

         theorem BHW.os45_BHWJostRealBoundaryEq_of_OSI45
             ...
             {ΩJ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
             {WJ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
             (hV_open : IsOpen V)
             (hV_adjLift_ET :
               let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
                 Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
               ∀ x, x ∈ V -> ∀ t,
                 BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                   (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x t ∈
                 BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
             (hV_sourcePatch :
               V ⊆ BHW.os45Figure24SourcePatch (d := d) n i hi)
             (hV_orientedPath_closure :
               ∀ x ∈ closure hChart.V0,
                 BHW.OS45Figure24OrientedPathField (d := d) n i hi x)
             (hW_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ WJ ΩJ)
             (hW_inv :
               ∀ Λ z, z ∈ ΩJ ->
                 BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ ΩJ ->
                   WJ (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = WJ z)
             (hW_Ωτ : ∀ z, z ∈ Dinit.Ωτ -> WJ z = Dinit.branchτ z)
             (hreal : ∀ x, x ∈ hChart.V0 -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ ΩJ) :
             ∀ χ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
               HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0 ->
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x
                   =
                 ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                     WJ (BHW.realEmbed x) * χ x

         theorem BHW.os45_BHWJostLiftPairing_of_OSI45
             ...
             {WJ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
             (hLift_transport :
               ∀ x, x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
                 WJ (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) =
                   Dinit.branchτ
                     (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k)))) :
             ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                 WJ (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
               =
             OS.S n ψZ
         ```

         The remaining transport theorem in this sequence is the equality
         consumed by `hLift_transport`:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45_BHWJostLiftTransport_onSupport_of_OSI45
             ...
             {ΩJ : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
             {WJ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ}
             (hW_inv :
               ∀ Λ z, z ∈ ΩJ ->
                 BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z ∈ ΩJ ->
                   WJ (BHW.complexLorentzAction Λ z) = WJ z)
             (hΩτ : Dinit.Ωτ ⊆ ΩJ)
             (hW_Ωτ : ∀ z, z ∈ Dinit.Ωτ -> WJ z = Dinit.branchτ z)
             (hlift : ∀ x, x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) ∈ ΩJ) :
             ∀ x, x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
               WJ (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) =
                 Dinit.branchτ
                   (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k)))
         ```

         This theorem is where the selected Figure-2-4 lift at `0` is related
         to the adjacent Wick edge.  Since the checked Figure-2-4 lift is the
         rotated two-plane realization
         `figure24RotateAdjacentConfig hd (permAct τ (IdentityPath x t))`,
         the proof is not an endpoint equality.  It is the same Lorentz
         normalization used by
         `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_extendF_eq_permutedWick_zero`: choose
         `Λinv` from `BHW.figure24RotateAdjacentConfig_lorentz_inverse`, prove

         ```lean
         BHW.complexLorentzAction Λinv
           (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) =
             fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (Dinit.τ k))
         ```

         using `hChart.adjLift_def`,
         `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift`, `BHW.permAct`, and
         `BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero`; then apply
         `hW_inv` with `hlift x hx` and
         `hΩτ (Dinit.wick_adjacent_mem x (hφ_supp hx))`, and finally rewrite
         the adjacent Wick value by `hW_Ωτ`.  This Lorentz transport theorem
         must be exported as its own proof step; it must not be hidden inside
         the integral proof.  Once `hLift_transport` is available,
         `os45_BHWJostLiftPairing_of_OSI45` is only `integral_congr` plus
         `Dinit.branchτ_boundary_pairing`, with the standard support split:
         on `tsupport φ` use `hLift_transport`, and off `tsupport φ` the test
         factor `φ x` is zero because `support φ ⊆ tsupport φ`.

         Lean-shaped assembly of the local carrier:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45_BHWJostLocalContinuationData_from_OSI45
             ... := by
           classical
           obtain
             ⟨ΩJ, hΩJ_open, hΩJ_conn, hΩJ_hull, hΩτ_sub,
               hDΩ_sub, hadj_mem, hreal_mem, hlift_mem⟩ :=
             BHW.os45_BHWJostHullData_of_figure24
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET
               hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor
           obtain
             ⟨WJ, hWJ_holo, hWJ_inv, hWJ_Ωτ⟩ :=
             BHW.os45_BHWJostBranch_onLocalHull_of_OSI45
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET
               hChart φ hφ_comp hφ_supp ψZ Dinit DΩ Dlor
               hΩJ_open hΩJ_conn hΩJ_hull hΩτ_sub
           have hrealBoundary :
               ∀ χ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
                 HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
                 tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0 ->
                   ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
