            `hV_adjLift_ET` and `hV_orientedPath_closure` are kept in the
            public theorem signature for compatibility with downstream
            Figure-2-4 consumers; the direct carrier itself is not allowed to
            call oriented branch-germ suppliers.

            Archived circular helper names, not active for this theorem:

            ```lean
            theorem BHW.os45CommonBoundary_wickTrace_zero_of_compactPairing_zero
                ...
                (hwick_pairing_zero_all :
                  ∀ χ, HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
                    tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ V0 ->
                    ∫ x,
                      (wickBranchDiff x) * χ x = 0) :
                ∀ x ∈ hChart.V0,
                  Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) 1
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) = 0
            ```

            The proof of this helper is not optional: it applies
            `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`
            on the open real patch `hChart.V0`, uses continuity of
            `x ↦ Hc (commonChart (wick x))`, and rewrites compact tests by
            `hwick_trace_c`.  Then use the product totally-real identity
            theorem on the connected common chart:

            ```lean
            theorem BHW.os45CommonBoundary_identity_of_wickTrace_zero
                ... :
                ∀ z ∈ Uc, Hc z = 0
            ```

            Its proof uses `hUc_open`, `hUc_conn`, `hHc_holo`, the Wick trace
            membership `hwick_mem_c`, and the fact that the Wick real section
            of `hChart.V0` is a nonempty open totally-real slice in the common
            chart.  It does not use scalar-source descent, PET, final
            locality, or global branch independence.

            The integral rewrite used inside the Wick-trace extraction is also
            bookkeeping, but it must be explicit so the support restriction is
            not hidden:

            ```lean
            theorem BHW.integral_commonBoundary_wickTrace_eq_zero_of_wickDiff_zero
                [NeZero d]
                (hd : 2 <= d)
                (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
                (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
                (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
                (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
                (ρ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                (Uc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
                (Hc : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
                (hwick_trace_c :
                  ∀ x ∈ V0,
                    Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) =
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k =>
                          wickRotatePoint
                            (x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k))) -
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
                (χ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
                (hχ_comp :
                  HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
                (hχ_supp :
                  tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ V0)
                (hzero :
                  ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                      (bvt_F OS lgc n
                          (fun k =>
                            wickRotatePoint
                              (x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k))) -
                        bvt_F OS lgc n
                          (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * χ x = 0) :
                ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * χ x
                  =
                ∫ x : NPointDomain d n, (0 : ℂ) * χ x := by
              have hpoint :
                  ∀ x,
                    Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * χ x =
                    (bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k =>
                          wickRotatePoint
                            (x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k))) -
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * χ x := by
                intro x
                by_cases hx : x ∈ V0
                · rw [hwick_trace_c x hx]
                · have hχx : χ x = 0 := by
                    exact
                      (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                        (fun hxSupp => hx (hχ_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
                  simp [hχx]
              calc
                ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * χ x
                    =
                  ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    (bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k =>
                          wickRotatePoint
                            (x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k))) -
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * χ x := by
                    exact MeasureTheory.integral_congr_ae
                      (Filter.Eventually.of_forall hpoint)
                _ = ∫ x : NPointDomain d n, (0 : ℂ) * χ x := by
                    simpa using hzero
            ```

            Lean-shaped proof of the Wick-trace extraction helper:

            ```lean
            theorem BHW.os45CommonBoundary_wickTrace_zero_of_compactPairing_zero
                [NeZero d]
                (hd : 2 <= d)
                (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
                (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
                (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
                (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
                (ρ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                (Uc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
                (Hc : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
                (hV0_open : IsOpen V0)
                (hHc_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ Hc Uc)
                (hwick_mem_c :
                  ∀ x ∈ V0,
                    BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Uc)
                (hwick_trace_c :
                  ∀ x ∈ V0,
                    Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) =
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k =>
                          wickRotatePoint
                            (x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k))) -
                      bvt_F OS lgc n
                        (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
                (hwick_pairing_zero_all :
                  ∀ (χ : SchwartzNPoint d n),
                    HasCompactSupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
                    tsupport (χ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ V0 ->
                    ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                        (bvt_F OS lgc n
                            (fun k =>
                              wickRotatePoint
                                (x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k))) -
                          bvt_F OS lgc n
                            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * χ x = 0) :
                ∀ x ∈ V0,
                  Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) = 0 := by
              classical
              let g : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ :=
                fun x =>
                  Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)))
              have hwick_cont :
                  Continuous
                    (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                      BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                        (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) :=
                (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ).continuous.comp
                  (BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n))
              have hg_cont : ContinuousOn g V0 := by
                exact hHc_holo.continuousOn.comp
                  hwick_cont.continuousOn hwick_mem_c
              have hzero_cont : ContinuousOn (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => 0) V0 :=
                continuous_const.continuousOn
              have hEq :
                  Set.EqOn g (fun _ : NPointDomain d n => 0) V0 := by
                refine
                  SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn
                    hV0_open hg_cont hzero_cont ?_
                intro χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
                have hzero := hwick_pairing_zero_all χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
                -- Rewrite `g` by the common-boundary trace formula on
                -- `tsupport χ`; outside `V0`, the support hypothesis kills χ.
                simpa [g] using
                  BHW.integral_commonBoundary_wickTrace_eq_zero_of_wickDiff_zero
                    (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V0 ρ Uc Hc
                    hwick_trace_c χ hχ_comp hχ_supp hzero
              intro x hx
              simpa [g] using hEq hx
            ```

            Lean-shaped proof of the connected-chart identity helper:

            ```lean
            theorem BHW.os45CommonBoundary_identity_of_wickTrace_zero
                [NeZero d]
                (hd : 2 <= d)
                (n : Nat)
                (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
                (Uc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ))
                (Hc : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ)
                (hV0_open : IsOpen V0)
                (hV0_nonempty : V0.Nonempty)
                (hUc_open : IsOpen Uc)
                (hUc_conn : IsConnected Uc)
                (hHc_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ Hc Uc)
                (hwick_mem_c :
                  ∀ x ∈ V0,
                    BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n)
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Uc)
                (hHc_zero_wick :
                  ∀ x ∈ V0,
                    Hc (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n)
                      (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) = 0) :
                ∀ z ∈ Uc, Hc z = 0 := by
              classical
              -- The Wick trace in the common chart is a complex-linear
              -- automorphic image of the standard real slice:
              -- `Lρ (realEmbed x) = commonChartρ (wick x)`.
              let Lρ : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≃L[ℂ]
                  (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
                BHW.os45WickRotateCLE (d := d) (n := n) |>.trans
                  (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n)
                    (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n)))
              let H' : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
                fun z => Hc (Lρ z)
              let U' : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
                Lρ ⁻¹' Uc
              have hU'_open : IsOpen U' := hUc_open.preimage Lρ.continuous
              have hU'_conn : IsConnected U' :=
                (Lρ.toHomeomorph.isConnected_preimage (s := Uc)).2
                  hUc_conn
              have hH'_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ H' U' :=
                hHc_holo.comp_differentiableOn
                  Lρ.differentiable.differentiableOn
                  (by intro z hz; exact hz)
              have hreal_mem :
                  ∀ x ∈ V0, BHW.realEmbed x ∈ U' := by
                intro x hx
                simpa [U', Lρ,
                  BHW.os45CommonChartCLE_wickRotateRealConfig_eq] using
                  hwick_mem_c x hx
              have hH'_zero_real :
                  ∀ x ∈ V0, H' (BHW.realEmbed x) = 0 := by
                intro x hx
                simpa [H', Lρ,
                  BHW.os45CommonChartCLE_wickRotateRealConfig_eq] using
                  hHc_zero_wick x hx
              have hH'_zero :
                  ∀ z ∈ U', H' z = 0 :=
                BHW.identity_theorem_totally_real_product
                  (hD_open := hU'_open)
                  (hD_conn := hU'_conn)
                  (hf := hH'_holo)
                  (hV_open := hV0_open)
                  (hV_ne := hV0_nonempty)
                  (hV_sub := hreal_mem)
                  (hf_zero := hH'_zero_real)
              intro z hz
              have hz' : Lρ.symm z ∈ U' := by
                simpa [U'] using hz
              have hzero := hH'_zero (Lρ.symm z) hz'
              simpa [H'] using hzero
            ```

            The new geometric lemmas in the identity helper are finite
            dimensional.  First define the complex-linear Wick-time map:

            ```lean
            noncomputable def BHW.os45WickRotateCLE
                (d n : Nat) :
                (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) ≃L[ℂ]
                  (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
              ContinuousLinearEquiv.piCongrRight fun _ =>
                ContinuousLinearEquiv.piCongrRight fun μ =>
                  if _ : μ = 0 then
                    ContinuousLinearEquiv.smulLeft
                      (Units.mk0 Complex.I Complex.I_ne_zero)
                  else
                    ContinuousLinearEquiv.refl ℂ ℂ

            @[simp] theorem BHW.os45WickRotateCLE_realEmbed
                (x : NPointDomain d n) :
                BHW.os45WickRotateCLE (d := d) (n := n)
                    (BHW.realEmbed x) =
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))
            ```

            In implementation, the unit in the time coordinate should be the
            standard complex unit `⟨Complex.I, by simp⟩`; the display above is
            shorthand for that `Units.mk0` term.  Then the chart calculation
            needed by the identity theorem is:

            ```lean
            @[simp] theorem BHW.os45CommonChartCLE_wickRotateRealConfig_eq
                (ρ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                (x : NPointDomain d n) :
                ((BHW.os45WickRotateCLE (d := d) (n := n)).trans
                  (BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ))
                    (BHW.realEmbed x) =
                  BHW.os45CommonChartCLE (d := d) (n := n) ρ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))
            ```

            The connectedness transfer uses the existing Mathlib theorem
            `Homeomorph.isConnected_preimage`:

            ```lean
            have hU'_conn : IsConnected U' :=
              (Lρ.toHomeomorph.isConnected_preimage (s := Uc)).2 hUc_conn
            ```

            These are finite-dimensional chart calculations, not QFT or
            locality inputs.

         4. Read `Hc = 0` on the real trace using `hreal_trace_c` and integrate
            pointwise on `hChart.V0`; outside `hChart.V0`, the test vanishes by
            `hψ_supp` and `notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq`.  The final line
            is just

            ```lean
            refine MeasureTheory.integral_congr_ae
              (Filter.Eventually.of_forall ?_)
            intro x
            by_cases hx : x ∈ hChart.V0
            · have hreal_zero :
                  BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                      (BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (τ k))) -
                    BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.realEmbed x) = 0 :=
                by simpa [hreal_trace_c x hx, τ] using
                  hHc_zero (commonChart (BHW.realEmbed x)) (hreal_mem_c x hx)
              rw [sub_eq_zero.mp hreal_zero]
            · have hψx : ψ x = 0 := ...
              simp [hψx, τ]
            ```

         This corrected theorem replaces the retired individual
         source-branch-to-Schwinger and lift-to-source comparison surfaces.
         It is the exact OS §4.5 common-boundary branch-difference step needed
         by the compact Figure-2-4 real-patch packet.  A proof term that
         proves either real branch separately equal to `OS.S`, invokes
         `SourceScalarRepresentativeData`, derives pointwise adjacent Wick
         equality first, uses final `bvt_W` locality, uses PET independence,
         or applies `extendF_eq_boundary_value*` directly at
         `realEmbed x`/`realEmbed (x ∘ τ)` is off route.

         Proof transcript for the two generic subtheorems.  First,
         `BHW.jostRuelle_realPatch_eqOn_of_distributionEq` applies
         `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`
         on `D.jostPatch` to the two real restrictions
         `x ↦ D.ordinaryBranch (BHW.realEmbed x)` and
         `x ↦ D.adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x)`.  Their continuity on
         `D.jostPatch` is
         obtained by composing `D.ordinary_holo.continuousOn` and
         `D.adjacent_holo.continuousOn` with the continuous real embedding,
         using `D.jostPatch_realEmbed_mem` as the `MapsTo` proof.  If this
         generic theorem is implemented in a lower BHW file, do not import the
         Figure-2-4 file only to use
         `BHW.continuous_realEmbedNPoint`; either move that elementary
         continuity helper to the lower BHW layer or prove it locally by
         `continuous_pi` and `Complex.continuous_ofReal`.  The compact-test
         pairing input is exactly `D.realBoundary_eq`; no OS axiom, scalar
         representative, PET independence, or local EOW statement enters this
         extraction.

         Lean-shaped proof of the real-patch extraction, matching the
         production signature of
         `SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn`:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_realPatch_eqOn_of_distributionEq
             ... := by
           classical
           let g : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ :=
             fun x => D.ordinaryBranch (BHW.realEmbed x)
           let h : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ :=
             fun x => D.adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x)
           have hReal_cont :
               Continuous
                 (BHW.realEmbed :
                   NPointDomain d n -> (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)) := by
             refine continuous_pi ?_
             intro k
             refine continuous_pi ?_
             intro μ
             exact Complex.continuous_ofReal.comp
               ((continuous_apply μ).comp (continuous_apply k))
           have hord_cont_on_Ω :
               ContinuousOn D.ordinaryBranch D.Ω :=
             D.ordinary_holo.continuousOn
           have hadj_cont_on_Ω :
               ContinuousOn D.adjacentBranch D.Ω :=
             D.adjacent_holo.continuousOn
           have hg_cont : ContinuousOn g D.jostPatch := by
             refine hord_cont_on_Ω.comp hReal_cont.continuousOn ?_
             intro x hx
             exact D.jostPatch_realEmbed_mem x hx
           have hh_cont : ContinuousOn h D.jostPatch := by
             refine hadj_cont_on_Ω.comp hReal_cont.continuousOn ?_
             intro x hx
             exact D.jostPatch_realEmbed_mem x hx
           have hEqOn : Set.EqOn g h D.jostPatch := by
             refine
               SCV.eqOn_open_of_compactSupport_schwartz_integral_eq_of_continuousOn
                 D.jostPatch_open hg_cont hh_cont ?_
             intro χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
             exact D.realBoundary_eq χ hχ_comp hχ_supp
           intro x hx
           exact hEqOn hx
         ```

         Second,
         `BHW.jostRuelle_branch_eqOn_connectedDomain` applies the product
         totally-real identity theorem
         `BHW.identity_theorem_totally_real_product` to
         `H z := D.ordinaryBranch z - D.adjacentBranch z` on `D.Ω`.  The
         hypotheses are `D.Ω_open`, `D.Ω_connected`,
         `D.ordinary_holo.sub D.adjacent_holo`, `D.jostPatch_open`,
         `D.jostPatch_nonempty`, `D.jostPatch_realEmbed_mem`, and the real
         zero statement supplied by
         `BHW.jostRuelle_realPatch_eqOn_of_distributionEq`.  The
         `D.jostPatch_jost`, `D.ordinary_lorentzInvariant`, and
         `D.adjacent_lorentzInvariant` fields are not decorative: they are
         the audit trail proving that the two branches supplied by the OS-I
         producer are the BHW/Jost branches on the correct real environment.
         The final connected-domain identity is the ordinary holomorphic
         uniqueness step.

         Lean-shaped proof of the connected-domain branch identity:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_branch_eqOn_connectedDomain
             ... := by
           classical
           let H : (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) -> ℂ :=
             fun z => D.ordinaryBranch z - D.adjacentBranch z
           have hH_holo : DifferentiableOn ℂ H D.Ω := by
             simpa [H] using D.ordinary_holo.sub D.adjacent_holo
           have hRealEq :
               Set.EqOn
                 (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                   D.ordinaryBranch (BHW.realEmbed x))
                 (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
                   D.adjacentBranch (BHW.realEmbed x))
                 D.jostPatch :=
             BHW.jostRuelle_realPatch_eqOn_of_distributionEq
               (d := d) hd n φ hφ_comp D
           have hH_zero_real : ∀ x ∈ D.jostPatch, H (BHW.realEmbed x) = 0 := by
             intro x hx
             have hxEq := hRealEq hx
             simpa [H] using sub_eq_zero.mpr hxEq
           have hH_zero_Ω : ∀ z ∈ D.Ω, H z = 0 :=
             BHW.identity_theorem_totally_real_product
               (hD_open := D.Ω_open)
               (hD_conn := D.Ω_connected)
               (hf := hH_holo)
               (hV_open := D.jostPatch_open)
               (hV_ne := D.jostPatch_nonempty)
               (hV_sub := D.jostPatch_realEmbed_mem)
               (hf_zero := hH_zero_real)
           intro z hz
           exact sub_eq_zero.mp (by simpa [H] using hH_zero_Ω z hz)
         ```

         The proof of
         `BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary` then sets
         `hΩeq := BHW.jostRuelle_branch_eqOn_connectedDomain ... D`,
         proves equality of the two integrands pointwise, and closes by
         `MeasureTheory.integral_congr_ae`.  At a point `x`, if
         `(φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) x = 0`, both integrands vanish.  If
         not, `Function.mem_support.mpr` and `subset_tsupport _` put `x` in
         `tsupport φ`, so `D.lift_mem_of_support x hx_tsupport` lets `hΩeq`
         identify the two branch values at `D.lift x`.  Thus the generic
         compact theorem has no hidden Figure-2-4 or OS-specific content.
         The proof does not know what `bvt_F` is and does not evaluate
         `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.branch_eq`.

         Lean-shaped proof of the final generic compact theorem:

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
             ... := by
           classical
           have hΩeq :
               Set.EqOn D.ordinaryBranch D.adjacentBranch D.Ω :=
             BHW.jostRuelle_branch_eqOn_connectedDomain
               (d := d) hd n φ hφ_comp D
           refine MeasureTheory.integral_congr_ae
             (Filter.Eventually.of_forall ?_)
           intro x
           by_cases hφx : (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) x = 0
           · simp [hφx]
           · have hx_support :
                 x ∈ Function.support (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) :=
               Function.mem_support.mpr hφx
             have hx_tsupport :
                 x ∈ tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) :=
               subset_tsupport _ hx_support
             exact congrArg (fun c => c * (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) x)
               (hΩeq (D.lift x) (D.lift_mem_of_support x hx_tsupport))
         ```

         With the generic uniqueness pieces, the public canonical-lift
         compact theorem has no hidden placeholder as a theorem landing in
         `OS.S n ψZ`.  It is still only one output of the OS45 compact
         producer: the source-patch PET-overlap theorem additionally needs
         the direct OS-I/BHW-Jost branch-difference theorem above.

         ```lean
         theorem BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
             ... := by
           classical
           let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
           intro φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
           let φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
             ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
               (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
               (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx))
               φ hφ_supp⟩
           let ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
             permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
           have D :
               BHW.OS45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData
                 hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost hV_ET hV_swapET hChart
                 φ hφ_comp hφ_supp :=
             BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45
               (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
               hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
               φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
           have huniq :
               ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                     (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
                 =
               ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   D.jr.adjacentBranch
                     (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x :=
             by
               have huniq0 :=
                 BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary
                   (d := d) hd n φ hφ_comp D.jr
               simpa [D.jr_lift_eq] using huniq0
           have hord :
               ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                     (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
                 =
               ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                   D.jr.ordinaryBranch
                     (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x := by
             exact integral_congr_ae (by
               filter_upwards with x
               by_cases hx : x ∈ hChart.V0
               · rw [D.ordinary_eq_extendF_on_lift x hx]
               · have hφx : φ x = 0 := by
                   exact
                     (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                       (fun hxSupp => hx (hφ_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
                 simp [hφx])
           exact hord.trans
             (huniq.trans D.adjacent_lift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger)
         ```

         The remaining non-mechanical source proof for the canonical-lift
         theorem is the producer
         `BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45` plus the
         standard generic Jost/Ruelle uniqueness theorem above.  The remaining
         non-mechanical source proof for the corrected Figure-2-4 real-patch
         packet is separate: prove
         `BHW.os45Figure24_sourcePatch_pairing_eq_swappedSourcePatch_of_OSI45`
         by the OS45 common-boundary branch-difference route.  Neither theorem
         may be introduced as an axiom or production `sorry`; if the common
         boundary envelope or its Wick-trace zero extraction is not proved
         before the Lean port, the source-patch compact packet is not
         implementation-ready.

         Local production-code audit, 2026-05-01: a search of
         `OSReconstruction/` finds no declarations named
         `BHW.os45CanonicalAdjacentBranchBoundaryData_of_OSI45`,
         `BHW.jostRuelle_uniqueContinuation_compactBoundary`,
         `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`,
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24`,
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq`,
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq`, or
         `BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq`.
         These names are therefore still proof-doc targets on this branch,
         not checked Lean inputs.  Downstream raw/canonical transports below
         may be implemented only after these producer surfaces are either
         proved locally or replaced by already checked sorry-free support with
         the same OS I §4.5/BHW/Jost content.

      Strict-route status of the next raw/canonical transport block.  The
      following statements with
      `hRep : BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n)`
      are retained only as the archived ordinary pure-Gram consumer form.  They
      are not active theorem-2 Lean targets on the OS I §4.5 proper-complex
      route.  On the active route, the canonical compact theorem above feeds
      the oriented sequence

      ```text
      os45Figure24_sourcePatch_pairing_eq_swappedSourcePatch_of_OSI45
        -> OS45CompactFigure24WickPairingEq
        -> sourceOrientedAdjacentTubeAnchor_of_compactWickPairingEq
        -> os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedRealSeed_of_compactWickPairingEq
        -> os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSourceEq_of_realSeed
        -> os45AdjacentSPrimeOrientedSeedFigure24Path_of_compactWickPairingEq
        -> os45OneBranchOrientedSourceEq_sourceInput_adjacent
        -> os45BranchHorizontalOrientedSourceGermAt_of_figure24_adjacent
        -> os45_adjacent_commonBoundaryEnvelope

      The ordinary raw/canonical block below may be ported only on the
      conditional full-component Hall-Wightman fork, after
      `BHW.sourceScalarRepresentativeData_bvt_F` is genuinely available.  It
      must not be used to bypass the oriented real-seed theorem, the oriented
      double-domain/corridor proof, or the branch-difference compact theorem.
      In particular, the presence of a checked canonical lift equality
      `= OS.S n ψZ` does not authorize an ordinary
      `SourceScalarRepresentativeData.branch_eq` proof on the strict route.

      This is the only non-mechanical input in
      `BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24`.
      The following raw/canonical transport is recorded only as a downstream
      consequence after the local scalar source equality has produced the raw
      pointwise branch theorem:

      ```lean
      have hBHWJost_raw :
          ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * φ x
            =
          OS.S n ψZ := by
        exact
          BHW.os45SPrime_permutedWickExtendF_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
            φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
      ```

      The checked geometric transport from raw adjacent Wick coordinates to
      the canonical Figure-2-4 lift is then:

      ```lean
      have hcanonical_to_raw :
          ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
            =
          ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
              BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) * φ x := by
        exact integral_congr_ae (by
          filter_upwards with x
          by_cases hx : x ∈ hChart.V0
          · have hnorm :
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                    (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
                  =
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (BHW.permAct (d := d) τ
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) :=
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_extendF_eq_permutedWick_zero
                (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hChart x hx
            rw [hnorm]
          · have hφx : φ x = 0 := by
              exact
                (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                  (fun hxSupp => hx (hφ_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
            simp [hφx])
      exact hcanonical_to_raw.trans hBHWJost_raw
      ```

      This downstream transport is the only allowed way to use the raw
      adjacent Wick section after the source equality has been proved.  In
      particular, the already implemented
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value` and
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value_ET` helpers are insufficient here:
      their conclusions concern real-embedded boundary configurations
      `BHW.realEmbed x`, while the adjacent Wick section
      `BHW.permAct τ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))` is a complex
      ordinary-extended-tube point selected by the Figure-2-4/BHW route.  A
      proof that applies `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube`,
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value`, or
      `BHW.extendF_eq_boundary_value_ET` directly at `wick (x ∘ τ)` or
      `permAct τ (wick x)` is therefore a route error unless it first proves
      the missing adjacent branch membership and boundary theorem above,
      which is exactly the OS-I §4.5 content.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          let Φτ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          ∀ (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_hφ_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
            (hφ_supp :
              tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0),
            let φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
                (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
                (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) φ
                hφ_supp⟩
            let ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
              permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
              =
            OS.S n ψZ
      ```

      Mathematical role of this theorem: it is the source-coordinate form of
      the canonical-lift boundary gate.  It says that the adjacent
      source-coordinate branch
      `Z ↦ hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ Z)`, pulled back along the
      Wick real section, has the compact boundary distribution selected by
      the Euclidean permuted test `ψZ`.  It is derived from the
      canonical-lift theorem by source-Gram rewriting, not used to prove that
      theorem.

      The implementation-level derivation obligations are:

      1. Build `φZ` and
         `ψZ := permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ`.
         `OS.E3_symmetric` supplies only the Euclidean symmetry
         `OS.S n φZ = OS.S n ψZ`; it is not used as pointwise permutation
         invariance of `bvt_F`.
      2. Prove the source branch is holomorphic on the selected chart:
         `hRep.Phi_holomorphic.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram τ`, restricted
         to the chart domain by `hChart.double_mem`, then composed with
         `sourceMinkowskiGram` on `hChart.Usrc` by
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      3. Rewrite the source pullback to the canonical lift on `hChart.V0`
         using `hRep.branch_eq` and `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram`; outside
         `hChart.V0`, the compact-support hypothesis gives `φ x = 0`.
      4. Apply the canonical-lift source-boundary theorem
         `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.
      5. Use `bvt_euclidean_restriction` only to rewrite the selected ACR(1)
         compact germ as `OS.S n ψZ`.  The finite permutation change of
         variables remains the checked one from
         `os45_adjacent_euclideanEdge_pairing_eq_on_timeSector`.
      6. The proof does not evaluate
         `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` at `wick (x ∘ τ)` or
         `permAct τ (wick x)`.  The adjacent Wick section is not obtained from
         ordered-positive-time forward-tube membership; it enters the ordinary
         extended tube only through the Figure-2-4 canonical lift.

      The canonical-lift theorem above is the mathematically primitive
      boundary statement in this block.  For Lean bookkeeping it is useful to
      isolate the source-coordinate ACR-kernel form below: the right hand side is
      exactly the `bvt_euclidean_restriction` pairing for the permuted
      zero-diagonal test `ψZ`.  Thus this theorem and
      `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` differ
      only by the already checked OS-II ACR boundary-value rewrite.  Neither
      theorem may be treated as proved by that rewrite alone; the hard
      content is still the canonical Figure-2-4 lift pairing, transported to
      the source branch on the left by `hRep.branch_eq` and
      `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram`.

      The isolated ACR-kernel form is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          (hRep :
            BHW.SourceScalarRepresentativeData
              (d := d) n (bvt_F OS lgc n))
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
          let Φτ :=
            fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
              hRep.Phi
                (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
          ∀ (φ : SchwartzNPoint d n)
            (_hφ_comp :
              HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ))
            (hφ_supp :
              tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ hChart.V0),
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
              =
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k))
      ```

      Proof transcript required for this theorem:

      1. Define
         `φZ := ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap ...⟩` and
         `ψZ := permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ`.
         The right side is exactly `OS.S n ψZ` rewritten by the checked
         theorem `bvt_euclidean_restriction`; this uses only the definitional
         equation
         `(permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz τ.symm φZ).1 x =
           φ (fun k => x (τ k))`.
      2. The left side is the compact Wick real-section pairing of the
         Hall-Wightman source branch
         `Z ↦ hRep.Phi (sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ Z)` restricted to
         `hChart.Usrc`.  Its holomorphy is obtained by
         `hRep.Phi_holomorphic.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram τ`,
         `hChart.double_mem`, and
         `SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      3. On `hChart.V0`, the deterministic Figure-2-4 lift gives the
         ordinary extended-tube realization of that same scalar point:
         `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram x hx 0` rewrites
         `sourceMinkowskiGram (hChart.adjLift x 0)` to
         `sourcePermuteComplexGram τ (sourceMinkowskiGram (wick x))`.
         Therefore `hRep.branch_eq` rewrites the left integrand to
         `extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n) (hChart.adjLift x 0) * φ x`.  Outside
         `hChart.V0`, `φ x = 0` by `hφ_supp`, so the rewrite is valid under
         the integral without choosing any existential `Δ_x`.
      4. The remaining equality

         ```lean
         ∫ x, BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
           =
         OS.S n ψZ
         ```

         is the genuine OS-I §4.5 Figure-2-4 `S'_n` boundary theorem.  It
         says that the rotated adjacent ordinary-extended-tube realization
         carried by the selected chart is the BHW continuation of the same
         symmetric Euclidean compact-test branch selected by E3.  This is not
         local EOW, not final Wightman locality, not global PET branch
         independence, and not a pointwise permutation-symmetry shortcut.
      5. Step 4 is supplied directly by
         `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger`.  The
         ACR-kernel theorem is therefore derived from the canonical compact
         theorem by `hRep.branch_eq`, `hChart.adjLift_sourceGram`, and the
         Euclidean restriction for `ψZ`; it does not call the raw pointwise
         branch theorem or any raw compact theorem.

      The current adjacent proof-document gap is therefore the canonical-lift
      OS-I §4.5/Bargmann-Hall-Wightman/Jost compact-boundary transcript, not a
      specialized raw adjacent-Wick pointwise comparison.  The displayed raw
      comparison and raw Schwinger-valued theorems are downstream
      deterministic transports after the local scalar source equality exists.
      Adding any of these statements to Lean as a wrapper, `sorry`, `admit`,
      or unapproved axiom before the canonical compact proof is audited would
      violate the implementation gate.

      Once the canonical-lift compact theorem is proved, the ACR-kernel form
      is mechanical:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary ... := by
        classical
        let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
        let Φτ :=
          fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        intro φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
        let φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) φ hφ_supp⟩
        let ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
        have hsource_to_lift :
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
              =
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x := by
          exact integral_congr_ae (by
            filter_upwards with x
            by_cases hx : x ∈ hChart.V0
            · have hET :
                  hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval) ∈
                    BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
                hChart.adjLift_mem_extendedTube x hx (0 : unitInterval)
              have hGram :
                  BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                      (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))
                    =
                  BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                    (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))) := by
                simpa [τ, BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero] using
                  hChart.adjLift_sourceGram x hx (0 : unitInterval)
              have hbranch :
                  hRep.Phi
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                        (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)))
                    =
                  BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                    (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) :=
                hRep.branch_eq _ hET
              calc
                Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
                    =
                  hRep.Phi
                      (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                        (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval))) * φ x := by
                      simp [Φτ, hGram]
                _ =
                  BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                    (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x := by
                      rw [hbranch]
            · have hφx : φ x = 0 := by
                exact
                  (notMem_tsupport_iff_eventuallyEq.mp
                    (fun hxSupp => hx (hφ_supp hxSupp))).self_of_nhds
              simp [hφx])
        have hlift :
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                  (hChart.adjLift x (0 : unitInterval)) * φ x
              =
            OS.S n ψZ :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hChart
            φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
        have hψ :
            OS.S n ψZ =
              ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)) := by
          simpa [ψZ, φZ, τ] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ψZ
        exact hsource_to_lift.trans (hlift.trans hψ)
      ```

      Lean-shaped proof skeleton for the source-pullback theorem after that
      comparison is available:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger ... := by
        classical
        let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
        let Φτ :=
          fun z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ =>
            hRep.Phi
              (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        intro φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
        let φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) φ hφ_supp⟩
        let ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
        have hsrc_acr :
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
              =
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)) := by
          exact
            BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
              hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hRep hChart
              φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
        have hψ :
            OS.S n ψZ =
              ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)) := by
          simpa [ψZ, φZ, τ] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ψZ
        exact hsrc_acr.trans hψ.symm
      ```

      The theorem
      `BHW.os45SPrime_canonicalLift_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` is the
      upstream OS-I §4.5/BHW/Jost compact-boundary proof obligation for the
      adjacent trace theorem.  It is deliberately earlier than the raw
      adjacent-Wick pointwise branch theorem; deriving it from that pointwise
      theorem would be circular.  The displayed
      `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_acrPermutedBoundary` theorem
      is a genuine derived theorem, not a wrapper: it removes only the
      already-checked Euclidean functional rewrite and the
      `hRep.branch_eq`/Figure-2-4 source-Gram rewrite from the
      Hall-Wightman comparison.

      The theorem
      `BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24`
      is then a short composition:

      1. apply
         `BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger` to get
         the source-side pairing as `OS.S n ψZ`;
      2. use `bvt_euclidean_restriction` for `ψZ`;
      3. change variables by the checked finite permutation invariance of
         product Lebesgue measure, using the public theorem
         `integral_perm_eq_self` and `Equiv.swap_inv`, to rewrite the result
         as the raw adjacent Wick pairing.  Do not depend on the private
         helper `integral_perm_eq_self_locality` from
         `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45.lean`; it is only the local proof helper
         for the checked edge theorem.

      Lean-shaped proof of the composition:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24 ... := by
        classical
        let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
        intro φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
        let φZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          ⟨φ, zeroDiagonal_of_tsupport_subset_jostOverlap
            (d := d) (n := n) hChart.V0
            (fun x hx => hV_jost x (hChart.V0_sub hx)) φ hφ_supp⟩
        let ψZ : ZeroDiagonalSchwartz d n :=
          permuteZeroDiagonalSchwartz (d := d) (n := n) τ.symm φZ
        have hsrc :
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x =
              OS.S n ψZ :=
          BHW.os45SPrime_sourcePullback_pairing_eq_permutedSchwinger
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
            hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hRep hChart
            φ hφ_comp hφ_supp
        have hψ :
            OS.S n ψZ =
              ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)) := by
          simpa [ψZ, φZ, τ] using
            bvt_euclidean_restriction (d := d) OS lgc n ψZ
        have hchange :
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k))
              =
            ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                bvt_F OS lgc n
                  (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) * φ x := by
          simpa [τ, BHW.permAct_wickRotatePoint, Equiv.swap_inv] using
            (integral_perm_eq_self
              (d := d) (n := n) τ
              (fun x =>
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) *
                  φ (fun k => x (τ k)))).symm
        exact hsrc.trans (hψ.trans hchange)
      ```

      The distributional theorem above is the gate for the adjacent
      `S'_n` scalarization chart: production Lean must start at its lower
      producers, not at the scalarization wrapper.  Any proof that replaces it
      by `hRep.branch_eq` followed by
      `BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube` at `wick (x ∘ τ)` is rejected: it proves
      the wrong membership statement.

      Lean-shaped pseudocode for the scalarization chart:

      The helper named below,
      `BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at`, is the Lean-facing packaging of the
      already selected Figure-2-4 source patch at `x0`.  It is not an analytic
      equality theorem.  Its fields are exactly: an open `V0 ⊆ V` with
      `x0 ∈ V0`, an open connected nonempty source neighborhood `Usrc`, Wick
      membership of `V0` in `Usrc`, the reverse Wick real-section
      equivalence
      `∀ x, (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc ↔ x ∈ V0`,
      double-scalar-domain membership of every source Gram point in `Usrc`,
      forward-tube membership of the identity Wick configuration on `V0`, and
      the deterministic canonical Figure-2-4 adjacent lift on `V0`.
      It deliberately does not carry adjacent raw-Wick forward-tube
      membership.  The lift fields are what prevent the distributional proof
      from integrating arbitrary existential `Δ_x` witnesses.  The continuity
      input used below must be made a
      public finite-coordinate helper
      `BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n) :
      Continuous (fun x : NPointDomain d n => fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))`;
      this helper is now checked in
      `OSToWightmanLocalityOS45BHWJost.lean` by `continuous_pi`,
      coordinate projections, and the coordinate definition of
      `wickRotatePoint`.

      Lean-facing support surfaces for the helper:

      ```lean
      def BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse
          (d n : Nat)
          (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :
          NPointDomain d n :=
        fun k μ => if hμ : μ = 0 then (z k 0).im else (z k μ).re

      theorem BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse_wickRotateRealConfig
          (d n : Nat) (x : NPointDomain d n) :
          BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) = x

      theorem BHW.continuous_wickRealSectionLeftInverse
          (d n : Nat) :
          Continuous (BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n)

      theorem BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig
          (d n : Nat) :
          Continuous
            (fun x : NPointDomain d n =>
              fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k))

      theorem BHW.convex_wickPreimage_ball
          (d n : Nat) (x0 : NPointDomain d n) (r : ℝ) :
          Convex ℝ
            {x : NPointDomain d n |
              (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈
                Metric.ball (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) r}

      theorem BHW.isConnected_wickPreimage_ball
          (d n : Nat) (x0 : NPointDomain d n) {r : ℝ}
          (hr : 0 < r) :
          IsConnected
            {x : NPointDomain d n |
              (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈
                Metric.ball (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) r}

      theorem BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage
          (d n : Nat)
          {Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hΩ_open : IsOpen Ω)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hz0Ω :
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) ∈ Ω)
          {V : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hx0V : x0 ∈ V) :
          ∃ (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)),
            IsOpen V0 ∧ IsConnected V0 ∧ x0 ∈ V0 ∧ V0 ⊆ V ∧
            IsOpen Usrc ∧ IsConnected Usrc ∧
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) ∈ Usrc ∧
            Usrc ⊆ Ω ∧
            (∀ x : NPointDomain d n,
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc ↔ x ∈ V0)

      Lean-shaped proof of the connected source-neighborhood helper:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.convex_wickPreimage_ball
          (d n : Nat) (x0 : NPointDomain d n) (r : ℝ) :
          Convex ℝ
            {x : NPointDomain d n |
              (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈
                Metric.ball (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) r} := by
        -- The Wick real section is an affine real-linear embedding:
        -- time coordinates are multiplied by `Complex.I`, spatial coordinates
        -- are included as real complex numbers.  Hence it carries real
        -- segments in `NPointDomain` to real segments in the complex
        -- configuration space.
        intro x hx y hy a b ha hb hab
        have hW_affine :
            (fun k : Fin n =>
                wickRotatePoint ((a • x + b • y) k))
              =
            a • (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k)) +
            b • (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (y k)) := by
          ext k μ
          simp [wickRotatePoint, Pi.smul_apply, Pi.add_apply,
            smul_add, mul_add]
        have hseg :
            a • (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k)) +
              b • (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (y k)) ∈
                Metric.ball (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) r :=
          (convex_ball
            (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) r)
            hx hy ha hb hab
        simpa [hW_affine] using hseg

      theorem BHW.isConnected_wickPreimage_ball
          (d n : Nat) (x0 : NPointDomain d n) {r : ℝ}
          (hr : 0 < r) :
          IsConnected
            {x : NPointDomain d n |
              (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈
                Metric.ball (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) r} := by
        have hconv := BHW.convex_wickPreimage_ball d n x0 r
        have hne :
            ({x : NPointDomain d n |
              (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈
                Metric.ball (fun k : Fin n => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) r}).Nonempty := by
          exact ⟨x0, Metric.mem_ball_self hr⟩
        exact hconv.isConnected hne

      theorem BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage
          (d n : Nat)
          {Ω : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)}
          (hΩ_open : IsOpen Ω)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hz0Ω :
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) ∈ Ω)
          {V : Set (NPointDomain d n)}
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hx0V : x0 ∈ V) :
          ∃ (V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n))
            (Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)),
            IsOpen V0 ∧ IsConnected V0 ∧ x0 ∈ V0 ∧ V0 ⊆ V ∧
            IsOpen Usrc ∧ IsConnected Usrc ∧
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)) ∈ Usrc ∧
            Usrc ⊆ Ω ∧
            (∀ x : NPointDomain d n,
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc ↔ x ∈ V0) := by
        let L := BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n
        let z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        let W : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
          Ω ∩ L ⁻¹' V
        have hW_open : IsOpen W :=
          hΩ_open.inter
            (hV_open.preimage
              (BHW.continuous_wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n))
        have hLz0 : L z0 = x0 := by
          simpa [L, z0] using
            BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse_wickRotateRealConfig d n x0
        have hz0W : z0 ∈ W := by
          exact ⟨hz0Ω, by simpa [hLz0] using hx0V⟩
        rcases Metric.mem_nhds_iff.mp (hW_open.mem_nhds hz0W) with
          ⟨r, hr_pos, hr_sub⟩
        let Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) := Metric.ball z0 r
        let V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n) :=
          {x | (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc}
        have hV0_open : IsOpen V0 := by
          simpa [V0, Usrc] using
            Metric.isOpen_ball.preimage
              (BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig (d := d) (n := n))
        have hV0_conn : IsConnected V0 := by
          simpa [V0, Usrc, z0] using
            BHW.isConnected_wickPreimage_ball d n x0 hr_pos
        have hx0V0 : x0 ∈ V0 := by
          simpa [V0, Usrc, z0] using Metric.mem_ball_self hr_pos
        have hV0_sub : V0 ⊆ V := by
          intro x hx
          have hLV : L (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ V :=
            (hr_sub (by simpa [V0, Usrc] using hx)).2
          have hLwick : L (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) = x := by
            simpa [L] using
              BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse_wickRotateRealConfig d n x
          simpa [hLwick] using hLV
        exact ⟨V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
          Metric.isOpen_ball, isConnected_ball hr_pos,
          by simpa [Usrc, z0] using Metric.mem_ball_self hr_pos,
          by
            intro z hz
            exact (hr_sub hz).1,
          by
            intro x
            rfl⟩
      ```

      This helper is pure finite-dimensional topology.  The only imported
      analysis beyond continuity of the Wick real section is
      `Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Module.Connected`, for `isConnected_ball`,
      and `Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.PathConnected`, for
      `Convex.isConnected`.  The real patch connectedness is not inferred
      from connectedness of `Usrc`: it is proved separately because `V0` is
      the Wick-real-section preimage of the selected metric ball, hence a
      convex finite-dimensional real set.  The proof does not use BHW
      analyticity, branch equality, or Figure-2-4 geometry.

      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.sourceMinkowskiGram_preimage_open
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          IsOpen {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ |
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈ U}

      Lean-shaped proof of the relative-open Gram-preimage helper:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.sourceMinkowskiGram_preimage_open
          (d n : Nat)
          {U : Set (Fin n -> Fin n -> ℂ)}
          (hU : BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n U) :
          IsOpen {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ |
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈ U} := by
        rcases hU with ⟨U0, hU0_open, hUeq⟩
        have hpre :
            {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ |
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈ U} =
            {z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ |
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈ U0} := by
          ext z
          constructor
          · intro hz
            rw [hUeq] at hz
            exact hz.1
          · intro hz
            rw [hUeq]
            exact ⟨hz, ⟨z, rfl⟩⟩
        rw [hpre]
        exact hU0_open.preimage
          (BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous
      ```

      The only mathematical point in this helper is definitional:
      `sourceComplexGramVariety d n` is the full range of
      `sourceMinkowskiGram d n`, so the variety conjunct in a relatively open
      scalar set is automatic after precomposition with `sourceMinkowskiGram`.
      The continuity input is the existing
      `BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram`; the source chart file must be
      imported rather than reproving polynomial continuity locally.

      structure BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (x0 : NPointDomain d n) where
        V0 : Set (NPointDomain d n)
        Usrc : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ)
        V0_open : IsOpen V0
        V0_connected : IsConnected V0
        x0_mem : x0 ∈ V0
        V0_sub : V0 ⊆ V
        Usrc_open : IsOpen Usrc
        Usrc_connected : IsConnected Usrc
        Usrc_nonempty : Usrc.Nonempty
        wick_mem :
          ∀ x, x ∈ V0 -> (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc
        wick_realSection_iff :
          ∀ x : NPointDomain d n,
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc ↔ x ∈ V0
        double_mem :
          ∀ z, z ∈ Usrc ->
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈
              BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n
                (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩)
        wick_id_forwardTube :
          ∀ x, x ∈ V0 ->
            (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n
        adjLift :
          NPointDomain d n -> unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ
        adjLift_def :
          ∀ x t,
            adjLift x t =
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd
                (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩) x t
        adjLift_continuousOn :
          ContinuousOn
            (fun p : NPointDomain d n × unitInterval =>
              adjLift p.1 p.2)
            (V0 ×ˢ (Set.univ : Set unitInterval))
        adjLift_mem_extendedTube :
          ∀ x, x ∈ V0 -> ∀ t, adjLift x t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n
        adjLift_sourceGram :
          ∀ x, x ∈ V0 -> ∀ t,
            BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (adjLift x t) =
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n
                (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩)
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                  (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t))

      theorem BHW.closure_sourceChart_sub_closure_input
          [NeZero d]
          {hd : 2 <= d}
          {OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d}
          {lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS}
          {n : Nat} {i : Fin n} {hi : i.val + 1 < n}
          {V : Set (NPointDomain d n)} {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hChart :
            BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0) :
          closure hChart.V0 ⊆ closure V :=
        closure_mono hChart.V0_sub

      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              x ∈ EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n) 1)
          (hV_swap_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                EuclideanOrderedPositiveTimeSector (d := d) (n := n)
                  (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩))
          (hV_adjLift_ET :
            let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) :=
              Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              ∀ t,
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x t ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          {x0 : NPointDomain d n}
          (hx0V : x0 ∈ V) :
          BHW.OS45Figure24SourceChartAt hd OS lgc n i hi V x0
      ```

      The proof of
      `BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage` is pure
      finite-dimensional topology.  Let
      `L := BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n` and
      `z0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`.  By
      `BHW.wickRealSectionLeftInverse_wickRotateRealConfig`, `L z0 = x0`.
      The set `Ω ∩ L ⁻¹' V` is open and contains `z0`, using
      `hΩ_open`, `hV_open.preimage
      (BHW.continuous_wickRealSectionLeftInverse d n)`, and `hx0V`.
      Choose a metric ball around `z0` contained in this intersection by
      `Metric.mem_nhds_iff`; take `Usrc` to be that ball and
      `V0 := {x | wick x ∈ Usrc}`.  Its openness is the preimage of
      `Metric.isOpen_ball` under `BHW.continuous_wickRotateRealConfig`; its
      connectedness is the separate convexity lemma
      `BHW.isConnected_wickPreimage_ball`; it contains `x0`; and its
      inclusion in `V` follows by applying the left inverse `L` to the
      ball-subset conclusion.  The complex ball `Usrc` is connected by the
      global Mathlib theorem `isConnected_ball`.  No analytic theorem is used
      in this helper.

      With this helper the source chart is constructed as follows.  Put
      `τ := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩`,
      `z0 := fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)`, and
      `Ωdouble := {z | BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈
      BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n τ}`.  Openness of `Ωdouble`
      is the preimage of the relatively open double scalar domain: use
      `BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain`
      together with `BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected` and
      `(BHW.contDiff_sourceMinkowskiGram d n).continuous`.  Membership
      `z0 ∈ Ωdouble` is the Figure-2-4 source realization at the selected
      base point: the identity Wick trace lies in the ordinary forward tube by
      `hV_ordered x0 hx0V`, while the adjacent permuted source Gram is
      realized by the canonical lift
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd τ x0 0`, whose extended-tube membership
      is supplied by `hV_adjLift_ET` and whose scalar Gram is identified by
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram`.  Apply
      `BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage` to
      `Ωdouble` and `V`; it returns both `Usrc` and the connected real patch
      `V0 := {x | (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ Usrc}`.  The fields are
      `V0_open`, `V0_connected`, `x0_mem`, `V0_sub`,
      `wick_mem` and `wick_realSection_iff` from the helper, `double_mem`
      from `Usrc ⊆ Ωdouble`, and
      `wick_id_forwardTube` from `V0_sub`, `hV_ordered`, and
      `wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector 1`.  The
      lift fields are filled by setting `adjLift :=
      BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift hd τ`, using
      `BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift`, restricting
      `hV_adjLift_ET` along `V0_sub`, and applying
      `BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram`.

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at ... := by
        classical
        let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
        let z0 : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          fun k => wickRotatePoint (x0 k)
        let Ωdouble : Set (Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ) :=
          {z | BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n τ}
        have hΩ_open : IsOpen Ωdouble := by
          have hUrel :
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
                (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n) :=
            (BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain_relOpen_connected
              (d := d) hd n).1
          have hDoubleRel :
              BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety d n
                (BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n τ) :=
            BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain_relOpen_of_sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain
              (d := d) (n := n) hUrel τ
          simpa [Ωdouble] using
            BHW.IsRelOpenInSourceComplexGramVariety.sourceMinkowskiGram_preimage_open
              (d := d) (n := n) hDoubleRel
        have hz0Ω : z0 ∈ Ωdouble := by
          have hleft :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0 ∈
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
            have hz0FT :
                z0 ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
              simpa [z0] using
                wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector
                  (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
                  (hV_ordered x0 hx0V)
            exact ⟨z0, BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube hz0FT, rfl⟩
          have hright :
              BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z0) ∈
                BHW.sourceExtendedTubeGramDomain d n := by
            let Δ0 :=
              BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift
                (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x0 (0 : unitInterval)
            exact ⟨Δ0, hV_adjLift_ET x0 hx0V (0 : unitInterval), by
              have hGram :=
                BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram
                  (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x0 (0 : unitInterval)
              simpa [Δ0, z0, BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath_zero] using hGram⟩
          exact ⟨hleft, hright⟩
        rcases BHW.exists_connected_sourceNeighborhood_with_wickPreimage
            (d := d) (n := n) hΩ_open hz0Ω hV_open hx0V with
          ⟨V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hz0Usrc,
            hUsrc_sub, hwick_realSection_iff⟩
        have hdouble :
            ∀ z, z ∈ Usrc ->
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z ∈
                BHW.sourceDoublePermutationGramDomain d n τ := by
          intro z hz
          exact hUsrc_sub hz
        have hwick_id_FT :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V0 ->
              (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n := by
          intro x hx
          exact wickRotate_mem_forwardTube_of_mem_orderedPositiveTimeSector
            (d := d) (n := n) (1 : Equiv.Perm (Fin n))
            (hV_ordered x (hV0_sub hx))
        let adjLift :
            NPointDomain d n -> unitInterval -> Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ :=
          BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift (d := d) (n := n) hd τ
        have hadj_cont :
            ContinuousOn
              (fun p : NPointDomain d n × unitInterval => adjLift p.1 p.2)
              (V0 ×ˢ (Set.univ : Set unitInterval)) := by
          exact
            (BHW.continuous_os45Figure24AdjacentLift
              (d := d) (n := n) hd τ).continuousOn
        have hadj_ET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V0 -> ∀ t,
              adjLift x t ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n := by
          intro x hx t
          exact hV_adjLift_ET x (hV0_sub hx) t
        have hadj_gram :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V0 -> ∀ t,
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n (adjLift x t) =
                BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
                  (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n
                    (BHW.os45Figure24IdentityPath (d := d) (n := n) x t)) := by
          intro x hx t
          simpa [adjLift] using
            BHW.os45Figure24AdjacentLift_sourceGram
              (d := d) (n := n) hd τ x t
          exact
          { V0 := V0, Usrc := Usrc,
            V0_open := hV0_open, V0_connected := hV0_conn,
            x0_mem := hx0V0, V0_sub := hV0_sub,
            Usrc_open := hUsrc_open, Usrc_connected := hUsrc_conn,
            Usrc_nonempty := ⟨z0, hz0Usrc⟩,
            wick_mem := by
              intro x hx
              exact (hwick_realSection_iff x).2 hx,
            wick_realSection_iff := hwick_realSection_iff,
            double_mem := by simpa [τ, Ωdouble] using hdouble,
            wick_id_forwardTube := hwick_id_FT,
            adjLift := adjLift,
            adjLift_def := by intro x t; rfl,
            adjLift_continuousOn := hadj_cont,
            adjLift_mem_extendedTube := hadj_ET,
            adjLift_sourceGram := by simpa [τ] using hadj_gram }
      ```

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeScalarizationChart_of_figure24 ... := by
        classical
        let τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩
        let Φ0 := fun z => hRep.Phi (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z)
        let Φτ := fun z =>
          hRep.Phi
            (BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ
              (BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n z))
        have hF_holo_BHW :
            DifferentiableOn ℂ (bvt_F OS lgc n) (BHW.ForwardTube d n) := by
          simpa [BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)] using
            bvt_F_holomorphic (d := d) OS lgc n
        have hF_restricted_BHW :
            ∀ (Λ : RestrictedLorentzGroup d)
              (z : Fin n -> Fin (d + 1) -> ℂ),
              z ∈ BHW.ForwardTube d n ->
              bvt_F OS lgc n
                (fun k μ => ∑ ν, (Λ.val.val μ ν : ℂ) * z k ν) =
              bvt_F OS lgc n z := by
          intro Λ z hz
          exact bvt_F_restrictedLorentzInvariant_forwardTube
            (d := d) OS lgc n Λ z
            ((BHW_forwardTube_eq (d := d) (n := n)) ▸ hz)
        -- Figure-2-4 / OS §4.5 source chart around the chosen base point.
        rcases BHW.os45Figure24_sourceChart_at
            (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_open hV_jost
            hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hV_adjLift_ET hx0V with
          ⟨V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
            hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
            hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
            hdouble, hwick_id_FT,
            adjLift, hadj_def, hadj_cont, hadj_ET, hadj_gram⟩
        have hΦ0_diff : DifferentiableOn ℂ Φ0 Usrc := by
          have hGramU :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Usrc ⊆ hRep.U := by
            intro Z hZ
            rcases hZ with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
            rw [hRep.U_eq]
            exact (hdouble z hz).1
          exact BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hRep.Phi_holomorphic hGramU
        have hΦτ_diff : DifferentiableOn ℂ Φτ Usrc := by
          have hpre :=
            BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.precomp_sourcePermuteComplexGram
              (d := d) (n := n) hRep.Phi_holomorphic τ
          have hGramU :
              BHW.sourceMinkowskiGram d n '' Usrc ⊆
                {Z | BHW.sourcePermuteComplexGram n τ Z ∈ hRep.U} := by
            intro Z hZ
            rcases hZ with ⟨z, hz, rfl⟩
            rw [hRep.U_eq]
            exact (hdouble z hz).2
          exact BHW.SourceVarietyGermHolomorphicOn.comp_sourceMinkowskiGram
            (d := d) (n := n) hpre hGramU
        have hΦ0_wick :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V0 ->
              Φ0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) =
                bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
          intro x hx
          have hwET : (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) ∈
              BHW.ExtendedTube d n :=
            BHW.forwardTube_subset_extendedTube (hwick_id_FT x hx)
          calc
            Φ0 (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k))
                = BHW.extendF (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                    (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
                    simpa [Φ0, hRep.U_eq] using
                      hRep.branch_eq _ hwET
            _ = bvt_F OS lgc n (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) := by
                    exact BHW.extendF_eq_on_forwardTube n
                      (bvt_F OS lgc n)
                      hF_holo_BHW hF_restricted_BHW
                      _ (hwick_id_FT x hx)
        have hΦτ_pairing :
            ∀ φ : SchwartzNPoint d n,
              HasCompactSupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ->
              tsupport (φ : NPointDomain d n -> ℂ) ⊆ V0 ->
                ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    Φτ (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x k)) * φ x
                  =
                ∫ x : NPointDomain d n,
                    bvt_F OS lgc n
                      (fun k => wickRotatePoint (x (τ k))) * φ x := by
          exact
            BHW.os45AdjacentWickTrace_sourceScalarRepresentative_pairing_eq_of_figure24
              (d := d) hd OS lgc n i hi V hV_jost
              hV_ET hV_swapET hV_ordered hV_swap_ordered hRep
              { V0 := V0, Usrc := Usrc,
                V0_open := hV0_open, V0_connected := hV0_conn,
                x0_mem := hx0V0, V0_sub := hV0_sub,
                Usrc_open := hUsrc_open, Usrc_connected := hUsrc_conn,
                Usrc_nonempty := hUsrc_ne, wick_mem := hwick_mem,
                wick_realSection_iff := hwick_realSection_iff,
                double_mem := hdouble,
                wick_id_forwardTube := hwick_id_FT,
                adjLift := adjLift, adjLift_def := hadj_def,
                adjLift_continuousOn := hadj_cont,
                adjLift_mem_extendedTube := hadj_ET,
                adjLift_sourceGram := hadj_gram }
        exact ⟨V0, Usrc, hV0_open, hV0_conn, hx0V0, hV0_sub,
          hUsrc_open, hUsrc_conn, hUsrc_ne,
          hwick_mem, hwick_realSection_iff,
          hdouble, hΦ0_diff, hΦτ_diff,
          hΦ0_wick, by simpa [τ] using hΦτ_pairing⟩
      ```

      The source-neighborhood seed theorem is:

      ```lean
      theorem BHW.os45AdjacentSPrimeSourceEq_of_compactWickPairingEq
          [NeZero d]
          (hd : 2 <= d)
          (OS : OsterwalderSchraderAxioms d)
          (lgc : OSLinearGrowthCondition d OS)
          (n : Nat) (i : Fin n) (hi : i.val + 1 < n)
          (V : Set (NPointDomain d n))
          (hV_open : IsOpen V)
          (hV_jost : ∀ x, x ∈ V -> x ∈ BHW.JostSet d n)
          (hV_ET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V -> BHW.realEmbed x ∈ BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_swapET :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
              BHW.realEmbed (fun k => x (Equiv.swap i ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ k)) ∈
                BHW.ExtendedTube d n)
          (hV_ordered :
            ∀ x, x ∈ V ->
